The helper reads `unique_name`/`nameid` and `role`; if a future token uses URI-style claim keys, the decode will yield null and log the user out — keep the fallback fields in sync if claim emission changes.; Order matters in AuthProvider: declare/initialize `token` state before `user` (or compute both from the same `localStorage.getItem('token')`), otherwise the user initializer may read a token the component hasn't validated/cleared yet.; Do not perform any client-side cryptographic signature verification — the HMAC key (JWT:Key) must never ship to the browser. We only decode and check `exp`; signature trust stays server-side.; Rollback is trivial: revert AuthContext.tsx to deriving user from `userid`/`permission`. No data migration and no backend changes are required, so this can ship independently.; exp is seconds since epoch in JWT; multiply by 1000 before comparing with Date.now() (already handled in the patch). A missing exp must be treated as invalid, not as 'never expires'.

> 🔍 **Verifier note.** Recommendation in the finding is sound (treat client guard as UX-only, ensure backend authorizes every request, validate JWT/exp client-side, derive role from verified claims). The most important mitigation it calls for already exists in the backend, which is why severity drops. Note the AuthController login (Backend/Controllers/AuthController.cs) is correctly [AllowAnonymous]; BaseAPIController is annotated [AllowAnonymous] at class level but each action overrides with [Authorize], so data actions are protected. Worth a quick confirmation that report controllers do not also re-open [AllowAnonymous] at action level, but the grep showed they carry [Authorize].


<a id="f-demo-creds-prefilled-login"></a>
#### ⚪ INFO — Login form pre-filled with demo@email.com / demo123

**ID:** `demo-creds-prefilled-login` &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Phase:** P3 &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Category:** Insecure Default &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Effort:** Trivial &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Confidence:** High

**Location(s):**
- `Frontend/src/pages/authentication/SignIn.tsx:121-125`
- `Frontend/src/pages/authentication/SignIn.tsx:121-125`

**Problem.** The SignIn form initialValues hardcode email: 'demo@email.com', password: 'demo123', remember: true. This is shipped to production.

**Impact.** Suggests a demo/seed account may exist with these well-known credentials; if such an account was ever created on the backend it is an immediate weak-credential foothold. At minimum it is unprofessional for a government portal and primes credential-guessing.

**Recommendation.** Remove the prefilled credentials (set empty initialValues). Verify no demo@email.com account with a weak password exists in any deployed database.

**🛠 How to Fix _(est. 15 min (code) + ops DB check)_**

Remove the hardcoded demo credentials from the SignIn form's `initialValues` so the login form ships empty on production, and stop logging submitted credential values to the browser console. Pair the code change with an operational check that no real `demo@email.com` weak-credential account exists in any deployed database.

1. Open Frontend/src/pages/authentication/SignIn.tsx and locate the antd <Form> at lines 116-130, specifically the initialValues prop at lines 121-125.
2. Replace the initialValues object that hardcodes email: 'demo@email.com', password: 'demo123', remember: true. Either drop the email/password defaults entirely (keeping only remember: true if you want the checkbox checked by default), or remove the initialValues prop completely. Recommended: keep `initialValues={{ remember: true }}` so the email and password fields render blank but 'Remember me' stays checked.
3. In the same file, remove the credential-leaking console.log in onFinish at line 39 (`console.log('Success:', values);`) — `values` contains the plaintext email and password and should not be written to the browser console in a government portal. Also remove the console.log at line 64 in onFinishFailed for consistency (optional, but it is dead debug logging).
4. Operational verification (no in-repo code creates this account — confirmed by grep): have ops query each deployed/live database for a user with email 'demo@email.com' and, if present, either delete it or rotate it to a strong unique password. The repo Backend directory contains no seeding tied to these creds, so this is purely a runtime/database check, not a code change.
5. Rebuild the frontend (npm run build in Frontend/) and confirm the bundled output no longer contains the literal strings 'demo@email.com' or 'demo123'.

```tsx
// Frontend/src/pages/authentication/SignIn.tsx

// onFinish (line ~38-39) — stop logging plaintext credentials
const onFinish = (values: AnyObject) => {
-  console.log('Success:', values);
   setLoading(true);
   ...

// <Form> initialValues (lines 121-125)
 <Form
   name="sign-up-form"
   layout="vertical"
   labelCol={{ span: 24 }}
   wrapperCol={{ span: 24 }}
-  initialValues={{
-    email: 'demo@email.com',
-    password: 'demo123',
-    remember: true,
-  }}
+  initialValues={{ remember: true }}
   onFinish={onFinish}
   onFinishFailed={onFinishFailed}
   autoComplete="off"
   requiredMark={false}
 >
```

**Verify:**
- Run `grep -rn "demo@email.com\|demo123" Frontend/src` from the repo root — it must return no matches (currently only SignIn.tsx:122-123 match).
- Run the frontend dev server (npm run dev in Frontend/), open the /sign-in page, and confirm the Email and Password fields render empty (no prefilled demo@email.com / demo123) while 'Remember me' is still checked if you kept remember: true.
- After `npm run build`, grep the dist/ output for 'demo@email.com' and 'demo123' to confirm the strings are not bundled into the production artifact.
- Open the browser devtools console, submit the login form, and confirm no 'Success:' line printing the submitted values appears.
- Ops check: query each deployed DB (e.g. SELECT * FROM <users table> WHERE Email = 'demo@email.com') and confirm either no row exists or the account has been removed / its password rotated to a strong value.

**Pitfalls / rollback.** Do not delete the `remember` Form.Item or its valuePropName='checked' binding (lines 154-158) — only the initialValues defaults change. If you remove initialValues entirely instead of keeping `{ remember: true }`, the 'Remember me' checkbox will start unchecked, which is fine but is a minor behavior change. The database cleanup is an operational task outside this repo; the code fix alone removes the hint but a pre-existing weak demo account on a server would remain a foothold until ops verifies/removes it, so do not mark the finding fully closed until that check is done.

> 🔍 **Verifier note.** Could not inspect any deployed/live database from here, so the recommendation to confirm no real demo@email.com account exists remains valid as an operational check — the code-level search just shows nothing in-repo creates one. Backend dir exists but contains no seeding tied to these creds.


<a id="f-fileupload-window-write-sink"></a>
#### ⚪ INFO — useFileUpload opens a new window and writes image markup built from user input

**ID:** `fileupload-window-write-sink` &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Phase:** P3 &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Category:** Potential XSS Sink &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Effort:** Small &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Confidence:** High

**Location(s):**
- `Frontend/src/hooks/useFileUpload.tsx:22-37 (note: this hook is dead/unused; live preview path is useFileUploadWithoutCrop.tsx:77-82, which uses plain window.open, no document.write)`
- `Frontend/src/hooks/useFileUpload.tsx:22-37`

**Problem.** onPreview constructs src from envConfig.imageUrl + a file name / FileReader data URL, builds an Image element, then does window.open(src) followed by imgWindow.document.write(image.outerHTML). document.write of dynamically-constructed markup into a new window is a classic XSS sink; the src is partly derived from user-controlled file names and the (untrusted) configured image URL.

**Impact.** If an attacker controls a file name or the image URL value, crafted content reflected through outerHTML/document.write could execute script in the opened window context. Lower severity because exploitation depends on the upstream image source, but it is fragile and unnecessary.

**Recommendation.** Avoid document.write entirely. Preview images via the Ant Design <Image> preview component or by setting an <img src> on a sanitized, validated URL. Whitelist allowed extensions and never inject user-derived strings into raw HTML.

**🛠 How to Fix _(est. 15 min)_**

This is Info/code-quality, not a live vulnerability: the cited hook `Frontend/src/hooks/useFileUpload.tsx` is dead code (zero importers, not in the hooks `index.ts` barrel), and its `document.write(image.outerHTML)` writes a DOM-encoded `Image` whose `src` carries a fixed `envConfig.imageUrl` scheme prefix. The correct, lowest-risk action is to delete the unused file outright, eliminating the `document.write` sink entirely.

1. Confirm the hook is unreferenced before deleting. From Frontend/, run: grep -rn "useFileUpload'" src --include=*.ts --include=*.tsx | grep -v useFileUploadWithoutCrop  (expect no output) and grep -rn "hooks/useFileUpload\b" src  (expect no output). The only file with a similar name in use is Frontend/src/hooks/useFileUploadWithoutCrop.tsx (exports useFileUploadNoCrop), consumed by Frontend/src/components/Chat/ChatBox.tsx — leave that one alone.
2. Delete the dead file: git rm "Frontend/src/hooks/useFileUpload.tsx". No edits to Frontend/src/hooks/index.ts are required because that barrel only re-exports usePageContext and useFetchData (it never exported useFileUpload).
3. Harden the surviving live preview path in Frontend/src/hooks/useFileUploadWithoutCrop.tsx (onPreview at lines 77-82). It already avoids document.write (uses window.open(src, '_blank')), but add rel-style hardening: open with window.open(src, '_blank', 'noopener,noreferrer') so the opened tab cannot reach back via window.opener.
4. Optional defense-in-depth in the same onPreview: before opening, validate the extension off fileList.name against a whitelist (e.g. const ok = /\.(png|jpe?g|webp|gif)$/i.test(fileList.name)) and bail out if it fails. Note beforeUpload at line 53 already forces a '.webp' extension on uploaded names, so live previews are normally .webp.
5. Run the TypeScript build to confirm nothing referenced the removed file: from Frontend/ run npx tsc --noEmit (or npm run build). It must compile with no 'Cannot find module' errors pointing at useFileUpload.

```tsx
// DELETE the entire dead file: Frontend/src/hooks/useFileUpload.tsx
// (it is the only place document.write is used in the upload hooks)
//   const imgWindow = window.open(src);
//   imgWindow?.document.write(image.outerHTML);   <-- the cited sink, now removed with the file

// OPTIONAL hardening of the surviving live path:
// Frontend/src/hooks/useFileUploadWithoutCrop.tsx  (onPreview, lines 77-82)
// before:
const onPreview = async (fileList: AnyObject) => {
  const src = envConfig.imageUrl + fileList.name;
  if (src) {
    window.open(src, '_blank');
  }
};
// after:
const onPreview = async (fileList: AnyObject) => {
  const src = envConfig.imageUrl + fileList.name;
  if (src && /\.(png|jpe?g|webp|gif)$/i.test(fileList.name)) {
    window.open(src, '_blank', 'noopener,noreferrer');
  }
};
```

**Verify:**
- From Frontend/: grep -rn "document.write" src returns no hits in the hooks directory after deletion.
- From Frontend/: npx tsc --noEmit completes with no errors (proves no module imported the deleted useFileUpload.tsx).
- From Frontend/: git ls-files src/hooks/useFileUpload.tsx returns empty (file removed from tracking).
- Manual: in the Chat upload UI (ChatBox.tsx), click preview on an uploaded image — it still opens in a new tab via useFileUploadWithoutCrop.onPreview, confirming the live feature is unaffected.

**Pitfalls / rollback.** Do NOT delete or rename Frontend/src/hooks/useFileUploadWithoutCrop.tsx — it exports useFileUploadNoCrop and is actively used by Frontend/src/components/Chat/ChatBox.tsx. Only useFileUpload.tsx (the crop variant) is dead. The hooks index.ts barrel does not export useFileUpload, so no barrel edit is needed; do not add/remove unrelated exports there. Rollback is trivial (git restore the deleted file) since nothing depends on it. The optional extension whitelist must allow .webp, since beforeUpload rewrites upload names to .webp.

> 🔍 **Verifier note.** Two real reasons to downgrade: the cited hook has zero importers (dead code), and the document.write payload is image.outerHTML built from an Image whose src is set via the DOM property — the browser attribute-encodes it, and the URL scheme is fixed by the configured prefix. The recommendation (drop document.write, use AntD Image preview / sanitized src, whitelist extensions) is reasonable as a cleanup, but this should be Info/code-quality rather than a security finding. If anything is acted on, simply delete the unused useFileUpload.tsx.


<a id="f-sheetjs-cve-not-exploitable-note"></a>
#### ⚪ INFO — xlsx (SheetJS 0.18.5) is used only for export, not for parsing untrusted files

**ID:** `sheetjs-cve-not-exploitable-note` &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Phase:** P3 &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Category:** Dependency Risk (assessed, not exploitable) &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Effort:** Small &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Confidence:** High

**Location(s):**
- `Frontend/package.json:44`
- `Frontend/src/components/My Components/Table/BasicTable.tsx:242-243`

**Problem.** package.json pins xlsx ^0.18.5, which has known prototype-pollution (CVE-2023-30533) and ReDoS (CVE-2024-22363) advisories. However those vulnerabilities live in the read/parse path (XLSX.read / readFile on attacker-supplied workbooks). A repo-wide search shows the app only calls XLSX.utils.table_to_book and XLSX.writeFile to generate downloads; there is no XLSX.read/readFile/sheet_to_json on untrusted input. So the CVEs are not currently reachable.

**Impact.** No direct exploit today because no untrusted .xlsx is parsed. Residual risk is supply-chain/version hygiene and the possibility that a future feature adds spreadsheet import.

**Recommendation.** Upgrade to the maintained SheetJS distribution (0.20.x from the official CDN; the npm 0.18.5 is end-of-life) for hygiene, and add a guard/code-review rule so that if file import is ever added it parses with a patched version and validates input.

**🛠 How to Fix _(est. 30-45 min)_**

This is an Info-level, not-currently-exploitable finding: xlsx is used only on the export path (XLSX.utils.table_to_book + XLSX.writeFile in BasicTable.tsx), never on a read/parse path, so CVE-2023-30533 / CVE-2024-22363 are unreachable. The fix is version hygiene plus a preventive guardrail: pin to the maintained SheetJS 0.20.x distribution (published on the SheetJS CDN, not the npm registry) and add an ESLint no-restricted-syntax rule banning XLSX.read/readFile/sheet_to_json so any future import feature fails lint until it parses with a patched version on validated input.

1. Confirm scope first (1 command): from Frontend/, run `grep -rnE "XLSX\.(read|readFile)|sheet_to_json|readFileSync" src --include=*.ts --include=*.tsx`. It returns nothing today, which is what makes this Info/not-exploitable. The only usage is the export path in `Frontend/src/components/My Components/Table/BasicTable.tsx`: line 19 `import * as XLSX from 'xlsx';`, line 242 `XLSX.utils.table_to_book(table, { sheet: 'Report' })`, line 243 `XLSX.writeFile(workbook, excelFileName)`. Do NOT change this code path; it is benign.
2. Move off the EOL npm `xlsx@0.18.5` to the maintained SheetJS 0.20.x distribution. Per the verifier note, there is NO drop-in npm `xlsx@0.20.x` — the npm `xlsx` package is frozen at 0.18.5. The maintained build is served from the SheetJS CDN, so this is an install-config change, not a plain version bump.
3. Edit `Frontend/package.json` line 44: replace the dependency `"xlsx": "^0.18.5"` with the CDN-hosted SheetJS tarball `"xlsx": "https://cdn.sheetjs.com/xlsx-0.20.3/xlsx-0.20.3.tgz"` (a direct tarball URL; this is the officially documented install method and avoids needing a custom registry). Keep the import specifier `'xlsx'` unchanged so `BasicTable.tsx` needs no code edit.
4. Regenerate the lockfile so `Frontend/package-lock.json` resolves to the CDN tarball instead of the npmjs.org 0.18.5 entry: run `npm install` from `Frontend/`. Verify the new lockfile `node_modules/xlsx` block points at `https://cdn.sheetjs.com/xlsx-0.20.3/...` and version `0.20.3` (it currently shows `version: 0.18.5`, resolved from registry.npmjs.org).
5. Add a preventive ESLint guardrail so any future import/parse usage is blocked at lint time. Edit `Frontend/.eslintrc.cjs` and add a `no-restricted-syntax` rule to the existing `rules` object (shown in the patch). Because usage is via the namespace `XLSX.*`, target the call expressions `XLSX.read`/`XLSX.readFile` and any `sheet_to_json` call — a plain `no-restricted-imports` would not catch namespace member calls.
6. Run `npm run lint` from `Frontend/` to confirm the new rule does not flag the existing export-only code (must stay green, since only write-path APIs are used), then `npm run build` to confirm the SheetJS swap compiles.
7. Since there are no CI workflows in the repo (`.github/workflows` does not exist), document the guardrail in team conventions instead of CI: add a one-line note to `AGENTS.md` / code-review checklist stating that adding any spreadsheet-import (`XLSX.read*` / `sheet_to_json` on uploaded files) requires a patched SheetJS version and explicit input validation. This satisfies the 'add a guard/code-review rule' part of the recommendation.

```json
// 1) Frontend/package.json  (line 44)  -- pin to maintained SheetJS CDN tarball
-    "xlsx": "^0.18.5"
+    "xlsx": "https://cdn.sheetjs.com/xlsx-0.20.3/xlsx-0.20.3.tgz"

// 2) Frontend/.eslintrc.cjs  -- add preventive guardrail in the `rules` object
   rules: {
     'react-refresh/only-export-components': [
       'warn',
       { allowConstantExport: true },
     ],
+    // Guard: xlsx is approved for EXPORT only (XLSX.utils.table_to_book / XLSX.writeFile).
+    // Reading/parsing untrusted workbooks reaches CVE-2023-30533 / CVE-2024-22363.
+    // If file import is ever needed, use a patched SheetJS build + validate input,
+    // then narrow this rule deliberately.
+    'no-restricted-syntax': [
+      'error',
+      {
+        selector:
+          "CallExpression[callee.object.name='XLSX'][callee.property.name=/^(read|readFile)$/]",
+        message:
+          'Parsing spreadsheets with XLSX.read/readFile is banned (CVE-2023-30533 / CVE-2024-22363). xlsx is export-only here; if import is required, use a patched SheetJS build and validate input first.',
+      },
+      {
+        selector: "CallExpression[callee.property.name='sheet_to_json']",
+        message:
+          'XLSX.utils.sheet_to_json on untrusted input is banned. xlsx is export-only here; validate input and use a patched build before enabling import.',
+      },
+    ],
   },

// 3) BasicTable.tsx lines 19, 242-243 -- UNCHANGED (export path, benign)
//    import * as XLSX from 'xlsx';
//    const workbook = XLSX.utils.table_to_book(table, { sheet: 'Report' });
//    XLSX.writeFile(workbook, excelFileName);
```

**Verify:**
- From Frontend/: `npm install`, then inspect package-lock.json — the `node_modules/xlsx` entry must show `version: 0.20.3` and `resolved: https://cdn.sheetjs.com/...` (no longer 0.18.5 from registry.npmjs.org).
- `npm run lint` passes green: the new no-restricted-syntax rule does NOT flag BasicTable.tsx (it uses only table_to_book/writeFile), proving the export path is unaffected.
- Negative test for the guardrail: temporarily add `const wb = XLSX.read(buf);` to a .tsx file and run `npm run lint` — it must fail with the CVE message; then remove the line.
- `npm run build` succeeds (tsc + vite build) and the Excel export button in any report (e.g. MemberRegistrationReport) still downloads a valid .xlsx, confirming the SheetJS swap is functionally identical.
- Repo-wide confirmation the CVE path stays absent: `grep -rnE "XLSX\.(read|readFile)|sheet_to_json" Frontend/src` returns no matches.

**Pitfalls / rollback.** Severity is Info / not-exploitable — treat as hygiene, not an emergency; do not refactor the working export path in BasicTable.tsx. The npm registry has no 0.20.x for `xlsx` (frozen at 0.18.5), so a naive `npm install xlsx@latest` or a Dependabot bump will NOT work and may silently keep 0.18.5 — you must use the CDN tarball URL. Direct-URL/tarball deps can trip locked-down corporate registries or air-gapped CI; confirm the build environment can reach cdn.sheetjs.com (or vendor/mirror the tarball to the internal registry) before merging. The `integrity` hash in package-lock.json will change — commit the regenerated lockfile. Rollback is trivial: revert package.json line 44 to `\"xlsx\": \"^0.18.5\"`, restore the old lockfile entry, run `npm install`; the ESLint rule is independent and safe to keep regardless.

> 🔍 **Verifier note.** Everything in the finding checks out as written; no correction to location or severity needed. One minor caveat: the recommendation to upgrade involves moving off the npm registry to the SheetJS CDN tarball, which is a non-trivial install/CI change (no drop-in npm 0.20.x exists), so the "hygiene fix" is slightly more involved than a version bump — but this does not affect the correctness or severity of the finding.


---

### Frontend — State & Data Fetching

> The frontend has effectively no shared state-management or data-fetching strategy. Redux is used only for a single `theme` slice; all server data (155 report pages, lookups, exports, dashboard) is fetched with hand-rolled axios calls and local `useState`, with no RTK Query, no caching/dedup, no request cancellation, and no normalization. The most material problems are: a race condition in the central `BasicTable` fetch (no abort, no response-staleness guard) that can show one report's data under another report's filters; auth token + permission stored in `localStorage` (XSS-exfiltratable, and the token is injected into every axios request); widespread swallowed errors that hide backend failures from operators; and the complete absence of a React error boundary, so any render exception blanks the entire admin app. There are also several smaller correctness bugs (stale-closure in `useFetchData`, a null-deref crash path in a dashboard card, redundant JSON round-tripping of every response).

<a id="f-auth-token-in-localstorage"></a>
#### 🟠 HIGH — JWT, user id and permission stored in localStorage and injected into every request

**ID:** `auth-token-in-localstorage` &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Phase:** P1 &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Category:** State persistence / auth storage &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Effort:** Medium &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Confidence:** High

**Location(s):**
- `Frontend/src/context/AuthContext.tsx:22-65`
- `Frontend/src/context/AuthContext.tsx:50-52`
- `Frontend/src/services/AxiosInstance.ts:10-13`

**Problem.** On login the bearer token, `userId`, and `permission` are written to `localStorage` (AuthContext.tsx:50-52) and re-read on every app start (lines 22-34). The request interceptor reads `localStorage.getItem('token')` and attaches it as `Authorization` on every outgoing request (AxiosInstance.ts:10-11), including when there is no token (it sends `Bearer null`). localStorage is readable by any JavaScript on the origin, so a single XSS (and this app renders untrusted report strings, runs MQTT/Firebase, and disables several CSP-relevant protections) exfiltrates a long-lived government-admin token. The client also trusts `permission` from localStorage for UI gating, which is user-editable.

**Impact.** Any XSS or malicious dependency can steal a privileged admin JWT and impersonate the operator against the Ministry backend. Because the token is in localStorage (not an httpOnly cookie) it survives tab close and is trivially scriptable. The `Bearer null` case also means unauthenticated requests are sent with a malformed header rather than none.

**Recommendation.** Prefer httpOnly+Secure+SameSite cookies for the session token so JS cannot read it. If localStorage must stay, at minimum: guard the interceptor to only attach the header when a real token exists, treat client-side `permission` as a hint only (server must authorize every endpoint), and add a strict CSP. Clear all three keys consistently on logout (logout already does this) and on 401.

**Example:**

```
// current
const token = localStorage.getItem('token');
config.headers.Authorization = `Bearer ${token}`; // sends 'Bearer null' when absent

// fixed
const token = localStorage.getItem('token');
if (token) config.headers.Authorization = `Bearer ${token}`;
```

**🛠 How to Fix _(est. Interceptor guard: ~30 min (incl. verification). CSP report-only rollout: ~half a day to inventory origins and tune. Full httpOnly-cookie migration: ~1-2 days (separate PR, needs backend + CSRF work).)_**

The strategic fix is to move the session token to an httpOnly+Secure+SameSite cookie so JavaScript (and thus any XSS) cannot read it; that requires a coordinated backend change. The immediate, low-risk hardening that lands now: guard the axios request interceptor so it only attaches `Authorization` when a real token exists (stop sending `Bearer null`), and add a strict Content-Security-Policy to shrink the XSS surface that makes localStorage exfiltration possible. Server must remain the source of truth for authorization since `isAuthenticated`/`permission` are derived purely from client-editable localStorage.

1. IMMEDIATE FIX (interceptor guard) — Edit Frontend/src/services/AxiosInstance.ts in the request interceptor (currently lines 9-11). Replace the unconditional `config.headers.Authorization = `Bearer ${token}`;` with a guarded version that reads the token, and only sets the header when the token is a non-empty string. This stops the `Bearer null` / `Bearer undefined` headers being sent on unauthenticated requests (e.g. the POST to 'Auth' login itself).
2. Keep the existing baseUrl rewrite (`config.url = envConfig.baseUrl + config.url`) and the `else { return Promise.reject('') }` branch unchanged — only the Authorization assignment changes.
3. DEFENSE IN DEPTH (CSP) — Add a Content-Security-Policy. Since this is a Vite SPA served as static files, set the policy at the web server / reverse proxy that fronts Frontend (preferred, because it can set it as a real HTTP response header and also set frame-ancestors). If no proxy is editable, add a `<meta http-equiv="Content-Security-Policy">` tag in Frontend/index.html <head> as a fallback (note: meta CSP cannot express frame-ancestors). Start in Report-Only mode to find violations, then enforce. The policy must allow the app's real origins: fonts.googleapis.com / fonts.gstatic.com (already used in index.html), the API baseUrl from src/config, plus the MQTT (wss) and Firebase endpoints this app uses — inventory them via grep before enforcing so you don't break MQTT/Firebase.
4. AUTHORIZATION HARDENING (backend, audit-driven, not a code change in this file) — Confirm every protected ASP.NET Core endpoint enforces `[Authorize]` (and role/policy checks) server-side rather than trusting the client. The client `permission` value in localStorage is user-editable and is only a UI hint; ProtectedRoute.tsx gates on `auth?.isAuthenticated` which is just `!!user` derived from localStorage, so it provides no real security. Add a follow-up ticket to audit controller authorization attributes.
5. STRATEGIC (token storage migration — separate larger ticket) — Change the /Auth endpoint to issue the JWT as an httpOnly, Secure, SameSite=Strict (or Lax) cookie instead of (or in addition to) returning it in the JSON body. Then in AuthContext.tsx stop calling `localStorage.setItem('token', ...)` and in AxiosInstance.ts set `axios.create({ withCredentials: true })` so the cookie is sent automatically and drop the manual Authorization header entirely. Keep a non-sensitive `userid`/`permission` in memory or localStorage only as a UI hint. This requires CORS `AllowCredentials()` + an explicit allowed-origin (not `*`) on the backend and CSRF protection (SameSite plus an anti-forgery token) — scope it as its own PR; do NOT bundle it with the interceptor guard.
6. Do NOT add 401 key-clearing — it already exists. The response interceptor in Frontend/src/layouts/app/App.tsx:134-151 calls `auth?.logout()` on HTTP 401, and logout() in AuthContext.tsx:67-73 already removes 'token', 'userid', and 'permission'. Leave it as is.

```ts
// Frontend/src/services/AxiosInstance.ts  (request interceptor, lines ~9-15)

// BEFORE
if (config.url) {
  const token = localStorage.getItem('token');
  config.headers.Authorization = `Bearer ${token}`; // sends "Bearer null" when absent
  if (config.url && envConfig.baseUrl) {
    config.url = envConfig.baseUrl + config.url;
  }
  return config;
}

// AFTER
if (config.url) {
  const token = localStorage.getItem('token');
  if (token) {
    config.headers.Authorization = `Bearer ${token}`;
  } else {
    // ensure no stale/placeholder header leaks out
    delete config.headers.Authorization;
  }
  if (envConfig.baseUrl) {
    config.url = envConfig.baseUrl + config.url;
  }
  return config;
}

/* Frontend/index.html — fallback CSP if no reverse proxy is available.
   Add inside <head>. Tune the source lists to your real API / MQTT / Firebase
   origins (replace API_ORIGIN, wss MQTT host, and *.firebaseio.com as needed)
   and start with Content-Security-Policy-Report-Only before enforcing. */
// <meta
//   http-equiv="Content-Security-Policy"
//   content="default-src 'self';
//            script-src 'self';
//            style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline' https://fonts.googleapis.com;
//            font-src 'self' https://fonts.gstatic.com;
//            img-src 'self' data:;
//            connect-src 'self' https://API_ORIGIN wss://MQTT_HOST https://*.firebaseio.com;
//            object-src 'none'; base-uri 'self'" />
```

**Verify:**
- Build the frontend (`cd Frontend && npm run build`) — TypeScript must compile cleanly after the interceptor edit.
- Open the app logged OUT (no 'token' in localStorage). In DevTools > Network, trigger the login POST to 'Auth' and confirm the request has NO `Authorization` header (previously it sent `Authorization: Bearer null`).
- Log in, then make any report request and confirm `Authorization: Bearer <jwt>` is present and correct — i.e. authenticated traffic is unchanged.
- Clear localStorage while logged in (simulate expiry) and make a request: confirm no Authorization header is attached and the app still functions (no `Bearer null`).
- Confirm the existing 401 flow still works: force a 401 from the backend and verify the App.tsx (layouts/app) response interceptor logs out and removes 'token'/'userid'/'permission' from localStorage.
- After adding the CSP in Report-Only mode, exercise MQTT, Firebase, fonts, and API calls; check the console/`report-uri` for CSP violations and widen the policy only as needed before switching to enforcing mode.
- For the cookie migration (when done): confirm the Set-Cookie on /Auth has HttpOnly + Secure + SameSite, that `document.cookie` in DevTools does NOT show the token, and that authenticated requests succeed with `withCredentials: true`.

**Pitfalls / rollback.** The interceptor guard is safe and self-contained; do it first and ship it independently. The httpOnly-cookie migration is a breaking, cross-cutting change (backend cookie issuance + CORS AllowCredentials with a fixed origin + CSRF protection) — keep it in a separate PR to avoid coupling a risky migration to a one-line hardening.; The localStorage key is 'userid' (lowercase) and is used consistently in AuthContext.tsx read (line 23), write (line 51), and logout (line 69) — do not 'fix' it to 'userId'; that would silently break session restore.; Do NOT re-add 401 key clearing — App.tsx (layouts/app) already does it via auth.logout(); adding a duplicate could double-fire logout/messages.; CSP risk: this app loads Google Fonts and uses MQTT (wss) + Firebase. A too-strict connect-src/font-src/style-src will silently break those features. Inventory the real origins (grep src/config and the MQTT/Firebase setup) and roll out CSP in Report-Only first. Meta-tag CSP cannot set frame-ancestors or use a nonce easily — prefer an HTTP header at the proxy if clickjacking protection is needed.; Until the token leaves localStorage, the core XSS-exfiltration risk remains only mitigated, not eliminated — make sure the cookie-migration ticket is actually scheduled, not dropped after the quick interceptor fix.

> 🔍 **Verifier note.** Two minor overstatements that do NOT change the verdict: (1) The finding says the client "trusts permission from localStorage for UI gating," but ProtectedRoute.tsx gates only on isAuthenticated (= !!user), not on the permission value itself; the broader point still holds because isAuthenticated is derived purely from client-side localStorage. (2) The recommendation's "clear all three keys ... on 401" is already implemented — App.tsx:134-151 has a response interceptor that calls auth.logout() on HTTP 401, which removes all three keys. Also, the actual localStorage key is 'userid' (lowercase), written/read consistently, not 'userId' as the prose loosely implies — no functional bug. Locations cited are accurate.


<a id="f-no-request-cancellation-race"></a>
#### 🟡 MEDIUM — Report grid fetch has no cancellation; rapid filter/page changes race and can show stale/wrong data

**ID:** `no-request-cancellation-race` &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Phase:** P2 &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Category:** Data fetching / race condition &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Effort:** Medium &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Confidence:** High

**Location(s):**
- `Frontend/src/components/My Components/Table/BasicTable.tsx:204-234`
- `Frontend/src/Report/Page/GenericReportPage.tsx:652-662`
- `Frontend/src/Report/Page/GenericReportPage.tsx:711-722`

**Problem.** BasicTable's load effect (lines 204-234) fires whenever `query` or `refreshKey` changes and calls `fetchData(query)` (the GenericReportPage `fetchRows` POST). There is no AbortController, no axios CancelToken, and no per-request sequence/staleness guard. `setData(result)` runs in `.then` regardless of whether a newer request has since been issued. On a government report with large result sets, switching pages quickly, hitting Filter repeatedly, or changing page size while a slow query is in flight means responses can resolve out of order: the response for an older filter/page overwrites the grid that the user is now looking at. grep confirms `AbortController`/`CancelToken`/`signal` appear nowhere in src.

**Impact.** An operator can be shown trade figures (transaction amounts, company lists, licence totals) that belong to a different filter or page than the one currently selected on screen. For a regulatory reporting tool this is a correctness/integrity problem, not just a UX glitch: decisions or exports may be made against mismatched data. Also wasted backend load from un-cancelled in-flight queries.

**Recommendation.** Cancel the previous request before issuing a new one and ignore stale responses. Minimal fix: create an AbortController per effect run, pass `signal` through `fetchData`->axios, abort in the effect cleanup. Belt-and-suspenders: capture a monotonically increasing request id and only `setData` if it is still the latest. Strategically, migrate report fetching to RTK Query (or TanStack Query), which gives request dedup, cancellation-on-unmount, and caching for free.

**Example:**

```
// current (BasicTable.tsx)
const result = fetchData ? await fetchData(query) : await fetch!(...);
setData(result ?? emptyPage<T>());

// fixed
useEffect(() => {
  const ctrl = new AbortController();
  (async () => {
    try { const r = await fetchData(query, ctrl.signal); if(!ctrl.signal.aborted) setData(r); }
    catch (e) { if(!ctrl.signal.aborted) setError(parseErr(e)); }
  })();
  return () => ctrl.abort();
}, [query, refreshKey]);
```

**🛠 How to Fix _(est. 30 min)_**

Add a monotonically increasing request-id (staleness) guard inside BasicTable's load effect so a late-resolving response for an older filter/page can no longer overwrite the grid; this one guard covers both the fetchData and legacy fetch paths.

1. Open Frontend/src/components/My Components/Table/BasicTable.tsx and locate the load effect at lines 204-234 (the `useEffect` that calls `fetchData(query)` / `fetch!(buildLegacyUrl(api!, query))` and ends with `}, [api, enabled, fetch, fetchData, query, refreshKey]);`).
2. Add a stable per-component request counter. Just below the existing `useState` declarations (after line 179, `const [data, setData] = useState<PaginationType<T>>(emptyPage<T>());`) add `const requestIdRef = useRef(0);` and add `useRef` to the React import on line 1 (`import React, { useEffect, useMemo, useRef, useState } from 'react';`).
3. Inside the effect, before any async work, capture the id for this run: `const requestId = ++requestIdRef.current;`. This must be incremented synchronously in the effect body (not inside the async `load`) so every new effect run claims a strictly higher id than any in-flight request.
4. Guard every state-mutating call that happens after the `await` with `if (requestId !== requestIdRef.current) return;`. Specifically: after `const result = ...await...`, only `setData(result ?? emptyPage<T>())` if this is still the latest request; in `catch`, only `setError(...)` if latest; in `finally`, only `setLoading(false)` if latest. A stale response must not flip `loading` off either, or it would clear the skeleton while the newer request is still running.
5. Keep the early-return branches (`!enabled` and `!fetchData && (!fetch || !api)`) as they are — those run synchronously before any await, so they are inherently the latest run and need no guard. The `setLoading(true)`/`setError(null)` before the try are also synchronous for the current run and stay unguarded.
6. Leave GenericReportPage.tsx unchanged. `fetchRows` (lines 652-662), `applyFilters` (711-715), and `resetFilters` (717-722) need no edit: bumping `refreshKey` / changing `filters` already re-runs the BasicTable effect, and the new guard handles the out-of-order resolution. Do not add a signal param to `fetchRows` — that would force every report to thread it through.
7. Run `npm run lint` and `npm run build` (or `tsc -b`) in Frontend/ to confirm the `useRef` import and types are clean.

```tsx
// Frontend/src/components/My Components/Table/BasicTable.tsx

// line 1 — add useRef
import React, { useEffect, useMemo, useRef, useState } from 'react';

// after line 179 (below the data useState) — add a request counter
const requestIdRef = useRef(0);

// replace the load effect (lines 204-234) with a staleness-guarded version:
useEffect(() => {
  const requestId = ++requestIdRef.current; // claim this run as the latest

  const load = async () => {
    if (!enabled) {
      setLoading(false);
      setError(null);
      setData(emptyPage<T>());
      return;
    }

    if (!fetchData && (!fetch || !api)) {
      return;
    }

    setLoading(true);
    setError(null);

    try {
      const result = fetchData
        ? await fetchData(query)
        : await fetch!(buildLegacyUrl(api!, query));

      if (requestId !== requestIdRef.current) return; // a newer request superseded us
      setData(result ?? emptyPage<T>());
    } catch {
      if (requestId !== requestIdRef.current) return;
      setError('Failed to load table data.');
    } finally {
      if (requestId === requestIdRef.current) {
        setLoading(false);
      }
    }
  };

  load();
}, [api, enabled, fetch, fetchData, query, refreshKey]);
```

**Verify:**
- Throttle the network to slow 3G in DevTools. On a heavy report (e.g. a Daily report) click Filter, then immediately change the page size from 10 to 1000 (or jump pages) while the first request is in flight. The grid must end up showing the data for the LAST action; the earlier (slower) response must not flash in or overwrite it.
- In the Network tab, confirm both requests still complete (no cancellation expected with this approach) but observe that the grid only ever reflects the most recent query — verify by comparing visible rows/totalCount against the active page/filter.
- Add a quick temporary `console.log(requestId, requestIdRef.current)` in the post-await guard to confirm stale responses hit the `return` branch and never call setData; remove the log after.
- Run `npm run build` in Frontend/ — it must compile with the new useRef import and no TS errors.
- Regression: a single Filter click still loads data, pagination still works, and the loading skeleton still clears normally when only one request is in flight.

**Pitfalls / rollback.** Increment the id synchronously in the effect body (`++requestIdRef.current`), NOT inside the async `load` callback — if you bump it inside `load` after an `await`, two rapid effect runs can both read the same id and the guard fails.; Do NOT use a `useState` counter for the id; state updates are async/batched and a stale closure would compare against an outdated value. A `useRef` is required so the comparison reads the live latest id.; The `finally` must also be guarded — if a stale response sets `loading=false` it would clear the skeleton while the genuinely-latest request is still loading, showing an empty/stale grid momentarily.; This guard does not cancel the HTTP request (no AbortController), so backend load from in-flight queries is unchanged — that is acceptable for the correctness fix. If you later want to also cancel, layer an AbortController + axios `signal` on top, but keep this guard because axios cancellation still allows a late `.then` unless guarded.; Rollback is trivial: revert the single file (remove the useRef, requestId capture, and the three guards) to restore prior behavior.

> 🔍 **Verifier note.** Locations are all correct. Note the legacy `fetch!(buildLegacyUrl(...))` path in BasicTable (line 223) has the same race, so a fix belongs in the BasicTable load effect itself (a request-id/staleness guard there covers both fetchData and fetch paths without needing to thread a signal through every report's fetchRows). A staleness guard in the effect is actually the more robust minimal fix here than AbortController alone, since axios cancellation would still need the guard to prevent a late .then from calling setData.


<a id="f-swallowed-errors"></a>
#### 🟡 MEDIUM — Backend errors are swallowed into generic strings; details discarded with empty catch blocks

**ID:** `swallowed-errors` &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Phase:** P2 &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Category:** Error handling / observability &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Effort:** Medium &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Confidence:** High

**Location(s):**
- `Frontend/src/components/My Components/Table/BasicTable.tsx:226-227`
- `Frontend/src/components/My Components/Table/BasicTable.tsx:257-258`
- `Frontend/src/hooks/useFormActions.tsx:45-48`
- `Frontend/src/hooks/useFormActions.tsx:57-60`
- `Frontend/src/Report/Page/GenericReportPage.tsx:639-643`

**Problem.** Catch blocks discard the actual error and replace it with a fixed message. BasicTable's grid catch is `} catch { setError('Failed to load table data.'); }` (line 226) — the error object isn't even bound, so the backend's status/message is unavailable. useFormActions shows a static `Modal.error('Something went wrong...')` for both Post and Put failures. The company-name lookup catch silently blanks the field. The Excel catch is generic. There is no central error normalization and (apart from the 401 interceptor) no logging.

**Impact.** Operators cannot tell a validation error from a server outage from a permission denial — every failure looks identical. Support/debugging on a government system becomes guesswork because the real error (400 with a server message, 500, network) is thrown away client-side. Genuine data problems can be hidden behind a bland 'Failed to load table data.'

**Recommendation.** Bind the caught error, extract `error.response?.status` and `error.response?.data` via a shared `parseApiError` helper, and surface a meaningful message (and a retry affordance) while logging the raw error. Distinguish 4xx (show server message) from 5xx/network (generic + retry). Consolidate this in the RTK Query baseQuery / a shared interceptor rather than per-call.

**🛠 How to Fix _(est. 1.5 hours)_**

Add a shared parseApiError helper and route all swallowed catches through it.

1. Create Frontend/src/utils/parseApiError.ts. Export a `ParsedApiError` type ({ status: number | null; message: string; isClientError: boolean; raw: unknown }) and a `parseApiError(error: unknown, fallback?: string)` function. Use axios's `isAxiosError` type guard (import { isAxiosError } from 'axios'; axios ^1.9.0 supports it). Logic: if !isAxiosError -> return generic fallback with status null. If `error.response` exists, read `status = error.response.status` and `data = error.response.data`; derive a serverMessage by: if typeof data === 'string' && data.trim() -> use it; else if data?.detail (ProblemDetails) -> use it; else if data?.title -> use it; else if data?.errors (ASP.NET ModelState) -> join the first messages. For status 400/404/409/422 (isClientError=true) return { status, message: serverMessage || fallback, isClientError: true }. For 401 return a session-expired message (the App.tsx interceptor already calls logout, so just surface text). For 5xx return fallback like 'The server encountered an error. Please try again.' (isClientError=false). If no response (network/timeout) return 'Could not reach the server. Check your connection and try again.' Always `console.error('[API error]', error)` inside the helper so the raw error is never lost. Provide a sensible default `fallback` param value of 'Something went wrong. Please try again.'
2. In Frontend/src/components/My Components/Table/BasicTable.tsx: add `import { parseApiError } from '../../../utils/parseApiError';` (verify the relative depth from src/components/My Components/Table/ -> src/utils; it is three levels up). Change the grid load catch at line 226-227 from `} catch { setError('Failed to load table data.'); }` to bind the error and call the helper: `} catch (err) { setError(parseApiError(err, 'Failed to load table data.').message); }`. Do the same for the Excel catch at lines 257-258: `} catch (err) { setError(parseApiError(err, 'Failed to generate Excel file.').message); }`. The existing Alert at lines 334-341 already renders the `error` string, so no UI change is needed.
3. In Frontend/src/hooks/useFormActions.tsx: import the helper `import { parseApiError } from '../utils/parseApiError';`. Replace the static `error()` modal so it accepts a message. Change the `error` helper (lines 29-34) to `const error = (msg = 'Something went wrong...') => { Modal.error({ title: 'Error', content: msg }); };`. Then in the New catch (lines 45-48) and Edit catch (lines 57-60) of `onFinish`, change `} catch (ex) { setWriteLoading(false); error(); }` to `} catch (ex) { setWriteLoading(false); error(parseApiError(ex).message); }`. Apply the same to the ToggleActive catch (lines 101-105) and to the onFinishStepper New catch (line 136-140, which already does console.error) and Edit catch (line 164-168): pass `parseApiError(ex).message` to `error(...)`. The helper already logs internally, so the existing `console.error(ex)` lines can stay or be removed.
4. In Frontend/src/Report/Page/GenericReportPage.tsx: the company-name lookup catch at lines 639-643 intentionally blanks the field on lookup failure (a not-found registration is an expected, non-error outcome), so keep the field-blanking behaviour BUT bind and log the error so a real server/network fault is observable: change `} catch {` to `} catch (err) {` and add `parseApiError(err, 'Company name lookup failed.');` (called for its console.error side effect) before/after the `if (!isCancelled) { form.setFieldValue(companyNameFilter.name, ''); }`. Add the import `import { parseApiError } from '../../utils/parseApiError';` (verify depth: src/Report/Page -> src/utils is two levels up). Do not pop a modal here — blanking the field silently to the user is the correct UX for a debounced lookup.
5. Optionally centralise logging in the axios response interceptor in Frontend/src/layouts/app/App.tsx (lines 132-157). It currently only handles 401. Leave the 401 logout branch intact; you may add an `else { console.error('[axios]', error.response?.status, error.config?.url); }` for non-401 failures so every rejected request is logged once even if a caller forgets to use the helper. Keep `return Promise.reject(error);` so per-call catches still run.
6. Run the type-check/lint to confirm no unused-var or import-path errors: from Frontend/, `npx tsc --noEmit` and `npm run lint`. Fix any path-depth mistakes in the new imports.

```tsx
// NEW FILE: Frontend/src/utils/parseApiError.ts
import { isAxiosError } from 'axios';

export type ParsedApiError = {
  status: number | null;
  message: string;
  isClientError: boolean;
  raw: unknown;
};

const extractServerMessage = (data: unknown): string | null => {
  if (typeof data === 'string' && data.trim()) return data.trim();
  if (data && typeof data === 'object') {
    const d = data as Record<string, unknown>;
    if (typeof d.detail === 'string' && d.detail) return d.detail;     // ProblemDetails
    if (typeof d.title === 'string' && d.title) return d.title;
    if (d.errors && typeof d.errors === 'object') {                    // ModelState
      const first = Object.values(d.errors as Record<string, string[]>)[0];
      if (Array.isArray(first) && first[0]) return first[0];
    }
  }
  return null;
};

export const parseApiError = (
  error: unknown,
  fallback = 'Something went wrong. Please try again.'
): ParsedApiError => {
  // Raw error is never discarded.
  console.error('[API error]', error);

  if (!isAxiosError(error)) {
    return { status: null, message: fallback, isClientError: false, raw: error };
  }

  const status = error.response?.status ?? null;

  if (!error.response) {
    return {
      status: null,
      message: 'Could not reach the server. Check your connection and try again.',
      isClientError: false,
      raw: error,
    };
  }
  if (status === 401) {
    return {
      status,
      message: 'Your session has expired. Please sign in again.',
      isClientError: true,
      raw: error,
    };
  }
  if (status !== null && status >= 400 && status < 500) {
    return {
      status,
      message: extractServerMessage(error.response.data) ?? fallback,
      isClientError: true,
      raw: error,
    };
  }
  return {
    status,
    message: 'The server encountered an error. Please try again.',
    isClientError: false,
    raw: error,
  };
};

// ---- BasicTable.tsx (grid load, was line 226) ----
// BEFORE
//   } catch {
//     setError('Failed to load table data.');
//   }
// AFTER
//   } catch (err) {
//     setError(parseApiError(err, 'Failed to load table data.').message);
//   }

// ---- BasicTable.tsx (Excel, was line 257) ----
// BEFORE: } catch { setError('Failed to generate Excel file.'); }
// AFTER:  } catch (err) { setError(parseApiError(err, 'Failed to generate Excel file.').message); }

// ---- useFormActions.tsx ----
// BEFORE
//   const error = () => { Modal.error({ title: 'Error', content: 'Something went wrong...' }); };
//   ... } catch (ex) { setWriteLoading(false); error(); }
// AFTER
//   const error = (msg = 'Something went wrong...') =>
//     Modal.error({ title: 'Error', content: msg });
//   ... } catch (ex) { setWriteLoading(false); error(parseApiError(ex).message); }
```

**Verify:**
- From Frontend/: `npx tsc --noEmit` and `npm run lint` pass with no unused `ex`/`err` or bad import-path errors.
- Run the app (`npm run dev`). Trigger a 400: open a report page and submit a filter the backend rejects (e.g. ToDate before FromDate hits the BadRequest("ToDate must be greater than or equal to FromDate.") path in the report controllers) — the BasicTable Alert / form Modal should now show that exact server sentence instead of 'Failed to load table data.' / 'Something went wrong...'.
- Simulate a network failure: stop the backend or block the API base URL in DevTools, then trigger a load — the Alert shows 'Could not reach the server...' and the browser console shows '[API error]' with the raw axios error object.
- Simulate a 500 (or hit an endpoint that throws server-side) — the message reads 'The server encountered an error. Please try again.' and the raw error is logged.
- Confirm the company-name lookup still silently blanks the field for an unknown registration number, but now logs '[API error]' to the console on a real fault.

**Pitfalls / rollback.** Import path depth differs per file: BasicTable.tsx is at src/components/My Components/Table/ so it needs `../../../utils/parseApiError`; GenericReportPage.tsx at src/Report/Page/ needs `../../utils/parseApiError`; useFormActions.tsx at src/hooks/ needs `../utils/parseApiError`. Verify each or tsc will fail.; Do NOT turn the company-name lookup (GenericReportPage 639-643) into a user-facing modal/alert — a not-found registration is an expected outcome of a debounced background lookup; only add logging while keeping the field-blanking behaviour.; ASP.NET BadRequest("...") returns the message as a raw JSON string, so `error.response.data` is a string in most 4xx cases here; the `typeof data === 'string'` branch must come first. Validation failures from model binding instead return a ProblemDetails/ModelState object — that's why the `detail`/`title`/`errors` branches exist. Don't assume one shape.; The 401 branch in App.tsx already calls auth.logout() and shows a message; parseApiError's 401 text is a fallback for callers and won't double-trigger logout — leave the App.tsx interceptor as the single place that logs out.; Keeping the existing `console.error(ex)` calls in onFinishStepper alongside the helper's internal console.error will double-log; harmless, but remove the redundant ones if clean logs matter.; Rollback is trivial: the change is additive (one new util file) plus localized catch edits; revert the edits and delete parseApiError.ts.

> 🔍 **Verifier note.** Minor inaccuracy in the recommendation, not the finding: this codebase uses axios (services/AxiosInstance.ts + a per-mount interceptor in App.tsx), not RTK Query — there is no baseQuery/createApi anywhere. The consolidation advice still holds but should target the axios response interceptor. Also worth noting the catches differ slightly in quality: useFormActions' New/Edit paths bind `ex` but never log it, whereas onFinishStepper and the Delete path do `console.error(ex)`; the BasicTable catches don't even bind the error. None of this changes the verdict or severity.


<a id="f-no-rtk-query-no-caching"></a>
#### 🟡 MEDIUM — All server data fetched manually with useState; no caching, dedup, or retry (Redux used only for theme)

**ID:** `no-rtk-query-no-caching` &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Phase:** P2 &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Category:** Architecture / data fetching &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Effort:** Large &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Confidence:** High

**Location(s):**
- `Frontend/src/redux/store.ts:12-32`
- `Frontend/src/Report/Page/GenericReportPage.tsx:552-662`
- `Frontend/src/services/BasicHttpServices.ts:7-53`
- `Frontend/src/hooks/useFetchData.tsx:1-28`

**Problem.** The Redux store contains only a `theme` slice (store.ts:12-14). grep for `createApi`/`createAsyncThunk`/`fetchBaseQuery` returns nothing — `@reduxjs/toolkit` is installed but not used for data. Every data interaction is a bespoke axios/fetch call with local `loading`/`error`/`data` useState triplets duplicated across BasicTable, ExportsDrive, GenericReportPage lookups, the form hooks, and useFetchData. Lookups (`ReportLookups/*`) are cached only in component-local `lookupOptions` state (GenericReportPage.tsx:511-609), so they are re-fetched fresh on every navigation to a report and never shared between reports.

**Impact.** No cross-component caching or request dedup means the same lookup lists (countries, business types, etc.) are fetched repeatedly; no automatic retry/backoff on transient failures; loading/error logic is reimplemented inconsistently (and sometimes wrongly) in every page, raising maintenance cost and bug surface across 155 report pages.

**Recommendation.** Adopt RTK Query (already have the dependency) or TanStack Query for all report/lookup/export fetching. Define endpoints once with a shared baseQuery (token injection, baseUrl, error normalization) and get caching, dedup, cancellation, and consistent loading/error state for free. At minimum, hoist lookup caching above the page (a single shared cache) so it survives navigation.

**🛠 How to Fix _(est. 1 day for the RTK Query infra + lookup/company-name migration + useFetchData fix; full migration of all report fetching is multi-day and incremental)_**

Adopt RTK Query (already shipped via @reduxjs/toolkit ^2.8.2 and already wired through <Provider store>) with one shared baseQuery that reuses the existing token-injection + baseUrl logic from AxiosInstance.ts. Migrate the highest-value shared fetches first — the ReportLookups endpoints in GenericReportPage — so lookups are cached/deduped across all ~134 report pages and survive navigation, then move report rows and Excel polling onto the same slice incrementally; also fix the latent useFetchData url-staleness bug the verifier flagged.

1. Create Frontend/src/redux/api/baseQuery.ts: an axiosBaseQuery() that wraps the existing axiosInstance (Frontend/src/services/AxiosInstance.ts) so token injection and baseUrl prefixing stay in one place. Signature: ({ url, method, data, params }) => async (...) returning { data } or { error: { status, data } }. Reusing axiosInstance (not fetchBaseQuery) keeps the Authorization header + envConfig.baseUrl behavior identical to AxiosInstance.ts.
2. Create Frontend/src/redux/api/reportApi.ts with createApi({ reducerPath: 'reportApi', baseQuery: axiosBaseQuery(), tagTypes: ['Lookup','CompanyName'], endpoints }). Define getLookup: builder.query<LookupOption[], string>({ query: (lookupName) => ({ url: `ReportLookups/${lookupName}`, method: 'GET' }), providesTags: (r,e,name) => [{ type:'Lookup', id:name }] }) and getCompanyName: builder.query<CompanyNameLookupResult, string>({ query: (companyRegistrationNo) => ({ url:'ReportLookups/company-name', method:'GET', params:{ companyRegistrationNo } }) }). Move the LookupOption and CompanyNameLookupResult interfaces (currently inline at GenericReportPage.tsx:60 and :72) into Frontend/src/types/ReportLookup.ts and import them in both files.
3. Wire the api slice into Frontend/src/redux/store.ts. Because the root reducer is wrapped in redux-persist, do NOT persist the RTK Query cache: add [reportApi.reducerPath]: reportApi.reducer to combineReducers, add blacklist: [reportApi.reducerPath] to persistConfig, and concat(reportApi.middleware) onto the existing getDefaultMiddleware({ serializableCheck: false }) chain. Extend the RootState interface to include [reportApi.reducerPath]: ReturnType<typeof reportApi.reducer>. Optionally add setupListeners(store.dispatch) in main.tsx for refetchOnFocus/refetchOnReconnect.
4. Refactor lookup loading in Frontend/src/Report/Page/GenericReportPage.tsx: replace the manual useEffect at lines 552-609 plus the lookupOptions/loadingLookupNames useState (511-516) with RTK Query. Drive one useGetLookupQuery(lookup.lookupName) per distinct lookup from reportLookupFilters (already computed at 542-550). Since the lookup count is dynamic, extract a small child component (e.g. <LookupLoader lookupName onLoaded>) rendered once per name, or a fixed-shape useReportLookups hook — never call the hook in a raw loop. RTK Query dedups identical lookupName requests across all mounted pages so countries/business-type lists are fetched once and shared.
5. Migrate the debounced company-name lookup (GenericReportPage.tsx:611-650) to useLazyGetCompanyNameQuery from reportApi: keep the 300ms setTimeout debounce but call the lazy trigger instead of axiosInstance.get('ReportLookups/company-name'). RTK Query caches by the companyRegistrationNo arg, so repeated lookups of the same reg-no are served from cache.
6. Fix the latent bug the verifier flagged in Frontend/src/hooks/useFetchData.tsx: the useCallback deps are [] while the effect deps are [url] (lines 19 and 23), so fetchData closes over a stale url. Preferred: delete the hook and replace call sites with an RTK Query endpoint; otherwise add url to the useCallback dependency array. Confirm call sites first with grep -rn useFetchData Frontend/src.
7. Document the pattern (single createApi slice, axiosBaseQuery, providesTags/invalidatesTags) so the report-rows POST (fetchRows, GenericReportPage.tsx:652-662) and the async Excel job polling can be migrated onto reportApi incrementally rather than all at once.

```ts
// Frontend/src/redux/api/baseQuery.ts  (NEW)
import type { BaseQueryFn } from '@reduxjs/toolkit/query';
import type { AxiosRequestConfig, AxiosError } from 'axios';
import axiosInstance from '../../services/AxiosInstance';

export const axiosBaseQuery =
  (): BaseQueryFn<
    { url: string; method?: AxiosRequestConfig['method']; data?: unknown; params?: unknown },
    unknown,
    { status?: number; data?: unknown }
  > =>
  async ({ url, method = 'GET', data, params }) => {
    try {
      const result = await axiosInstance({ url, method, data, params });
      return { data: result.data };
    } catch (axiosError) {
      const err = axiosError as AxiosError;
      return { error: { status: err.response?.status, data: err.response?.data || err.message } };
    }
  };

// Frontend/src/redux/api/reportApi.ts  (NEW)
import { createApi } from '@reduxjs/toolkit/query/react';
import { axiosBaseQuery } from './baseQuery';
import type { LookupOption, CompanyNameLookupResult } from '../../types/ReportLookup';

export const reportApi = createApi({
  reducerPath: 'reportApi',
  baseQuery: axiosBaseQuery(),
  tagTypes: ['Lookup', 'CompanyName'],
  endpoints: (builder) => ({
    getLookup: builder.query<LookupOption[], string>({
      query: (lookupName) => ({ url: `ReportLookups/${lookupName}`, method: 'GET' }),
      providesTags: (_r, _e, name) => [{ type: 'Lookup', id: name }],
    }),
    getCompanyName: builder.query<CompanyNameLookupResult, string>({
      query: (companyRegistrationNo) => ({
        url: 'ReportLookups/company-name',
        method: 'GET',
        params: { companyRegistrationNo },
      }),
    }),
  }),
});

export const { useGetLookupQuery, useLazyGetCompanyNameQuery } = reportApi;

// Frontend/src/redux/store.ts  (BEFORE -> AFTER)
- const rootReducer = combineReducers({
-   theme: themeReducer,
- });
+ import { reportApi } from './api/reportApi';
+ const rootReducer = combineReducers({
+   theme: themeReducer,
+   [reportApi.reducerPath]: reportApi.reducer,
+ });

- const persistConfig: PersistConfig<RootState> = { key: 'root', storage, version: 1 };
+ const persistConfig: PersistConfig<RootState> = {
+   key: 'root', storage, version: 1,
+   blacklist: [reportApi.reducerPath], // never persist RTK Query cache
+ };

  export const store = configureStore({
    reducer: persistedReducer,
    middleware: (getDefaultMiddleware) =>
-     getDefaultMiddleware({ serializableCheck: false }),
+     getDefaultMiddleware({ serializableCheck: false }).concat(reportApi.middleware),
  });
```

**Verify:**
- Run grep -rn 'createApi' Frontend/src and confirm reportApi.ts matches (it previously returned nothing).
- npm run build (or tsc -b) in Frontend passes with RootState now including [reportApi.reducerPath].
- In the running app, open DevTools Network, navigate to a report whose filters use a shared lookup (countries/business types), then navigate to a second report using the same lookup: confirm ReportLookups/<name> is requested only ONCE for the session (served from cache on the second page) instead of refetching on every navigation.
- With Redux DevTools, confirm a 'reportApi' slice appears holding cached lookup entries keyed by lookupName, and that it is NOT present in localStorage under persist:root (verifies the blacklist).
- Block ReportLookups in DevTools to force a transient failure and confirm RTK Query surfaces isError/error and that refetch recovers, replacing the inconsistent manual loading/error handling.
- Verify the useFetchData fix: the hook re-fetches when url changes (url added to useCallback deps), or grep -rn useFetchData Frontend/src shows no remaining call sites if the hook was removed.

**Pitfalls / rollback.** redux-persist ordering: the api slice MUST be in persistConfig.blacklist, otherwise the serialized RTK Query cache rehydrates stale and PersistGate can desync the cache — keep it in memory only. Add reportApi.middleware to the chain before shipping, or queries silently never run. Keep using the existing axiosInstance inside axiosBaseQuery instead of fetchBaseQuery so JWT Authorization header injection and envConfig.baseUrl prefixing (AxiosInstance.ts) are not duplicated and cannot drift from auth behavior. Lookup count is dynamic per report, so you cannot call useGetLookupQuery in a raw loop (rules-of-hooks) — render one query hook per lookup via a child component or fixed-shape hook. This is incremental and reversible: migrating only lookups first leaves the old fetchRows/Excel paths untouched. Rollback = remove reportApi from combineReducers/middleware/blacklist and restore GenericReportPage's local lookupOptions useState.

> 🔍 **Verifier note.** Minor imprecision: the "155 report pages" figure is loose — the config files (reportConfigs.ts) contain ~134 controllerName entries; the ~155 number is the broader report-controller count used elsewhere in the project (CLAUDE.md says ~134, memory says ~155). The order of magnitude and the architectural point are sound, so this does not change the verdict. Locations are all accurate; the line ranges match. The useFetchData url-staleness bug (useCallback deps [] vs effect deps [url]) is extra supporting evidence for the "sometimes wrongly" claim and is itself a latent defect worth noting.


<a id="f-redundant-json-roundtrip"></a>
#### 🔵 LOW — Every API response is round-tripped through JSON.parse(JSON.stringify()) before use

**ID:** `redundant-json-roundtrip` &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Phase:** P3 &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Category:** Performance / data handling &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Effort:** Trivial &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Confidence:** High

**Location(s):**
- `Frontend/src/services/BasicHttpServices.ts:10-11`
- `Frontend/src/services/BasicHttpServices.ts:17-18`
- `Frontend/src/services/BasicHttpServices.ts:24-25`
- `Frontend/src/services/BasicHttpServices.ts:37-38`
- `Frontend/src/services/BasicHttpServices.ts:44-45`
- `Frontend/src/hooks/useFormload.tsx:19-20`

**Problem.** Axios already parses the JSON body into `resp.data`. Every helper then does `JSON.parse(JSON.stringify(data))` to produce the return value, and useFormLoad does it a second time on the already-parsed result. This is a pure-overhead deep clone of the entire payload.

**Impact.** For large report/company payloads this doubles serialization work on the main thread for no benefit, and it silently drops anything non-JSON-serializable. It also obscures intent and discards typing. Minor but pervasive (every read/write goes through these helpers).

**Recommendation.** Return `resp.data` directly (it is already a parsed object). If a defensive clone is ever needed, use `structuredClone`. Remove the duplicate parse in useFormLoad.

**Example:**

```
// current
const data = await resp.data;
const responseData = JSON.parse(JSON.stringify(data));
return responseData;

// fixed
return resp.data;
```

**🛠 How to Fix _(est. 15 min)_**

Axios's default instance already parses the response body into `resp.data` as a live JS object, so the `JSON.parse(JSON.stringify(...))` deep-clone in each `BasicHttpServices` helper (and the duplicate clone in `useFormLoad`) is pure overhead. Return `resp.data` directly from each helper and drop the redundant re-parse in the hook.

1. Open Frontend/src/services/BasicHttpServices.ts. There are SIX exported helpers, not five — the audit listed Get/GetSingle/Post/Put/Delete (lines 10-11, 17-18, 24-25, 37-38, 44-45) but ToggleUserActive at lines 51-52 has the identical pattern and must be fixed too.
2. In `Get` (lines 8-11): collapse the body to `const resp = await axiosInstance.get(url); return resp.data;` removing the `await resp.data` and the `JSON.parse(JSON.stringify(data))` clone. (resp.data is not a Promise, so `await` on it is unnecessary.)
3. In `GetSingle` (lines 15-18): replace the `const data = await resp.data;` + clone with `return resp.data;`.
4. In `Post` (lines 22-25): replace `const temp = await resp.data;` + clone with `return resp.data;`. Keep the `await axiosInstance.post(url, data)` call unchanged.
5. In `Put` (lines 35-38): keep the `jsonObject.id = id` mutation and the `axiosInstance.put(url + '/' + id, jsonObject)` call; replace the temp+clone with `return resp.data;`.
6. In `Delete` (lines 42-45): replace the temp+clone with `return resp.data;`.
7. In `ToggleUserActive` (lines 49-52): replace the temp+clone with `return resp.data;`.
8. Note a pre-existing type inconsistency: GetSingle/Post/Put/Delete/ToggleUserActive are declared `Promise<AnyObject>` but currently annotate the local as `: PaginationType`. By returning `resp.data` directly you let the function's declared return type apply — keep each function's existing `Promise<...>` signature; do not re-introduce the `PaginationType` local annotation.
9. Open Frontend/src/hooks/useFormload.tsx. At lines 19-21 replace `const resp = JSON.parse(JSON.stringify(await GetSingle(url + '/' + id)));` with `const resp = await GetSingle(url + '/' + id);`. GetSingle now already returns a parsed object, so the second clone is redundant. Everything downstream (`const { applicationNo } = resp;`, `formRef.current?.setFieldsValue(resp)`, `Object.assign(result, resp)`) works unchanged on the plain object.
10. If a defensive clone is genuinely wanted anywhere later, use the built-in `structuredClone(resp.data)` instead of the JSON round-trip — but it is not needed for these single-record form-load / user-CRUD flows.

```ts
// Frontend/src/services/BasicHttpServices.ts
// --- before (one representative helper) ---
export const Get = async (url: string): Promise<PaginationType> => {
  const resp = await axiosInstance.get(url);
  const data = await resp.data;
  const responseData: PaginationType = JSON.parse(JSON.stringify(data));
  return responseData;
};
// --- after ---
export const Get = async (url: string): Promise<PaginationType> => {
  const resp = await axiosInstance.get(url);
  return resp.data;
};

// Apply the same collapse to GetSingle, Post, Put, Delete, ToggleUserActive
// (keep their post/put/delete calls and the Put `jsonObject.id = id` mutation):
export const Post = async (url: string, data: unknown): Promise<AnyObject> => {
  const resp = await axiosInstance.post(url, data);
  return resp.data;
};

export const ToggleUserActive = async (url: string, id: string): Promise<AnyObject> => {
  const resp = await axiosInstance.put(`${url}/ToggleActive/${id}`);
  return resp.data;
};

// Frontend/src/hooks/useFormload.tsx (lines 19-21)
// --- before ---
const resp = JSON.parse(
  JSON.stringify(await GetSingle(url + '/' + id))
);
// --- after ---
const resp = await GetSingle(url + '/' + id);
```

**Verify:**
- From Frontend/, run `npx tsc --noEmit` (or `npm run build`) to confirm no type errors after removing the `PaginationType` local annotations.
- Run `npx eslint src/services/BasicHttpServices.ts src/hooks/useFormload.tsx` to confirm no new lint issues (unused `data`/`temp` vars are gone).
- Manually exercise the 3 consuming files in the running app: load a record-edit form (useFormload.tsx path), perform a user create/update/delete and the active-toggle in pages/User/UserList.tsx (useFormActions.tsx). Confirm the form fields populate and CRUD round-trips behave identically — same data appears, applicationNo still resolves.
- Optional regression check: `grep -rn 'JSON.parse(JSON.stringify' Frontend/src/services Frontend/src/hooks` should return no matches for these files.

**Pitfalls / rollback.** Scope/impact caveat per the verifier: these helpers are only imported by hooks/useFormload.tsx, hooks/useFormActions.tsx and pages/User/UserList.tsx — single-record form-load and user-CRUD flows with small payloads. The large report grids (BasicTable.tsx / reportConfigs) do their own fetching and never route through BasicHttpServices, so the real perf win is negligible; this is a clean-up, not a hot-path optimization. Functional risk: the JSON round-trip silently dropped any non-serializable fields (undefined, Date instances, functions). If any consumer relied on a field being absent/undefined after the clone, behavior could change subtly — but these endpoints return plain JSON DTOs, so this is not expected. Do not add `transformResponse` to AxiosInstance.ts; the default already parses JSON. No data migration or ordering constraints; rollback is reverting the two files.

> 🔍 **Verifier note.** Severity Low is correct (borderline Info). One caveat on the IMPACT framing, not the bug: these helpers are imported by only 3 files (hooks/useFormload.tsx, hooks/useFormActions.tsx, pages/User/UserList.tsx). They drive form-load and user-CRUD flows, which are single-record/small payloads — NOT the large report grids. The big report tables (BasicTable.tsx / reportConfigs) do their own fetching and do not route through BasicHttpServices. So the finding's phrasing 'every API response' / 'large report/company payloads doubles serialization work' is overstated; the realistic payloads here are small, which makes the perf impact negligible and reinforces Low. The duplicate parse in useFormLoad operates on a single form record. The bug is genuine and the fix is a clean, low-risk cleanup, but its practical cost is smaller than the impact text implies.


<a id="f-no-error-boundary"></a>
#### 🔵 LOW — No React error boundary anywhere; a single render throw blanks the entire admin app

**ID:** `no-error-boundary` &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Phase:** P3 &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Category:** Error handling / resilience &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Effort:** Small &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Confidence:** High

**Location(s):**
- `Frontend/src/App.tsx:114 (RouterProvider has no top-level wrapper); residual gap only on routes lacking errorElement, e.g. Frontend/src/routes/routes.tsx:101-107 (/Certificate)`
- `Frontend/src/App.tsx:30-117`
- `Frontend/src/layouts/app/App.tsx:159-292`

**Problem.** grep for `ErrorBoundary`, `componentDidCatch`, `getDerivedStateFromError` returns zero hits across src. `RouterProvider` is rendered directly under `ConfigProvider`/`HelmetProvider` with nothing to catch render-time exceptions. Report rendering does plenty that can throw on unexpected backend shapes — e.g. custom `render` functions and `value.toString()` in BasicTable (renderCell line 280), money/date parsing in GenericReportPage, and the dashboard card null-deref noted separately.

**Impact.** If any report page throws while rendering (a malformed row, a null where an object is expected, a parsing edge case), React unmounts the whole tree and the operator sees a blank white screen with no recovery path other than a full reload — losing their place and filter state. For an internal government tool used daily, this is a real availability problem.

**Recommendation.** Add a top-level ErrorBoundary around `RouterProvider` (and ideally a per-route one around report content) that renders a fallback with a 'reload / go back' action and logs the error. React Router's `errorElement` per route is a low-effort option that also catches loader errors.

**🛠 How to Fix _(est. 30 min)_**

Add a single top-level React class `ErrorBoundary` (the only way to catch render throws in React 19 — there are no hooks for `getDerivedStateFromError`/`componentDidCatch`) and wrap `RouterProvider` with it in `Frontend/src/App.tsx`, rendering an antd `Result` fallback with a reload action. Also close the residual gap by adding `errorElement: <ErrorPage />` to the two `/Certificate` routes in `routes.tsx`, which currently have no error handling. This is defense-in-depth: per-route `errorElement` already covers the daily report pages.

1. Create the boundary component at Frontend/src/components/ErrorBoundary/ErrorBoundary.tsx as a React class component. It is the only React-supported way to catch render-time throws (no hook equivalent exists in React 19). Implement static getDerivedStateFromError to flip a hasError flag, componentDidCatch(error, info) to console.error the error and component stack, and render() that shows an antd Result with a RefreshBtn fallback when hasError is true, otherwise this.props.children. Reuse the existing RefreshBtn component (Frontend/src/components/RefreshBtn/RefreshBtn.tsx, which already calls window.location.reload()) so the UX matches ErrorPage.
2. Export it from the components barrel Frontend/src/components/index.ts: add `export { ErrorBoundary } from './ErrorBoundary/ErrorBoundary.tsx';` next to the existing BackBtn (line 1) / RefreshBtn (line 15) exports.
3. In Frontend/src/App.tsx, import ErrorBoundary from './components' (add to the import block near lines 1-8) and wrap `<RouterProvider router={routes} />` at line 114 with `<ErrorBoundary>...</ErrorBoundary>`. Keep it inside StylesContext.Provider so the fallback still renders inside ConfigProvider/HelmetProvider and inherits the antd theme.
4. Close the residual per-route gap: in Frontend/src/routes/routes.tsx, add `errorElement: <ErrorPage />` to both `/Certificate` route objects (lines 100-103 `path: 'Certificate'` and lines 104-107 `path: 'Certificate/:id'`). ErrorPage is already imported at line 9 of routes.tsx, so no new import is needed. This brings them in line with the other 8 routes that already declare errorElement.
5. Do NOT change the existing per-route `errorElement: <ErrorPage />` on /, /auth, /dashboards, /User, /Report, /Timeline, /Test, errors — they already handle router/loader errors and remain the primary mechanism for report-page throws. The new ErrorBoundary is a backstop only.

```tsx
// NEW FILE: Frontend/src/components/ErrorBoundary/ErrorBoundary.tsx
import { Component, ErrorInfo, ReactNode } from 'react';
import { Result } from 'antd';
import { RefreshBtn } from '../RefreshBtn/RefreshBtn.tsx';

type Props = { children: ReactNode };
type State = { hasError: boolean };

export class ErrorBoundary extends Component<Props, State> {
  state: State = { hasError: false };

  static getDerivedStateFromError(): State {
    return { hasError: true };
  }

  componentDidCatch(error: Error, info: ErrorInfo) {
    // Mirror ErrorPage's logging so chrome-level throws are visible in the console.
    console.error(error, info.componentStack);
  }

  render() {
    if (this.state.hasError) {
      return (
        <Result
          status="error"
          title="Oops!"
          subTitle="Sorry, an unexpected error has occurred."
          extra={[<RefreshBtn key="refresh" type="primary" />]}
        />
      );
    }
    return this.props.children;
  }
}

// ---- Frontend/src/components/index.ts (add near line 1/15) ----
export { ErrorBoundary } from './ErrorBoundary/ErrorBoundary.tsx';

// ---- Frontend/src/App.tsx ----
// import { RouterProvider } from 'react-router-dom';
// + import { ErrorBoundary } from './components';
// ...
-          <RouterProvider router={routes} />
+          <ErrorBoundary>
+            <RouterProvider router={routes} />
+          </ErrorBoundary>
```
