**Pitfalls / rollback.** The audit lists only 3 locations but the repo actually has 6 files with this pattern (EarningsCard, CampaignsAdsCard, DeliveryAnalyticsCard were missed) — fix all 6 or the bundle pattern persists. Order matters: delete the barrel re-export lines in the same commit as deleting the files, otherwise `tsc`/Vite will fail to resolve the missing module. Do NOT remove lodash from package.json until grep confirms no remaining source imports it. lodash `chain()` (Faqs.tsx, Help.tsx, CampaignsAdsCard.tsx) is the one case that does not tree-shake via per-method imports — if those files are kept rather than deleted, either rewrite the chain into plain function composition or move to lodash-es. Note CampaignsAdsCard.tsx:42 `_.toLowerCase()` is called with no argument (a latent bug); deleting the file is the safest resolution rather than salvaging it. The verifier flagged that the 'tens of KB shipped to users' impact is likely overstated since these are unrouted (code-split out of the prod bundle), so this is low-priority cleanup, not a perf regression fix.

> 🔍 **Verifier note.** Severity Low is correct (arguably could be Info). One caveat: because these files are unrouted dead code with no entry path, a correctly configured Vite production build with code-splitting would likely not include them in any shipped chunk, so the "tens of KB of unnecessary JS shipped to users" impact may be overstated — the concrete cost is lingering dead demo code plus an unnecessarily non-tree-shakeable import pattern that would bloat the bundle if any of these files were ever wired into a route. Recommendation (delete the files, or switch to per-method/lodash-es imports) is sound. This does not change the Low rating, so keeping verdict=Confirmed.


<a id="f-basictable-no-row-cell-memo"></a>
#### 🔵 LOW — BasicTable rows/cells not memoized; full re-render on every state change

**ID:** `basictable-no-row-cell-memo` &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Phase:** P3 &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Category:** Re-render optimization &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Effort:** Medium &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Confidence:** High

**Location(s):**
- `Frontend/src/components/My Components/Table/BasicTable.tsx:264-281`
- `Frontend/src/components/My Components/Table/BasicTable.tsx:398-432`

**Problem.** No React.memo (0 in codebase); renderCell/getRowKey/tbody map run fully every render, so any state change re-renders every visible cell. Compounds the unvirtualized-table issue.

**Impact.** Avoidable CPU per interaction proportional to row*column count; meaningful at 100-1000 rows.

**Recommendation.** Memoize a Row component; wrap renderCell/getRowKey in useCallback.

**🛠 How to Fix _(est. 45 min)_**

Extract each tbody row into a React.memo'd Row component fed by stable useCallback'd renderCell/getRowKey, so unrelated re-renders skip the body.

1. Open Frontend/src/components/My Components/Table/BasicTable.tsx. Note `useCallback` is NOT currently imported (line 1 imports only `useEffect, useMemo, useState`). Either add `useCallback` to that import or reference it as `React.useCallback` as shown in the patch.
2. Add a `BasicTableRow` component just above `export const BasicTable` (after `buildLegacyUrl`, ~line 119). Define `BasicTableRowInner<T extends AnyObject>` rendering the `<tr>` exactly as the current inline body row (lines 402-422): row-number `<td>`, the `normalizedColumns.map` cell `<td>`s calling `renderCell`, and the optional action `<td>` (`actionComponent` via `React.createElement` else `<TableAction id={row['id']} />`). Wrap it: `const BasicTableRow = React.memo(BasicTableRowInner) as typeof BasicTableRowInner;` — the cast preserves the generic signature React.memo would otherwise widen.
3. Pass the already-computed row number into the Row as a prop (`rowNumber={index + 1 + query.pageIndex * pageSize}`) instead of recomputing inside the Row, so the Row does not need `pageIndex`/`pageSize` and stays cheap to compare.
4. Replace the `getRowKey` function (lines 264-270) with a `React.useCallback` version, deps `[rowKey, pageIndex]` (it reads `pageIndex` in the fallback key). This keeps `getRowKey` referentially stable across loading/error re-renders.
5. Replace the `renderCell` function (lines 272-281) with a `React.useCallback` version, deps `[]` — it only closes over its arguments and the per-column `column.render`, so an empty dep array is correct and gives a permanently stable reference (required for `React.memo` on the Row to actually skip).
6. In the tbody (lines 401-423), replace the inline `<tr>...</tr>` returned by `data.data.map` with `<BasicTableRow<T> ... />`, forwarding `row`, `index`, `rowNumber`, `columns={normalizedColumns}`, `showRowNumber`, `showActions={shouldShowActions}`, `actionComponent`, and `renderCell`. Keep `key={getRowKey(row, index)}` on the `<BasicTableRow>` element.
7. Leave the skeleton `<tbody>` (434-454), the empty-state `<tr>` (425-429), the `<tfoot>` total row (456-488), and the `<thead>` untouched — only the data-row map changes.
8. Run the frontend type-check/lint (`npm run lint` / `tsc`) from Frontend/ to confirm the generic cast and new component compile with no `any` regressions.

```tsx
// === 1. Add a memoized Row component ABOVE `export const BasicTable` (after buildLegacyUrl, ~line 119) ===

interface BasicTableRowProps<T extends AnyObject> {
  row: T;
  index: number;
  rowNumber: number;          // index + 1 + pageIndex*pageSize, computed by parent
  columns: BasicTableColumn<T>[];
  showRowNumber: boolean;
  showActions: boolean;
  actionComponent?: React.FC<{ id: string }>;
  renderCell: (column: BasicTableColumn<T>, row: T, index: number) => React.ReactNode;
}

function BasicTableRowInner<T extends AnyObject>({
  row,
  index,
  rowNumber,
  columns,
  showRowNumber,
  showActions,
  actionComponent,
  renderCell,
}: BasicTableRowProps<T>) {
  return (
    <tr>
      {showRowNumber && <td>{rowNumber}</td>}
      {columns.map((column) => (
        <td key={column.key.toString()}>{renderCell(column, row, index)}</td>
      ))}
      {showActions && (
        <td className="action-column-cell">
          {actionComponent ? (
            React.createElement(actionComponent, { id: row['id'] })
          ) : (
            <TableAction id={row['id']} />
          )}
        </td>
      )}
    </tr>
  );
}
// Cast keeps the generic signature after React.memo erases it.
const BasicTableRow = React.memo(BasicTableRowInner) as typeof BasicTableRowInner;

// === 2. Wrap renderCell + getRowKey in useCallback (replace lines 264-281) ===
// before:
//   const getRowKey = (row: T, index: number) => { ... };
//   const renderCell = (column, row, index) => { ... };
// after:
const getRowKey = React.useCallback(
  (row: T, index: number) => {
    if (typeof rowKey === 'function') {
      return rowKey(row, index);
    }
    return (rowKey ? row[rowKey] : row['id']) ?? `${pageIndex}-${index}`;
  },
  [rowKey, pageIndex]
);

const renderCell = React.useCallback(
  (column: BasicTableColumn<T>, row: T, index: number) => {
    const key = (column.dataIndex ?? column.key).toString();
    const value = row[key];
    if (column.render) {
      return column.render(value, row, index);
    }
    return value?.toString() ?? 'N/A';
  },
  []
);

// === 3. Use the memoized Row in the tbody map (replace the inline <tr> at lines 401-422) ===
{data.data.map((row, index) => (
  <BasicTableRow<T>
    key={getRowKey(row, index)}
    row={row}
    index={index}
    rowNumber={index + 1 + query.pageIndex * pageSize}
    columns={normalizedColumns}
    showRowNumber={showRowNumber}
    showActions={shouldShowActions}
    actionComponent={actionComponent}
    renderCell={renderCell}
  />
))}
```

**Verify:**
- From Frontend/, run `npm run build` (or `npx tsc --noEmit`) — must compile clean; the `as typeof BasicTableRowInner` cast must preserve `<BasicTableRow<T> />` generic usage with no TS errors.
- Run `npm run lint` from Frontend/ — no new warnings (especially react-hooks/exhaustive-deps on the new useCallback hooks).
- Load any report page (e.g. via GenericReportPage), apply filters to populate the grid, set page size to 100 or 1000. Open React DevTools Profiler, enable 'Highlight updates when components render', then type in the company filter / trigger an unrelated parent state change — the body `BasicTableRow`s should NOT flash/re-render, only the changed parts should.
- Confirm rendered output is byte-identical to before: row numbering (`index + 1 + pageIndex*pageSize`), cell values (`value?.toString() ?? 'N/A'`), custom `column.render` cells, action column, and the Excel export (`document.getElementById(tableId)` table_to_book) all still work.
- Confirm pagination still updates row numbers correctly after changing page — the `rowNumber` prop must reflect the new `pageIndex`.

**Pitfalls / rollback.** React.memo erases the generic type parameter; without `as typeof BasicTableRowInner` the call site `<BasicTableRow<T> />` will fail to type-check. Keep the cast.; renderCell's useCallback dep array must stay `[]`. If you accidentally add `normalizedColumns` or `data`, the reference changes every render and React.memo on the Row stops skipping — you get zero benefit (this is exactly the verifier's warning that useCallback alone is useless without the memoized Row, and vice-versa).; Do not move the row-number computation back inside the Row using `pageIndex`/`pageSize` — passing the precomputed `rowNumber` prop keeps the Row's prop set minimal and its shallow comparison fast.; Default shallow compare is correct here because all props (`row`, `columns`=`normalizedColumns` from useMemo, the useCallback'd `renderCell`, primitives) are referentially stable across unrelated re-renders. Do NOT add a custom `areEqual` comparator — it is unnecessary and risks stale cells if `row` identity changes on real data updates.; Severity is Low and the verifier confirmed real-world impact is a few ms even at the 1000-row max (cheap string cells, default pageSize 10). This is a perf hygiene change, not a correctness fix — keep it scoped to the body map and ship it alongside (not instead of) the separately-reported table-virtualization work. Rollback is trivial: revert the three edits, restore the inline `<tr>`.; If you choose to add `useCallback` to the line-1 import instead of using `React.useCallback`, update both new hooks consistently.

> 🔍 **Verifier note.** Severity Low is correctly rated and I am leaving it as-is. Mitigating context that caps real-world impact: default pageSize is 10 (line 135), and cells are cheap plain-string renders (value?.toString()), not heavy components — even at the 1000-row max (pageSizeOptions includes 1000, line 497) a full body re-render is a few ms, not a jank source. The "100-1000 rows" framing is plausible only at the top of that range. Minor recommendation nuance: wrapping renderCell/getRowKey in useCallback alone does nothing unless rows are split into a React.memo'd Row component (the memoized-Row part of the recommendation is the actual fix). Compounds the separately-reported unvirtualized-table issue. Files: Frontend/src/components/My Components/Table/BasicTable.tsx (264-281 functions, 398-432 tbody map, line 121 unmemoized export); contributing parent re-render triggers in Frontend/src/Report/Page/GenericReportPage.tsx (lines 524, 726-732).


<a id="f-scrolltotop-smooth-on-every-nav"></a>
#### ⚪ INFO — ScrollToTop runs smooth-scroll on every route change

**ID:** `scrolltotop-smooth-on-every-nav` &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Phase:** P3 &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Category:** Rendering &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Effort:** Trivial &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Confidence:** High

**Location(s):**
- `Frontend/src/routes/routes.tsx:28-54`

**Problem.** PageWrapper mounts ScrollToTop firing window.scrollTo({behavior:'smooth'}) on each pathname change, competing with layout/paint after a large table renders.

**Impact.** Minor: brief animated scroll + extra layout per navigation.

**Recommendation.** Use behavior:'auto' or react-router ScrollRestoration.

**🛠 How to Fix _(est. 5 min)_**

Change the one-line scroll behavior in the existing ScrollToTop component from animated smooth scrolling to instant ('auto'), so each route change no longer fires a multi-frame scroll animation that competes with paint after a large report table renders. This is an Info-level cleanup contained to a single file.

1. Open Frontend/src/routes/routes.tsx.
2. In the ScrollToTop component (lines 28-40), inside the useEffect's window.scrollTo({...}) call, change the property `behavior: 'smooth'` to `behavior: 'auto'`. Leave `top: 0` and `left: 0` and the `[pathname]` dependency array unchanged.
3. Do not change the PageWrapper structure or where ScrollToTop is mounted — keeping it at the layout level (per /Report, /User, /Timeline group) is fine; the only issue was the animated behavior, and the useEffect correctly still resets scroll on each pathname change.
4. (Optional, only if a future cleanup is desired) react-router-dom v7.6.2 is installed, so the component could later be replaced with the built-in <ScrollRestoration /> placed in the layout, but this is NOT required to resolve this Info finding and would change behavior (it restores prior scroll positions on back/forward rather than always scrolling to top).

```tsx
export const ScrollToTop: React.FC = () => {
  const { pathname } = useLocation();

  useEffect(() => {
    window.scrollTo({
      top: 0,
      left: 0,
-     behavior: 'smooth',
+     behavior: 'auto',
    }); // Scroll to the top when the location changes
  }, [pathname]);

  return null; // This component doesn't render anything
};
```

**Verify:**
- Run the frontend (npm run dev in Frontend/) and navigate between report routes (e.g. /Report/... pages with large tables). Confirm the viewport jumps to the top instantly on each navigation with no visible animated scroll.
- Grep to confirm the change landed and no smooth behavior remains: `grep -rn "behavior:" Frontend/src/routes/routes.tsx` should show only `behavior: 'auto'`.
- Run `npx tsc --noEmit` (or `npm run build`) in Frontend/ to confirm no type errors were introduced.

**Pitfalls / rollback.** No data migration or ordering constraints. Rollback is a one-line revert back to 'smooth'. Note this changes the visible behavior from an animated scroll to an instant jump — if any stakeholder specifically wanted the animation, flag it, but the audit explicitly recommends 'auto'. Do not swap in react-router's <ScrollRestoration /> as a drop-in replacement without realizing it restores previous scroll positions on back/forward navigation rather than always returning to top.

> 🔍 **Verifier note.** Minor caveat: ScrollToTop is mounted at the layout level (per route group like /Report, /User, /Timeline) rather than re-mounted for every individual child route, but since pathname changes on each navigation the useEffect still fires per nav, so the finding holds. The 'extra layout per navigation' impact wording is slightly overstated (scrollTo's smooth animation competes with paint but doesn't synchronously reflow the table), which is why this remains correctly rated Info rather than anything higher.


---

### Frontend — UI / UX & Accessibility

> The report frontend is a config-driven system (GenericReportPage + BasicTable) covering ~134 government trade reports. The Excel-export queue page (ExportsDrive) and the lookup/filter form are well built (loading states, toasts, Popconfirm, skeletons, idle text). However the core report grid has serious UX gaps: it advertises sorting and searching but wires neither, so operators cannot sort or find rows inside reports that return thousands of records. Navigation and i18n are the weakest areas — there is no in-app search over the 134-item sidebar, no internationalization despite a Myanmar/English mandate (and no AntD locale, so all date pickers/pagination render English-only), and the page breadcrumb collapses the current report name to "...". A few correctness/feedback issues round it out: the sign-in form ships hardcoded demo credentials and weak validation, the User list renders a raw password column, and client-side Excel export silently exports only the current page.

<a id="f-userlist-password-column"></a>
#### 🟠 HIGH — User list renders a 'password' column and can export it client-side

**ID:** `userlist-password-column` &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Phase:** P0 &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Category:** Sensitive data display &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Effort:** Trivial &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Confidence:** High

**Location(s):**
- `Frontend/src/pages/User/UserList.tsx:27-35`
- `Frontend/src/components/My Components/Table/BasicTable.tsx:236-244`

**Problem.** UserList passes displayData={['name', 'password', 'permission', 'isActive', 'id']} (line 29), so the admin user grid renders a column of user passwords directly in the table. Because UserList provides no onExcel, the Excel button falls through to exportClientTableToExcel (BasicTable.tsx:236-244), which serializes the visible DOM table — including the password column — to an .xlsx file.

**Impact.** Anyone who can open the User list sees other users' passwords on screen and can one-click export them to a spreadsheet (shoulder-surfing, screenshots, leaked exports). For a government admin system this is a serious confidentiality exposure even before considering the backend returning the field.

**Recommendation.** Remove 'password' from displayData (never display credentials). Ensure the User API does not return the password field at all. If a credential-management UI is needed, use a reset-password action rather than showing the value.

**🛠 How to Fix _(est. 30 min (frontend + DTO backend fix); hashing follow-up is a separate ~1 day task)_**

Stop exposing credentials on the User grid by removing 'password' from the table's displayData (this also kills its leak into the client-side Excel export), then harden the backend so GET /api/User never serializes Password — without breaking the login path that still needs Password during deserialization.

1. Frontend (primary, trivial): In /Users/saobranaung/Code/Ministry of Commerce/tradenet-admin-report/tradenet-admin-report/Frontend/src/pages/User/UserList.tsx line 29, remove 'password' from the displayData array so it reads displayData={['name', 'permission', 'isActive', 'id']}. This removes the on-screen column AND, because UserList passes no onExcel prop, prevents exportClientTableToExcel (BasicTable.tsx:236-244, XLSX.utils.table_to_book on the visible DOM) from including passwords in the .xlsx.
2. Backend (defense-in-depth — the field is still in the JSON payload even if hidden in the grid): the generic GET in /Users/saobranaung/Code/Ministry of Commerce/tradenet-admin-report/tradenet-admin-report/Backend/Controllers/BaseAPIController.cs (Get action, lines 27-47) serializes the full User entity, and Backend/Model/User.cs exposes Password ([Required] string, plaintext). Do NOT add a bare [JsonIgnore] to User.Password: AuthController.Login (Backend/Controllers/AuthController.cs:25) binds a User from the request body and JWTManagerService.Authenticate compares x.Password == users.Password (Backend/Service/JWTManagerService.cs:32), so the default [JsonIgnore(Condition=Always)] would stop the login password from deserializing and break auth.
3. Preferred backend fix: stop returning the raw User entity from the list endpoint. Override Get in UserController (Backend/Controllers/UserController.cs) to project to a password-free shape, or change the User screen to a dedicated UsersController endpoint that selects new { u.Id, u.Name, u.Permission } (the frontend grid only uses name, permission, isActive, id). Note: isActive is not a property on API.Model.User (User.cs has only Id, Name, Password, Permission), so the grid already shows N/A for it — keep that behavior or drop the column.
4. Minimal alternative backend fix (if you must keep the shared User model on the response): annotate Password with System.Text.Json so it is write-only — accepted on deserialize (login) but never serialized (responses). Add `using System.Text.Json.Serialization;` to Backend/Model/User.cs (this attribute namespace is already used in Backend/Model/APIResult.cs:7) and decorate Password with [JsonIgnore(Condition = JsonIgnoreCondition.WhenWritingDefault)] ONLY if you also ensure the entity's Password is reset/cleared before returning (WhenWritingDefault hides empty strings, not populated ones); since DB rows have non-empty passwords this will still serialize them — so this alternative is NOT sufficient on its own and the DTO projection in the previous step is the correct fix. Prefer the DTO.
5. Per verifier note (out of original scope but flagged High-aggravating): passwords are stored in plaintext (User.cs Password set to "" in ctor, no hashing; Authenticate does a plaintext equality compare at JWTManagerService.cs:32). Open a follow-up to hash credentials at rest (e.g. ASP.NET Core PasswordHasher<User> or BCrypt) and change Authenticate to verify a hash instead of x.Password == users.Password. This is a separate migration, not part of this finding's quick fix.
6. Per verifier note: BaseAPIController is decorated [AllowAnonymous] at the class level (BaseAPIController.cs:15) while every action has [Authorize] (the action attribute wins, so the list endpoint is gated). Remove the misleading class-level [AllowAnonymous] from BaseAPIController so the auth posture isn't fragile/ambiguous; all CRUD actions already carry [Authorize].

```tsx
// Frontend/src/pages/User/UserList.tsx (line 29) — primary fix
// BEFORE
displayData={['name', 'password', 'permission', 'isActive', 'id']}
// AFTER
displayData={['name', 'permission', 'isActive', 'id']}

// ---------------------------------------------------------------
// Backend defense-in-depth (preferred): password-free projection.
// Backend/Controllers/UserController.cs
using System.Linq;
using System.Threading.Tasks;
using API.DBContext;
using API.Model;
using Microsoft.AspNetCore.Authorization;
using Microsoft.AspNetCore.Mvc;

namespace API.Controllers
{
    [ApiController]
    [Authorize]
    [Route("api/[controller]")]
    public class UserController : BaseAPIController<User>
    {
        private readonly ApplicationDbContext _context;
        public UserController(ApplicationDbContext context) : base(context)
        {
            _context = context;
        }

        // Shadow the generic Get so the User grid never receives Password.
        [HttpGet]
        [Authorize]
        public new async Task<ActionResult<ApiResult<UserListDto>>> Get(
            int pageIndex = 0, int pageSize = 10,
            string? sortColumn = null, string? sortOrder = null,
            string? filterColumn = null, string? filterQuery = null)
        {
            var query = _context.Users
                .Select(u => new UserListDto { Id = u.Id, Name = u.Name, Permission = u.Permission });
            return await ApiResult<UserListDto>.CreateAsync(
                query, pageIndex, pageSize, sortColumn, sortOrder, filterColumn, filterQuery);
        }
    }

    public class UserListDto
    {
        public string Id { get; set; } = "";
        public string Name { get; set; } = "";
        public string Permission { get; set; } = "";
    }
}

// Backend/Controllers/BaseAPIController.cs — remove fragile class-level attr
// BEFORE
[Route("api/[controller]")]
[ApiController]
[AllowAnonymous]
public class BaseAPIController<T> : ControllerBase where T : class
// AFTER
[Route("api/[controller]")]
[ApiController]
public class BaseAPIController<T> : ControllerBase where T : class
```

**Verify:**
- Frontend: run the User list page; confirm the grid shows only Name / Permission / Active / Id and no Password column. Click the Excel button and open the downloaded .xlsx — it must not contain a password column.
- Backend: with a valid JWT call GET /api/User?pageIndex=0&pageSize=10 and assert the JSON 'data' items have no 'password' property (e.g. curl ... | grep -i password returns nothing).
- Regression: confirm login still works — POST /api/Auth/Login with body {"name":"<user>","password":"<pw>"} must still return a token (proves Password still deserializes on the request side after the change).
- Auth gate: call GET /api/User with no Authorization header and confirm 401 (action-level [Authorize] still enforced after removing class-level [AllowAnonymous]).
- Build: dotnet build the Backend solution and run the existing Backend.Tests to ensure the new UserController.Get override and UserListDto compile and don't break report tests.

**Pitfalls / rollback.** Do NOT slap a default [JsonIgnore] on User.Password — AuthController.Login binds Password from the request body and JWTManagerService.Authenticate (line 32) compares it, so a default-condition JsonIgnore breaks login. Use a read DTO/projection instead.; The frontend grid references item.isActive in transformUserData, but API.Model.User has no IsActive property (only Id, Name, Password, Permission) — it already renders N/A. The DTO above intentionally omits it; if you want to keep the column, leave the 'isActive' key in displayData (harmless N/A) but don't fabricate the field on the backend.; Using `public new` to shadow the base Get: ensure the route still resolves to UserController's action and not the base. Confirm with the GET verification above; if ambiguity arises, give the override a distinct name and add [HttpGet] without changing the route, or remove the inherited Get from the User route surface.; This change only stops display/export and response serialization. Plaintext-at-rest passwords remain a separate High-priority issue (verifier note) — schedule the hashing migration separately; it requires a data migration and a change to Authenticate's comparison logic.; Rollback is low-risk: reverting the one-line displayData change and the UserController override restores prior behavior; no DB migration is involved in this fix.

> 🔍 **Verifier note.** Severity is fair at High. Note an aggravating factor beyond the finding's scope: passwords are stored in plaintext in the DB (User.cs constructor/property, no hashing) and returned by the generic API with no DTO/JsonIgnore — so the recommendation should go further than just removing 'password' from displayData. Add [JsonIgnore] or a DTO projection on the User read path, and ideally hash credentials at rest. Also note BaseAPIController is decorated [AllowAnonymous] at the class level though the Get action has [Authorize], so auth does gate the list endpoint (action-level attribute wins), but the mixed decoration is fragile.


<a id="f-no-table-sort-or-search"></a>
#### 🟠 HIGH — Report grid advertises sorting/searching but wires neither — no way to sort or find rows in large reports

**ID:** `no-table-sort-or-search` &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Phase:** P1 &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Category:** Table UX &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Effort:** Medium &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Confidence:** High

**Location(s):**
- `Frontend/src/components/My Components/Table/BasicTable.tsx:21`
- `Frontend/src/components/My Components/Table/BasicTable.tsx:39-40`
- `Frontend/src/components/My Components/Table/BasicTable.tsx:60`
- `Frontend/src/components/My Components/Table/BasicTable.tsx:64-65`
- `Frontend/src/components/My Components/Table/BasicTable.tsx:157-176`
- `Frontend/src/components/My Components/Table/BasicTable.tsx:191-202`
- `Frontend/src/components/My Components/Table/BasicTable.tsx:376-395`
- `Frontend/src/Report/Page/GenericReportPage.tsx:800-801`

**Problem.** BasicTable defines a full sort/search API (SortOrder type, column.sortable/searchable/filterKey/sortKey, extraFilters, initialSortColumn/initialSortOrder props) and computes searchableColumns/firstDataColumn, but none of it reaches the rendered UI. The query object hardcodes sortColumn:'' and sortOrder:'' (lines 195-196) and filterQuery is destructured value-only with no setter (`const [filterQuery] = useState('')`, line 176), so it can never change. The <thead> renders plain non-interactive <th> headers (376-395) with no click handlers, sort arrows, or search box. extraFilters, sortable, initialSortColumn and initialSortOrder are never referenced in the component body. GenericReportPage passes initialSortColumn/initialSortOrder (800-801) that are silently ignored.

**Impact.** Across ~134 reports — many returning hundreds to thousands of rows (page sizes go up to 1000, BasicTable.tsx:497) — an operator cannot click a column to sort, cannot type to find a company/licence, and cannot reorder by date or amount. They must page through manually or re-export to Excel and search there. This is a major productivity loss for daily ministry reporting work and makes wide financial reports nearly unusable in-app.

**Recommendation.** Either wire the existing API (make <th> clickable to toggle sortColumn/sortOrder and feed them into the query; add a per-column search input that sets filterColumn/filterQuery via a real setState), or replace the hand-rolled <table> with AntD <Table> (as ExportsDrive.tsx already does) which gives sorting, column filters, and sticky headers for free. If sorting/search will genuinely never be supported, delete the dead props so they don't mislead callers.

**🛠 How to Fix _(est. 4-6 hours)_**

Wire the already-declared sort/search API inside BasicTable.tsx (backend already accepts the params); make headers clickable to sort and add a per-column search box driven by real setState.

1. Open Frontend/src/components/My Components/Table/BasicTable.tsx. The component already imports SortOrder, declares column.sortKey/sortable/searchable/filterKey, and threads sortColumn/sortOrder/filterColumn/filterQuery into the query object and buildLegacyUrl — only the rendered UI and state are missing. Destructure the two currently-ignored props in the function signature (after initialPageSize = 10 around L135): add `initialSortColumn = '',` and `initialSortOrder = 'desc',`.
2. Replace the value-only filterQuery state at L176 (`const [filterQuery] = useState('');`) with a real setter: `const [filterQuery, setFilterQuery] = useState('');`. Add two new state hooks right after it: `const [sortColumn, setSortColumn] = useState(initialSortColumn);` and `const [sortOrder, setSortOrder] = useState<SortOrder>(initialSortOrder);`.
3. In the `query` useMemo (L191-202), stop hardcoding sort. Change `sortColumn: '',` -> `sortColumn,` and `sortOrder: '',` -> `sortOrder,`, and extend the dependency array from `[filterColumn, filterQuery, pageIndex, pageSize]` to `[filterColumn, filterQuery, pageIndex, pageSize, sortColumn, sortOrder]`.
4. Add a sort toggle handler above the return (e.g. after renderCell, ~L281). It resolves the backend column name from sortKey, then dataIndex, then key: `const handleSort = (column: BasicTableColumn<T>) => { if (column.sortable === false) return; const colKey = (column.sortKey ?? column.dataIndex ?? column.key).toString(); setPageIndex(0); if (sortColumn === colKey) { setSortOrder((prev) => (prev === 'asc' ? 'desc' : 'asc')); } else { setSortColumn(colKey); setSortOrder('asc'); } };`. Reset pageIndex so sorting starts at page 1.
5. Make the column-header <th> interactive in the header row (L378-391). Compute `const colKey = (column.sortKey ?? column.dataIndex ?? column.key).toString(); const isSorted = sortColumn === colKey; const sortable = column.sortable !== false;` inside the map. Add `onClick={() => handleSort(column)}`, `style={{ width: column.width, cursor: sortable ? 'pointer' : 'default', userSelect: 'none' }}`, and render a sort indicator after the title: `{sortable && (isSorted ? (sortOrder === 'asc' ? ' ▲' : ' ▼') : ' ⇅')}`. Guard the click with the sortable flag so non-sortable columns stay inert.
6. Add a per-column search box. Use the existing `searchable` prop (PropsType L59) plus per-column `column.searchable`/`filterKey`. Below the column-header <tr> (before </thead> at L395) add a filter row that renders only when `searchable` is true and the column is searchable: `<input value={filterColumn === (column.filterKey ?? column.dataIndex ?? column.key).toString() ? filterQuery : ''} onChange={(e) => { setPageIndex(0); setFilterColumn((column.filterKey ?? column.dataIndex ?? column.key).toString()); setFilterQuery(e.target.value); }} placeholder="Search" />`. (Single active filterColumn matches the backend, which takes one filterColumn/filterQuery pair.) Debounce optional — keep simple for first pass.
7. In GenericReportPage.tsx the props are already forwarded at L800-801; no change needed there except confirming the value flows. Since config.initialSortColumn holds the backend SQL column name (e.g. 'VoucherDate', 'Date' in reportConfigs.ts) it is fed directly as sortColumn, which is what the stored proc's @SortColumn expects (Backend/StoredProcedureToLinq/sp_VoucherReport.cs L160) — so the seed value is correct as-is.
8. To make a column's clickable header sort by the right backend column when it differs from camelCase dataIndex, add an optional `sortKey?: string;` to ReportColumnConfig in Frontend/src/Report/config/reportTypes.ts (after fallbackDataIndexes, L27). BasicTableColumn already has sortKey (BasicTable.tsx L36) and toTableColumn spreads the config (GenericReportPage.tsx L326-340 `{ ...column }`), so a sortKey set on a config column will propagate automatically. Populate sortKey only where dataIndex !== the SQL column name; otherwise the dataIndex fallback in handleSort is sufficient.
9. Add minimal styling in Frontend/src/components/My Components/Table/style.css near the existing `.table-container thead th` rule (L132): a `.table-container thead th.sortable { cursor: pointer; }` helper and a `.table-container thead .column-filter-row input { width: 100%; }` rule so the search inputs fit the column width.

```tsx
// Frontend/src/components/My Components/Table/BasicTable.tsx

// --- props signature (~L135) ---
  initialPageSize = 10,
+  initialSortColumn = '',
+  initialSortOrder = 'desc',
   emptyText = 'No data',

// --- state (~L176) ---
-  const [filterQuery] = useState('');
+  const [filterQuery, setFilterQuery] = useState('');
+  const [sortColumn, setSortColumn] = useState(initialSortColumn);
+  const [sortOrder, setSortOrder] = useState<SortOrder>(initialSortOrder);

// --- query useMemo (L191-202) ---
   const query = useMemo<BasicTableQuery>(
     () => ({
       pageIndex: pageIndex < 0 ? 0 : pageIndex,
       pageSize,
-      sortColumn: '',
-      sortOrder: '',
+      sortColumn,
+      sortOrder,
       filterColumn,
       filterQuery,
       includeTotalCount: true,
     }),
-    [filterColumn, filterQuery, pageIndex, pageSize]
+    [filterColumn, filterQuery, pageIndex, pageSize, sortColumn, sortOrder]
   );

// --- new handler (after renderCell, ~L281) ---
+  const handleSort = (column: BasicTableColumn<T>) => {
+    if (column.sortable === false) return;
+    const colKey = (column.sortKey ?? column.dataIndex ?? column.key).toString();
+    setPageIndex(0);
+    if (sortColumn === colKey) {
+      setSortOrder((prev) => (prev === 'asc' ? 'desc' : 'asc'));
+    } else {
+      setSortColumn(colKey);
+      setSortOrder('asc');
+    }
+  };

// --- header row (replace L378-391 map) ---
   {normalizedColumns.map((column) => {
     const key = column.key.toString();
-    return (
-      <th key={key} style={{ width: column.width }}>
-        {column.title ?? NameConvert(key)}
-      </th>
-    );
+    const colKey = (column.sortKey ?? column.dataIndex ?? column.key).toString();
+    const sortable = column.sortable !== false;
+    const isSorted = sortColumn === colKey;
+    return (
+      <th
+        key={key}
+        className={sortable ? 'sortable' : undefined}
+        onClick={() => handleSort(column)}
+        style={{ width: column.width, cursor: sortable ? 'pointer' : 'default', userSelect: 'none' }}
+      >
+        {column.title ?? NameConvert(key)}
+        {sortable && (isSorted ? (sortOrder === 'asc' ? ' ▲' : ' ▼') : ' ⇅')}
+      </th>
+    );
   })}

// --- optional per-column search row, inserted before </thead> (~L395) ---
+  {searchable && (
+    <tr className="column-filter-row">
+      {showRowNumber && <th />}
+      {normalizedColumns.map((column) => {
+        const fKey = (column.filterKey ?? column.dataIndex ?? column.key).toString();
+        const colSearchable = column.searchable !== false && fKey.toLowerCase() !== 'id';
+        return (
+          <th key={column.key.toString()}>
+            {colSearchable && (
+              <input
+                value={filterColumn === fKey ? filterQuery : ''}
+                placeholder="Search"
+                onChange={(e) => {
+                  setPageIndex(0);
+                  setFilterColumn(fKey);
+                  setFilterQuery(e.target.value);
+                }}
+              />
+            )}
+          </th>
+        );
+      })}
+      {shouldShowActions && <th />}
+    </tr>
+  )}

// Frontend/src/Report/config/reportTypes.ts (ReportColumnConfig, after L27)
   fallbackDataIndexes?: string[];
+  /** Backend SQL column name for sorting when it differs from dataIndex (e.g. 'VoucherDate'). */
+  sortKey?: string;
```

**Verify:**
- cd Frontend && npm run build (or tsc --noEmit) — confirm no type errors after destructuring initialSortColumn/initialSortOrder and adding sortKey to ReportColumnConfig.
- Run the app (npm run dev), open a high-volume report (e.g. Voucher report, config initialSortColumn 'VoucherDate'). Confirm a sort arrow renders on every header and the active column shows the seeded descending arrow on first load.
- Open browser DevTools Network tab, click the 'Date'/'Amount' column header, and confirm the POST to config.apiRoute body now contains a non-empty sortColumn plus sortOrder 'ASC'/'DESC' (GenericReportPage.buildRequest uppercases sortOrder, L309); click again and confirm sortOrder flips and the visible row order reverses.
- Type a value into a column search box (with searchable enabled) and confirm the request body carries filterColumn = that column and filterQuery = the typed text, pageIndex resets to 0, and the grid narrows to matching rows.
- Confirm pagination still works after sorting/searching (total count updates, page 1 is reselected).

**Pitfalls / rollback.** initialSortColumn in reportConfigs.ts holds BACKEND SQL column names (PascalCase like 'VoucherDate', 'Date', 'SakhanId'), NOT the camelCase dataIndex — do not lowercase or remap it when seeding sortColumn; it must be passed through verbatim to match the stored proc @SortColumn (sp_VoucherReport.cs L160). For header-click sorting where the displayed column's dataIndex differs from the SQL column, you MUST set a per-column sortKey in the config, otherwise the proc will receive an unknown column name; the proc decides what an unknown/empty @SortColumn does, so verify a few reports rather than assuming all columns are sortable — consider only marking columns sortable where the backend supports them, or default sortable:false and opt columns in. The backend takes a single filterColumn/filterQuery pair, so the per-column search must be single-active (selecting a new column replaces the prior filter) — do not try to send multiple column filters. If unsure whether every report's proc validates @SortColumn safely, gate the feature by leaving search off (searchable defaults undefined/false) and rolling sort out report-by-report. Rollback is trivial: revert BasicTable.tsx and reportTypes.ts; no data migration involved.

> 🔍 **Verifier note.** Severity High is defensible but borderline-Medium. Mitigations exist that the finding understates: every report has a substantial pre-load filter form (date ranges, business-type dropdowns, etc.) that narrows results before fetch, plus per-report Excel export for offline sort/search, plus pagination with total counts. This is a productivity/UX gap, not a correctness or data-integrity bug. Given the breadth (all ~135 reports), the misleading dead props (initialSortColumn set in 128 configs but ignored), and real pain on wide multi-thousand-row financial reports, High is acceptable; I would not object to Medium. The recommendation to wire the existing API or migrate to AntD Table is sound and the ExportsDrive precedent is real. One nuance: GenericReportPage forwards query.sortColumn/filterQuery to the backend (L308-311), so the backend likely supports these params (buildLegacyUrl L108-116 also forwards them) — meaning only the front-end UI wiring is missing, making the fix lower-risk than the finding implies.


<a id="f-huge-unsearchable-report-nav"></a>
#### 🟡 MEDIUM — 134-report sidebar has no search/filter, and clicks log to console

**ID:** `huge-unsearchable-report-nav` &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Phase:** P2 &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Category:** Navigation &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Effort:** Medium &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Confidence:** High

**Location(s):**
- `Frontend/src/Report/reportNavItems.tsx:223-242`
- `Frontend/src/layouts/app/SideNav.tsx:31-36`
- `Frontend/src/layouts/app/SideNav.tsx:88-97`

**Problem.** reportNavItems builds a single inline AntD Menu of ~134 reports grouped into 23 expandable categories (reportNavItems.tsx:223-242). The SideNav (88-97) renders this menu with no search box or filter to locate a report by name. Every menu click also fires console.log('click ', e) (SideNav.tsx:32) and the menu onClick handler in App layout uses console.log for collapse debugging too.

**Impact.** Finding a specific report among 134 means knowing which of 23 categories it lives in and scrolling/expanding — slow and error-prone for daily use, especially given many near-identical names (e.g. Import vs Border Import Licence variants). Stray console logging clutters the console in production.

**Recommendation.** Add a search/filter input above the report menu that filters items by title (the config already exposes config.title), or provide a searchable command-palette to jump to a report. Remove the debug console.log calls.

**🛠 How to Fix _(est. 1.5 hours)_**

Add a title-filter search box above the AntD inline Menu in SideNav.tsx that filters/searches the 134 report items (using config.title, already in the nav labels), and remove the stray debug console.log calls in SideNav.tsx and layouts/app/App.tsx. No backend change.

1. In Frontend/src/Report/reportNavItems.tsx, export a reusable item-builder so SideNav can rebuild a filtered tree. Add an exported function `buildReportNavItems(filter?: string)` that returns the same structure currently produced inline at lines 223-242, but filters report items by `config.title` (case-insensitive `includes`). Keep `reportNavItems` exported as `buildReportNavItems()` for callers that want the full tree. Reuse the existing `createReportItem`, `reportCategoryDefinitions`, `getReportCategoryKey`, and `reportConfigList`.
2. Inside `buildReportNavItems`, when a non-empty filter is supplied: (a) keep the top-level 'Exports' item only if 'exports' matches the filter; (b) for each category, filter its `reportConfigList` children by `config.title.toLowerCase().includes(q)` and drop categories whose children become empty (the existing `.filter((category) => category.children.length > 0)` already does this); (c) filter the trailing uncategorized configs the same way.
3. In Frontend/src/layouts/app/SideNav.tsx, import `useMemo` from 'react' and `Input` from 'antd' (and optionally `SearchOutlined` from '@ant-design/icons'). Replace the static `const items: MenuProps['items'] = reportNavItems;` (line 17) with a stateful query: add `const [query, setQuery] = useState('');` inside the component and compute `const items = useMemo(() => buildReportNavItems(query), [query]);`. Update the import on line 11 from `reportNavItems` to `buildReportNavItems`.
4. When `query` is non-empty, force the matching categories open so results are visible without manual expansion: compute the open keys from the filtered tree. e.g. `const filteredOpenKeys = useMemo(() => query ? items.filter(i => 'children' in (i ?? {})).map(i => i!.key as string) : openKeys, [query, items, openKeys]);` and pass `openKeys={filteredOpenKeys}` to the `<Menu>` (line 93). Keep `onOpenChange` for the non-search case.
5. Render the search box directly above the `<Menu>` (inside the `<ConfigProvider>` at lines 76-98, before `<Menu>` at line 88): `<Input allowClear prefix={<SearchOutlined />} placeholder="Search reports" value={query} onChange={(e) => setQuery(e.target.value)} style={{ margin: '0.5rem 1rem', width: 'calc(100% - 2rem)' }} />`.
6. Remove the debug log at SideNav.tsx line 32: delete `console.log('click ', e);` from the `onClick` handler, leaving the `if (isMobile) { setCollapse(true); }` behavior intact.
7. In Frontend/src/layouts/app/App.tsx, remove the two collapse-debug logs inside the `useEffect(..., [collapsed])`: delete `console.log(collapsed);` at line 115 and line 122. Leave the surrounding `if (!collapsed) { ... } else { ... }` DOM logic untouched. (Optionally keep the 401 log at line 139, or downgrade it; it is not part of this finding.)
8. Do not edit `Frontend/src/layouts/app/SideNav copy.tsx` — it is an unused backup copy and not imported anywhere; leave it or delete it separately.

```tsx
// reportNavItems.tsx — replace the inline export (lines 223-242)
export const buildReportNavItems = (
  filter?: string
): Required<MenuProps>['items'] => {
  const q = (filter ?? '').trim().toLowerCase();
  const match = (title: string) => !q || title.toLowerCase().includes(q);
  return [
    ...(match('Exports')
      ? [{
          key: 'Exports',
          icon: <CloudDownloadOutlined />,
          label: <Link to="/Report/Exports">Exports</Link>,
        }]
      : []),
    ...reportCategoryDefinitions
      .map((category) => ({
        key: category.key,
        label: category.title,
        icon: category.icon,
        children: reportConfigList
          .filter(
            (config) =>
              category.matches(config.controllerName) && match(config.title)
          )
          .map(createReportItem),
      }))
      .filter((category) => category.children.length > 0),
    ...reportConfigList
      .filter(
        (config) =>
          !getReportCategoryKey(config.controllerName) && match(config.title)
      )
      .map(createReportItem),
  ];
};

// keep the old export working for any other importers
export const reportNavItems = buildReportNavItems();

// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
// SideNav.tsx
// import { useEffect, useMemo, useRef, useState } from 'react';
// import { ConfigProvider, Input, Layout, Menu, MenuProps, SiderProps } from 'antd';
// import { SearchOutlined } from '@ant-design/icons';
// import { buildReportNavItems, ... } from '../../Report/reportNavItems.tsx';

// remove:  const items: MenuProps['items'] = reportNavItems;
// inside component:
const [query, setQuery] = useState('');
const items = useMemo(() => buildReportNavItems(query), [query]);
const filteredOpenKeys = useMemo(
  () =>
    query
      ? (items ?? [])
          .filter((i) => i && 'children' in i)
          .map((i) => i!.key as string)
      : openKeys,
  [query, items, openKeys]
);

const onClick: MenuProps['onClick'] = () => {   // was (e) => { console.log('click ', e); ... }
  if (isMobile) {
    setCollapse(true);
  }
};

// JSX, just before <Menu ...>:
<Input
  allowClear
  prefix={<SearchOutlined />}
  placeholder="Search reports"
  value={query}
  onChange={(e) => setQuery(e.target.value)}
  style={{ margin: '0.5rem 1rem', width: 'calc(100% - 2rem)' }}
/>
<Menu
  className="app-side-nav-menu"
  mode="inline"
  items={items}
  onClick={onClick}
  openKeys={filteredOpenKeys}   // was openKeys={openKeys}
  onOpenChange={onOpenChange}
  selectedKeys={[current]}
  style={{ border: 'none' }}
/>

// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
// layouts/app/App.tsx — inside useEffect(..., [collapsed])
if (!collapsed) {
  // console.log(collapsed);   <-- delete
  if (temp) { /* ... */ }
} else {
  // console.log(collapsed);   <-- delete
  if (temp) {
    temp.style.display = 'block';
    footer.style.display = 'block';
  }
}
```

**Verify:**
- Run the Vite dev server (npm run dev in Frontend/) and open the report sidebar. Type 'licence' in the new search box: the menu should collapse to only matching report items with their parent categories auto-expanded; clear it (allowClear X) to restore all 134 reports.
- Search a distinctive substring like 'Member' and confirm only the Member category and matching reports show; confirm clicking a filtered result still navigates to /Report/<controllerName>.
- Open browser DevTools console, click several menu items and toggle the sidebar collapse: confirm no 'click {...}' or bare boolean (collapsed) logs appear anymore.
- Run `npm run build` (or tsc) in Frontend/ to confirm no TypeScript errors from the new buildReportNavItems signature and the Input/useMemo imports.
- grep to confirm cleanup: `grep -rn "console.log" Frontend/src/layouts/app/SideNav.tsx Frontend/src/layouts/app/App.tsx` returns no menu-click or collapse-debug lines (the only remaining hit may be the intentional 401 logout log).

**Pitfalls / rollback.** Keep `reportNavItems` exported (aliased to buildReportNavItems()) so you don't break any other importers — grep `reportNavItems` before removing it. The `openKeys`/`onOpenChange` logic in SideNav uses controlled state; when forcing categories open during search via filteredOpenKeys, do not also call setOpenKeys in a render path or you'll get a render loop — derive it with useMemo only. The category.matches predicates run on controllerName (unchanged); only the title filter is new, so do not accidentally filter on controllerName. App.tsx here is Frontend/src/layouts/app/App.tsx, NOT Frontend/src/App.tsx (the audit's App.tsx:115/122 line numbers map to the layouts copy). Leave the 401 console.log (App.tsx:139) and the unused 'SideNav copy.tsx' out of scope unless asked. Rollback is trivial: revert the three files; no data/migration involved.

> 🔍 **Verifier note.** Minor inaccuracy in the finding's wording: the App-layout console.log calls (App.tsx:115/122) are inside a useEffect on [collapsed], not a menu onClick handler as the problem text implies ("the menu onClick handler in App layout"). The substance (stray collapse-debug console.log in the App layout) is still correct. The genuine menu onClick console.log is in SideNav.tsx:32, which is correctly cited. Locations are all accurate. The config does expose config.title (used at reportNavItems.tsx:212 for the label), so the recommended title-based filter is feasible.


<a id="f-reset-and-logout-no-confirmation"></a>
#### 🔵 LOW — Filter Reset wipes applied filters and Logout signs out with no confirmation

**ID:** `reset-and-logout-no-confirmation` &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Phase:** P3 &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Category:** Feedback / destructive actions &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Effort:** Trivial &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Confidence:** High

**Location(s):**
- `Frontend/src/Report/Page/GenericReportPage.tsx:717-722`
- `Frontend/src/Report/Page/GenericReportPage.tsx:777-779`
- `Frontend/src/layouts/app/App.tsx:70-83`

**Problem.** resetFilters (717-722) immediately clears the form back to defaults, clears applied filters, and resets the grid to the idle state with no confirmation and no toast — a user who spent time setting many filters on a wide report can lose them in one click. The header Logout menu item (App.tsx:70-83) logs the user out instantly (only a 'signing you out' loading toast) with no confirm step; ExportsDrive by contrast correctly Popconfirms its delete action.

**Impact.** Accidental Reset forces re-entering complex filter sets; accidental Logout interrupts work and forces re-authentication. Inconsistent with the app's own Popconfirm pattern used for export deletion.

**Recommendation.** Add a confirm (Popconfirm/Modal.confirm) to Logout, and consider confirming Reset only when filters have been applied (or show an undo toast). Align destructive-action affordances across the app.

**🛠 How to Fix _(est. 30 min)_**

Reuse the app's established Ant Design `Popconfirm` pattern (already used in `ExportsDrive.tsx`) for both destructive actions: gate the header Logout menu item behind a confirm, and wrap the report Reset button in a confirm that only triggers when filters have actually been applied (so the common case of an unfiltered/idle page stays one click). Add an undo-style success toast on Reset for extra safety.

1. Reset button — open Frontend/src/Report/Page/GenericReportPage.tsx. The Reset handler `resetFilters` is at lines 717-722 and the button is rendered at lines 777-779 inside the `<Space wrap>` next to the primary Filter submit button. The `hasAppliedFilters` boolean state is already in scope (used at lines 713/720/727).
2. Add `Popconfirm` to the existing antd import block at the top of GenericReportPage.tsx (currently imports Button, Card, Col, DatePicker, Form, Input, InputNumber, Row, Select, Space, message on lines 2-14). Insert `Popconfirm,` into that destructured import list.
3. In `resetFilters` (lines 717-722), after the existing `setRefreshKey((current) => current + 1);` line, add `message.info('Filters reset.');` so the user gets feedback that the action happened (the `message` API is already imported).
4. Replace the bare Reset `<Button onClick={resetFilters} ...>` (lines 777-779) with a conditional render: when `hasAppliedFilters` is true, wrap the button in `<Popconfirm>` so applied filters cannot be wiped by a stray click; when false (idle / no applied filters), keep the plain one-click button calling `resetFilters` directly. Move the `onClick={resetFilters}` off the button when wrapped (Popconfirm fires the action via its `onConfirm`).
5. Logout confirmation — open Frontend/src/layouts/app/App.tsx. The logout menu item is the `items` array entry at lines 70-83 (key `user-logout-link`). Because this is a Dropdown menu item config (not JSX), use `Modal.confirm` rather than a Popconfirm wrapper — Ant Design menu items render as plain rows and cannot host a Popconfirm child cleanly.
6. In App.tsx add `Modal` to the existing antd import (lines 1-12, which currently import Avatar, Button, Dropdown, Flex, FloatButton, Layout, MenuProps, message, theme, Tooltip). Insert `Modal,` into that list.
7. Change the logout menu item's `onClick` (lines 75-82) so that instead of immediately calling `auth?.logout()` and navigating, it opens `Modal.confirm({ title: 'Log out?', content: 'You will need to sign in again to continue.', okText: 'Log out', okType: 'danger', cancelText: 'Cancel', onOk: () => { message.open({ type: 'loading', content: 'signing you out' }); auth?.logout(); navigate(PATH_AUTH.signin, { replace: true }); } })`. Keep the existing loading toast inside `onOk` so behaviour after confirmation is unchanged.
8. Run the Frontend lint/build to confirm no unused imports and types are valid (see verification).

```tsx
// Frontend/src/Report/Page/GenericReportPage.tsx
// 1) import (lines 2-14): add Popconfirm
import {
  Button,
  Card,
  Col,
  DatePicker,
  Form,
  Input,
  InputNumber,
  Popconfirm,   // <-- add
  Row,
  Select,
  Space,
  message,
} from 'antd';

// 2) resetFilters (lines 717-722): add feedback toast
  const resetFilters = () => {
    form.setFieldsValue(initialFormValues);
    setFilters(normalizeReportFilters(initialFormValues));
    setHasAppliedFilters(false);
    setRefreshKey((current) => current + 1);
    message.info('Filters reset.');   // <-- add
  };

// 3) Reset button (lines 777-779): confirm only when filters applied
//    BEFORE:
//      <Button onClick={resetFilters} icon={<ReloadOutlined />}>
//        Reset
//      </Button>
//    AFTER:
{hasAppliedFilters ? (
  <Popconfirm
    title="Reset all filters?"
    description="This clears the applied filters and the loaded report."
    onConfirm={resetFilters}
    okText="Reset"
    cancelText="Cancel"
  >
    <Button icon={<ReloadOutlined />}>Reset</Button>
  </Popconfirm>
) : (
  <Button onClick={resetFilters} icon={<ReloadOutlined />}>
    Reset
  </Button>
)}

// ---------------------------------------------------------------
// Frontend/src/layouts/app/App.tsx
// 4) import (lines 1-12): add Modal
import {
  Avatar,
  Button,
  Dropdown,
  Flex,
  FloatButton,
  Layout,
  MenuProps,
  message,
  Modal,        // <-- add
  theme,
  Tooltip,
} from 'antd';

// 5) logout menu item onClick (lines 75-82): confirm before signing out
    {
      key: 'user-logout-link',
      label: 'logout',
      icon: <LogoutOutlined />,
      danger: true,
      onClick: () => {
        Modal.confirm({
          title: 'Log out?',
          content: 'You will need to sign in again to continue.',
          okText: 'Log out',
          okType: 'danger',
          cancelText: 'Cancel',
          onOk: () => {
            message.open({ type: 'loading', content: 'signing you out' });
            auth?.logout();
            navigate(PATH_AUTH.signin, { replace: true });
          },
        });
      },
    },
```

**Verify:**
- Frontend lint/build: from Frontend/ run `npm run lint` (or `npx tsc --noEmit`) and `npm run build` — must pass with no unused-import or type errors from the new Popconfirm/Modal symbols.
- Reset (applied state): open any report (e.g. an Import Licence report), set filters and click Filter so the grid loads (hasAppliedFilters=true), then click Reset — a Popconfirm pops with Reset/Cancel; Cancel keeps filters, Reset clears them and shows the 'Filters reset.' info toast.
- Reset (idle state): on a freshly opened report with no filters applied, click Reset — it should clear immediately with no popconfirm (one-click), confirming the gating logic.
- Logout: click the avatar dropdown -> logout; a Modal.confirm appears ('Log out?'). Cancel keeps the session; confirming shows the 'signing you out' loading toast, calls auth.logout(), and redirects to PATH_AUTH.signin.
- Regression: confirm the 401 interceptor logout path in App.tsx (lines 132-157) is untouched and still logs out automatically on expired session (it must NOT prompt).

**Pitfalls / rollback.** Do not wrap the antd Dropdown/Menu logout item in a Popconfirm component — menu items are config objects, not JSX children, so Popconfirm won't render correctly there; Modal.confirm is the right tool for the menu-item case. Popconfirm is only appropriate for the Reset button because it is real JSX.; Keep the loading toast and navigate call INSIDE Modal.confirm's onOk; if left outside they will fire before the user confirms, defeating the purpose.; Only gate Reset with Popconfirm when hasAppliedFilters is true — wrapping it unconditionally would add friction to the common idle case and regress UX.; Do NOT add a confirm to the 401 response-interceptor logout (App.tsx lines 132-157); that path is automatic/session-expiry and must remain prompt-free.; Rollback is trivial: revert the two files; no state, API, or data-migration changes are involved.

> 🔍 **Verifier note.** Minor nuance: Logout is partially self-signaling (menu label 'logout' + danger styling), so the strongest part of the finding is the Reset case and the cross-app inconsistency with the established Popconfirm pattern. Severity Low is appropriate; recommendation (add Popconfirm/Modal.confirm to logout, optionally confirm/undo on Reset only when filters applied) is reasonable and consistent with the existing ExportsDrive pattern.


<a id="f-breadcrumb-collapses-current-page"></a>
#### 🔵 LOW — Page breadcrumb shows '...' instead of the report name, using the raw URL segment

**ID:** `breadcrumb-collapses-current-page` &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Phase:** P3 &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Category:** Navigation &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Effort:** Small &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Confidence:** High

**Location(s):**
- `Frontend/src/components/PageHeader/PageHeader.tsx:16, Frontend/src/components/PageHeader/PageHeader.tsx:31-43`
- `Frontend/src/components/PageHeader/PageHeader.tsx:16`
- `Frontend/src/components/PageHeader/PageHeader.tsx:31-52`

**Problem.** PageHeader builds breadcrumbs from location.pathname.split('/'), i.e. the controllerName URL segment (e.g. 'ImportLicenceTotalValueLicencesReport'), not the human title. For the last crumb it renders '...' whenever the text length > 10 and only puts the real text in a hover Tooltip (lines 36-43). Because virtually every report controllerName exceeds 10 characters, the breadcrumb for nearly every page reads 'Home / Report / ...'. Line 16 also has a dead/buggy initializer (`[...'Home', ...]` spreads the string into individual letters) that is immediately overwritten by the effect.

**Impact.** Operators lose the breadcrumb as an orientation aid — every report looks identical ('...') in the breadcrumb, so users cannot tell at a glance which report they are on, and middle crumbs show machine names like 'Report' rather than readable categories. Confusing for a system with 134 similarly named reports.

**Recommendation.** Show the actual report title for the current crumb (the page already has config.title) and never truncate it to '...'; if space is a concern use CSS ellipsis with the full text still in the DOM/title attribute. Map URL segments to human labels for intermediate crumbs. Remove the dead line-16 initializer.

**🛠 How to Fix _(est. 30 min)_**

Rewrite the breadcrumb logic in PageHeader.tsx so the LAST crumb renders the human-readable `title` prop (which GenericReportPage already passes as `config.title`) instead of the raw URL segment, never collapses to '...', and uses CSS ellipsis (full text kept in DOM + `title` attribute) for overflow. Map intermediate URL segments to friendly labels and delete the dead line-16 initializer.

1. Open Frontend/src/components/PageHeader/PageHeader.tsx. The component already receives the readable name via its `title: string` prop (GenericReportPage.tsx line 736 calls `<PageHeader title={config.title} />`); the bug is that the breadcrumb ignores `title` and is built from `location.pathname.split('/')`.
2. Delete the dead initializer on line 16 (`const temp = [...'Home', location.pathname.toString().split('/')];`) and the `temp` reference in the `useState` on line 17. Replace the `useState`/`useEffect` pair with a single derivation of the path segments, e.g. `const segments = location.pathname.split('/').filter(Boolean);` (drops the empty leading segment so the Home crumb is handled explicitly). You can remove the `useState`/`useEffect` imports if no longer used elsewhere in the file.
3. Add a small label map above the component for intermediate segments so machine names become readable, e.g. `const SEGMENT_LABELS: Record<string, string> = { Report: 'Reports', User: 'Users', dashboards: 'Dashboards' };` and a helper `const labelFor = (seg: string) => SEGMENT_LABELS[seg] ?? seg;`.
4. Rebuild the `item` array: index 0 stays the Home `<Link to="/"><HomeOutlined/></Link>` crumb. For the LAST crumb, render the human `title` prop (not the URL segment) inside a `<Typography.Text type="secondary">` that has the CSS ellipsis class and a native `title={title}` attribute so the full name stays in the DOM and on hover. For intermediate crumbs, render `labelFor(segment)`.
5. Remove the `'...'` truncation and the hover-only `Tooltip`: replace `{x.length > 10 ? '...' : x}` with the actual text and let CSS handle overflow. The antd `Tooltip` import can be dropped if no longer referenced.
6. In Frontend/src/components/PageHeader/styles.css add an ellipsis rule for the last crumb, e.g. `.page-header-breadcrumbs .ant-breadcrumb li:last-child .ant-breadcrumb-link { display: inline-block; max-width: 360px; overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; white-space: nowrap; vertical-align: bottom; }` so long titles clip visually but remain fully present in the DOM and the `title` tooltip.
7. Verify the other callers (ListOfDirectors.tsx, CompanyProfile.tsx, MemberRegistrationReport.tsx, ExportsDrive.tsx, UserList.tsx, etc.) still pass a meaningful `title` prop; they already do (e.g. GenericReportPage passes `config.title`). No call-site changes are required since the `Props` contract is unchanged.

```tsx
// PageHeader.tsx — BEFORE (lines 14–52, abridged)
export const PageHeader = ({ breadcrumbs, title, ...others }: Props) => {
  const location = useLocation();
  const temp = [...'Home', location.pathname.toString().split('/')];   // dead/buggy
  const [link, setLink] = useState(temp);
  useEffect(() => { setLink([...location.pathname.split('/')]); }, [location]);
  const item = link.map((x, i) => {
    if (i === 0) { /* Home link */ }
    else if (i === link.length - 1) {
      return { key: `breadcrumb-${i}`, title: (
        <Tooltip placement="topLeft" title={x.length > 10 ? x : undefined} arrow>
          <Typography.Text type="secondary">{x.length > 10 ? '...' : x}</Typography.Text>
        </Tooltip>) };
    } else {
      return { key: `breadcrumb-${i}`, title: <Typography.Text type="secondary">{x}</Typography.Text> };
    }
  });

// PageHeader.tsx — AFTER
const SEGMENT_LABELS: Record<string, string> = {
  Report: 'Reports',
  User: 'Users',
  dashboards: 'Dashboards',
};
const labelFor = (seg: string) => SEGMENT_LABELS[seg] ?? seg;

export const PageHeader = ({ breadcrumbs, title, ...others }: Props) => {
  const location = useLocation();
  const segments = location.pathname.split('/').filter(Boolean); // e.g. ['Report','BorderExportLicenceTotalValueLicencesReport']

  const item = [
    { key: 'home', title: (<Link to="/"><HomeOutlined /></Link>) },
    ...segments.map((seg, i) => {
      const isLast = i === segments.length - 1;
      // Last crumb: show the human title prop; never '...'; CSS ellipsis + native title attr.
      const text = isLast ? title : labelFor(seg);
      return {
        key: `breadcrumb-${i}`,
        title: (
          <Typography.Text type="secondary" title={text}>
            {text}
          </Typography.Text>
        ),
      };
    }),
  ];
// ...unchanged render below (Breadcrumb items={item}, Typography.Title{title})
```

**Verify:**
- Run `npm run dev` in Frontend and navigate to /Report/BorderExportLicenceTotalValueLicencesReport. The breadcrumb should read 'Home / Reports / Border Export Licence Total Value Licences Report' (the config.title) instead of 'Home / Report / ...'.
- Hover the last crumb (or shrink the window so the CSS clips it): the full title appears via the native `title` tooltip and the complete string is present in the DOM (inspect element).
- Visit several distinct reports and confirm each breadcrumb shows its own title rather than an identical '...'.
- Run `npm run build` (or `tsc --noEmit`) in Frontend to confirm no unused-import/type errors after removing useState/useEffect/Tooltip if they became unused.

**Pitfalls / rollback.** The cited 'line 736' in verifierNotes is the GenericReportPage.tsx call site, not this component; inside PageHeader.tsx the title renders at line 71 — don't edit GenericReportPage.; If you remove the `useState`, `useEffect`, or `Tooltip` imports, make sure they aren't used elsewhere in the file (they currently are not) to avoid a leftover-import lint error.; config.title is capitalized via the existing `.page-header-breadcrumbs .ant-breadcrumb-link { text-transform: capitalize }` rule in styles.css; titles that are already proper-cased multi-word strings are unaffected, but be aware all-caps acronyms would be down-cased — keep the existing capitalize rule scoped to crumbs only, as it already is.; Pure cosmetic change, no data/state migration; rollback is reverting PageHeader.tsx and styles.css.

> 🔍 **Verifier note.** All code claims hold up exactly. The dead line-16 initializer, the '...'-when->10-chars truncation, the hover-only Tooltip, and the raw-URL-segment breadcrumb source are all real. Severity adjusted down to Low because the page's human title is always rendered immediately below the breadcrumb (config.title via PageHeader line 736 -> Typography.Title), so the orientation impact the finding leans on is materially mitigated and the defect is cosmetic/non-functional. The finder's note that middle crumbs show 'Report' rather than a readable category is accurate but minor. Fix is low-effort and worthwhile (use config.title for the current crumb, CSS ellipsis instead of '...', map segments to labels, drop the dead line-16 init).


<a id="f-signin-hardcoded-demo-creds"></a>
#### 🔵 LOW — Sign-in form prefills hardcoded demo credentials and has weak validation/feedback

**ID:** `signin-hardcoded-demo-creds` &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Phase:** P3 &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Category:** Form UX &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Effort:** Small &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Confidence:** High

**Location(s):**
- `Frontend/src/pages/authentication/SignIn.tsx:121-125 (prefilled creds), :133-141 (email field validation), :39,52-57,64 (toast + console.log); related AuthContext.tsx:36-65 (login swallows error, prevents specific failure messaging)`
- `Frontend/src/pages/authentication/SignIn.tsx:121-125`
- `Frontend/src/pages/authentication/SignIn.tsx:133-141`
- `Frontend/src/pages/authentication/SignIn.tsx:38-58`

**Problem.** The login form ships initialValues { email: 'demo@email.com', password: 'demo123', remember: true } (121-125), so the production sign-in page renders prefilled credentials. The email field is a plain <Input> with only a 'required' rule — no email-format validation and no type/autoComplete='username' (133-141). On failure the handler shows a generic 'Login fail' toast (lines 52-57) with no reason, and logs values/errors to console (39, 64).

**Impact.** A government login screen presenting prefilled demo credentials looks unprofessional and untrustworthy and can confuse real users (they may submit demo creds). Lack of email validation lets malformed input hit the API; the vague 'Login fail' gives users no idea whether it was a wrong password, locked account, or server error; console logging of submitted values is a hygiene concern.

**Recommendation.** Remove the prefilled initialValues (leave fields blank). Add an email-type validation rule and autoComplete attributes. Surface the server's actual error reason in the toast (e.g. invalid credentials vs. server unavailable) and drop the console.log of credentials.

**🛠 How to Fix _(est. 30 min)_**

Strip the prefilled demo credentials and credential logging from the sign-in form, add email-format validation plus autoComplete attributes, and make AuthContext.login() return a discriminated result (or thrown error) so SignIn.tsx can show the server's real failure reason (bad credentials vs. server unavailable) instead of a generic "Login fail".

1. In Frontend/src/context/AuthContext.tsx, change the login signature so it conveys the failure reason instead of a bare boolean. Define a result type above the interface: `type LoginResult = { ok: true } | { ok: false; reason: 'invalid' | 'server' | 'network' };` and update the interface member to `login: (name: string, password: string) => Promise<LoginResult>;`.
2. In AuthContext.tsx login(), import `axios` (add `import axios from 'axios';` at the top) so you can use `axios.isAxiosError`. Replace `return false;` (line 47) inside the `!temp.token` guard with `return { ok: false, reason: 'invalid' };`, and replace the success `return true;` (line 60) with `return { ok: true };`.
3. In AuthContext.tsx, rewrite the catch block (lines 61-64): remove `console.log(ex);`, and classify the error: `if (axios.isAxiosError(ex)) { const status = ex.response?.status; if (status === 401 || status === 400) return { ok: false, reason: 'invalid' }; if (status) return { ok: false, reason: 'server' }; return { ok: false, reason: 'network' }; } return { ok: false, reason: 'server' };`.
4. In Frontend/src/pages/authentication/SignIn.tsx, delete `console.log('Success:', values);` (line 39) and `console.log('Failed:', errorInfo);` (line 64) so submitted credentials are never logged.
5. In SignIn.tsx onFinish, update the result handling: `const result = await auth?.login(values['email'], values['password']);` then `if (result?.ok) { ... success ... } else { const reason = result?.reason; const content = reason === 'invalid' ? 'Invalid email or password.' : reason === 'network' ? 'Cannot reach the server. Check your connection.' : 'Login failed. Please try again later.'; message.open({ type: 'error', content }); }`. Keep the existing `setLoading(false)` calls in both branches.
6. In SignIn.tsx remove the demo prefill: delete the `initialValues={{ email: 'demo@email.com', password: 'demo123', remember: true }}` object (lines 121-125). If you want 'Remember me' checked by default without prefilling credentials, replace it with `initialValues={{ remember: true }}`; otherwise drop the prop entirely.
7. In SignIn.tsx, harden the Email Form.Item (lines 133-141): add an email-format rule and give the Input the proper type/autocomplete: change the `rules` to `[{ required: true, message: 'Please input your email' }, { type: 'email', message: 'Please enter a valid email address' }]` and change `<Input />` to `<Input type="email" autoComplete="username" />`.
8. In SignIn.tsx, add `autoComplete="current-password"` to the password field: `<Input.Password autoComplete="current-password" />` (line 151). Note the Form already has `autoComplete="off"`; leave that as-is or remove it so the field-level autoComplete is honored by the browser password manager (optional).

```tsx
// AuthContext.tsx
+ import axios from 'axios';

+ type LoginResult = { ok: true } | { ok: false; reason: 'invalid' | 'server' | 'network' };
  interface AuthContextType {
-   login: (name: string, password: string) => Promise<boolean>;
+   login: (name: string, password: string) => Promise<LoginResult>;
  }

- const login = async (name, password): Promise<boolean> => {
+ const login = async (name, password): Promise<LoginResult> => {
    try {
      const resp = await axiosInstance.post('Auth', PostBody);
      const temp = await resp.data;
-     if (!temp || !temp.token) { return false; }
+     if (!temp || !temp.token) { return { ok: false, reason: 'invalid' }; }
      ... setToken / setUser ...
-     return true;
+     return { ok: true };
-   } catch (ex) {
-     console.log(ex);
-     return false;
+   } catch (ex) {
+     if (axios.isAxiosError(ex)) {
+       const status = ex.response?.status;
+       if (status === 401 || status === 400) return { ok: false, reason: 'invalid' };
+       if (status) return { ok: false, reason: 'server' };
+       return { ok: false, reason: 'network' };
+     }
+     return { ok: false, reason: 'server' };
    }
  };

// SignIn.tsx
  const onFinish = (values: AnyObject) => {
-   console.log('Success:', values);
    setLoading(true);
    const login = async () => {
-     const response = await auth?.login(values['email'], values['password']);
-     if (response) { ... 'Login successful' ... }
-     else { ... message 'Login fail' ... }
+     const result = await auth?.login(values['email'], values['password']);
+     setLoading(false);
+     if (result?.ok) {
+       message.open({ type: 'success', content: 'Login successful' });
+       setTimeout(() => navigate(PATH_DASHBOARD.default), 450);
+     } else {
+       const content =
+         result?.reason === 'invalid' ? 'Invalid email or password.'
+         : result?.reason === 'network' ? 'Cannot reach the server. Check your connection.'
+         : 'Login failed. Please try again later.';
+       message.open({ type: 'error', content });
+     }
    };
    login();
  };

- const onFinishFailed = (errorInfo) => { console.log('Failed:', errorInfo); };
+ const onFinishFailed = () => {};

- initialValues={{ email: 'demo@email.com', password: 'demo123', remember: true }}
+ initialValues={{ remember: true }}

  <Form.Item label="Email" name="email"
    rules={[
      { required: true, message: 'Please input your email' },
+     { type: 'email', message: 'Please enter a valid email address' },
    ]}>
-   <Input />
+   <Input type="email" autoComplete="username" />
  </Form.Item>

- <Input.Password />
+ <Input.Password autoComplete="current-password" />
```

**Verify:**
- Run the frontend dev server (cd Frontend && npm run dev) and open the login page: confirm the Email and Password fields render EMPTY (no demo@email.com / demo123 prefilled).
- Type an invalid email like 'abc' and blur/submit: the field shows 'Please enter a valid email address' and the API is not called.
- Submit valid-format but wrong credentials (backend returns 401/400 or no token): toast reads 'Invalid email or password.' not 'Login fail'.
- Stop the API or point baseUrl at an unreachable host and submit: toast reads 'Cannot reach the server. Check your connection.' (network) or 'Login failed. Please try again later.' (5xx).
- With DevTools Console open, submit the form: no 'Success:' / 'Failed:' log lines and no credential or exception objects appear in the console.
- Run `cd Frontend && npx tsc --noEmit` (or `npm run build`) to confirm the new LoginResult type compiles and no other caller of auth.login() relied on the boolean return.

**Pitfalls / rollback.** "AuthContext.login() return type changes from Promise<boolean> to Promise<LoginResult>, which is a breaking contract change. Grep for other callers before committing: `grep -rn \"\\.login(\" Frontend/src` — currently only SignIn.tsx uses it, but any new caller doing `if (await login(...))` would now always be truthy (object is truthy even on failure), so all callers must switch to checking `.ok`. Do the AuthContext change and the SignIn change together in one commit. The Form already sets autoComplete=\"off\"; field-level autoComplete=\"username\"/\"current-password\" may be ignored by some browsers while that is present — acceptable, but if password-manager integration is desired, remove the form-level autoComplete=\"off\". Rollback is trivial (revert the two files); no data migration or backend change involved."

> 🔍 **Verifier note.** The recommendation to "surface the server's actual error reason" is correct but cannot be done in SignIn.tsx alone — AuthContext.login() must be changed to return the error/status instead of a bare boolean. Severity reduced from Medium to Low because these are UX/professionalism/hygiene issues with no functional or security breakage. Confidence High: code matches the finding verbatim.


<a id="f-client-excel-exports-current-page-only"></a>
#### 🔵 LOW — Client-side Excel export silently exports only the current page, not the full report

**ID:** `client-excel-exports-current-page-only` &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Phase:** P3 &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Category:** Export UX &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Effort:** Small &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Confidence:** High

**Location(s):**
- `Frontend/src/components/My Components/Table/BasicTable.tsx:236-262 (exportClientTableToExcel + handleExcel fallback; button label line 330) and Frontend/src/pages/User/UserList.tsx:27-35 (the only live caller of the fallback). MemberRegistrationReport.tsx should be REMOVED from locations — it passes onExcel and uses the server-side path, not the buggy DOM path.`
- `Frontend/src/components/My Components/Table/BasicTable.tsx:236-262`
- `Frontend/src/pages/User/UserList.tsx:27-35`
- `Frontend/src/Report/Page/MemberRegistrationReport.tsx`

**Problem.** When a caller does not pass onExcel, handleExcel calls exportClientTableToExcel, which runs XLSX.utils.table_to_book on the rendered DOM table (236-244). The DOM only contains the current page's rows (pagination is server-side), so the resulting workbook contains just that page. There is no warning that the export is partial, and the button label is the same 'Excel' as the full server-side queue export used by GenericReportPage. UserList is one live caller that uses this path.

**Impact.** An operator on page 1 of a multi-thousand-row list clicks Excel and gets a 10-row file believing it is the whole dataset — silent data loss that could lead to incorrect government reporting decisions.
