**Recommendation.** Route all exports through the server-side job queue (as GenericReportPage does), or, for the few client-side cases, fetch the full dataset before building the workbook and/or clearly label the button 'Export current page'. Disable the fallback for paginated tables.

**🛠 How to Fix _(est. 30 min)_**

Make the client-side fallback in BasicTable honest instead of silent: when no server-side `onExcel` is supplied, relabel the Excel button to "Export current page" and (when the data spans more than one page) confirm/warn before exporting only the visible rows. This removes the silent-data-loss trap while keeping the single live caller (UserList) working, with no backend change.

1. Open Frontend/src/components/My Components/Table/BasicTable.tsx. The buggy fallback is `exportClientTableToExcel` (lines 236-244), which calls `XLSX.utils.table_to_book(document.getElementById(tableId))` — the DOM only holds the current server-side page. The button label is the hardcoded 'Excel' (line 330) regardless of whether the export is the full server queue export or the partial DOM export.
2. Add an import for `Modal` from 'antd' (the existing import block on lines 5-13 already pulls Alert/Button/Empty/Flex/Pagination/Skeleton/Typography — add `Modal` to that list).
3. Compute whether the current export path is the partial DOM path. Right after the existing `query` useMemo (around line 202), the component already knows `onExcel`, `data.totalCount`, and `pageSize`. Add a derived const: `const isClientExport = !onExcel;` and `const isPartialExport = isClientExport && data.totalCount > pageSize;`.
4. In `exportClientTableToExcel` (lines 236-244), keep the table_to_book/writeFile logic but guard it so a multi-page dataset triggers a confirmation modal first. Wrap the actual export in a helper `runClientExport()` and have `exportClientTableToExcel()` show `Modal.confirm` when `isPartialExport` is true, only calling `runClientExport()` on confirm; when not partial, call `runClientExport()` directly.
5. Relabel the button so the partial path is never disguised as a full export. Replace the hardcoded `Excel` on line 330 with `{isClientExport ? 'Export current page' : 'Excel'}` and add a matching `title` tooltip when `isClientExport` (e.g. `title={isClientExport ? 'Exports only the rows on the current page' : undefined}` on the Button at lines 323-331).
6. Leave UserList.tsx (the only fallback caller) as-is for the export wiring — the relabel and warning now make its behavior honest. Separately, the verifier flagged that UserList renders a 'password' column (line 29 `displayData={['name', 'password', 'permission', 'isActive', 'id']}`); that is out of scope for THIS finding but should be raised as its own ticket — do NOT silently fold it into this change.
7. Do NOT touch MemberRegistrationReport.tsx or ListOfDirectors.tsx — both pass `onExcel={generateExcel}` and use the server-side job queue, so they keep the 'Excel' label and are unaffected (the audit's location list incorrectly included MemberRegistrationReport).

```tsx
// 1) import (line ~5-13): add Modal
import { Alert, Button, Empty, Flex, Modal, Pagination, Skeleton, Typography } from 'antd';

// 2) derived flags, after the `query` useMemo (~line 202)
const isClientExport = !onExcel;
const isPartialExport = isClientExport && data.totalCount > pageSize;

// 3) replace exportClientTableToExcel (lines 236-244)
const runClientExport = () => {
  const table = document.getElementById(tableId);
  if (!table) {
    return;
  }
  const workbook = XLSX.utils.table_to_book(table, { sheet: 'Report' });
  XLSX.writeFile(workbook, excelFileName);
};

const exportClientTableToExcel = () => {
  if (isPartialExport) {
    Modal.confirm({
      title: 'Export current page only',
      content: `This export contains only the ${data.data?.length ?? 0} rows on the current page, not all ${data.totalCount} rows. Continue?`,
      okText: 'Export current page',
      onOk: runClientExport,
    });
    return;
  }
  runClientExport();
};

// 4) button (lines 323-331): relabel + tooltip when client path
<Button
  type="primary"
  icon={<FileExcelOutlined />}
  loading={excelLoading}
  disabled={!excelEnabled}
  title={isClientExport ? 'Exports only the rows on the current page' : undefined}
  onClick={handleExcel}
>
  {isClientExport ? 'Export current page' : 'Excel'}
</Button>
```

**Verify:**
- Run the frontend (cd Frontend && npm run dev) and open the User list page (UserList, route backed by the `User` API). Confirm the export button now reads 'Export current page' instead of 'Excel'.
- Set the page size so totalCount > pageSize (e.g. 10 of many users). Click 'Export current page' and confirm a Modal.confirm appears stating it exports only the current page's rows out of the total; cancelling produces no file, confirming produces a workbook with exactly the visible rows.
- Open any GenericReportPage-backed report (e.g. MemberRegistrationReport / ListOfDirectors). Confirm the button still reads 'Excel', no modal appears, and clicking it still enqueues/downloads the full server-side export (onExcel/generateExcel path unchanged).
- Run `cd Frontend && npx tsc --noEmit` (or `npm run build`) to confirm no TypeScript errors from the new Modal import and added consts.

**Pitfalls / rollback.** Scope: do not 'fix' MemberRegistrationReport.tsx or ListOfDirectors.tsx — they use onExcel (server queue) and the audit location list is wrong about MemberRegistrationReport. Touching them risks regressing the working full-export path.; isPartialExport relies on `data.totalCount` and `pageSize` being current; both are component state already used by the Pagination control, so no extra fetch is needed. If a future caller uses the client fallback without server pagination (totalCount <= pageSize), the modal correctly does not appear and behavior is unchanged.; The UserList 'password' column is a separate security/privacy concern (sensitive field rendered in a grid and exportable). File it as its own finding; do not bundle it into this UX change or the fix's scope becomes ambiguous.; Rollback is trivial: revert BasicTable.tsx; no data migration, no API/contract change, no backend impact.

> 🔍 **Verifier note.** The recommendation is still reasonable (route through server queue, or relabel/disable the client fallback for paginated tables). Practically, the cleanest fix is to make the fallback safe or remove it, since the only consumer (UserList) is paginated server-side. Note also: UserList renders a 'password' column, which is a separate concern not in scope here but worth flagging. Remove MemberRegistrationReport.tsx from the finding's locations — it is not affected.


<a id="f-table-a11y-and-sticky-header"></a>
#### 🔵 LOW — Report table lacks header scope/caption for screen readers and has no sticky header on tall/wide grids

**ID:** `table-a11y-and-sticky-header` &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Phase:** P3 &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Category:** Accessibility &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Effort:** Small &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Confidence:** High

**Location(s):**
- `Frontend/src/components/My Components/Table/BasicTable.tsx:362-395`
- `Frontend/src/components/My Components/Table/style.css:52-56`
- `Frontend/src/components/My Components/Table/style.css:112-139`

**Problem.** BasicTable renders a raw <table> with <th> cells that have no scope="col" attribute and no <caption> (362-395), so screen readers cannot reliably associate data cells with their column headers. The table container is overflow-x:auto (style.css:52-56) but there is no position:sticky on thead and no sticky first column, and reports can have many wide columns (reportConfigs.ts defines 1200+ columns across reports). With up to 1000 rows per page (BasicTable.tsx:497) the header scrolls out of view.

**Impact.** Assistive-technology users get an unlabeled grid of numbers; sighted users scrolling a wide/tall financial report lose the column headers and row context, increasing the chance of misreading values in a government report.

**Recommendation.** Add scope="col" to header <th> and a visually-hidden <caption> with the report title; apply position:sticky; top:0 to thead (and optionally sticky the No/first column) so headers stay visible while scrolling.

**🛠 How to Fix _(est. 30 min)_**

Add semantic table-header associations (scope="col" on every column header <th> and a visually-hidden <caption> built from the report title) and make the <thead> sticky via CSS so headers stay visible while scrolling tall/wide report grids. All changes live in BasicTable.tsx (markup) and style.css (sticky rules + visually-hidden caption); no API or data changes.

1. Edit `Frontend/src/components/My Components/Table/BasicTable.tsx`. Right after the opening `<table id={tableId}>` on line 362, add a `<caption>` element that uses the existing `title` prop (already destructured at line 128, defaults to 'Table'): `<caption className="visually-hidden">{title}</caption>`. Place it before `<thead>` (caption must be the first child of the table per HTML spec).
2. In the same file, add `scope="col"` to the row-number header `<th>` at line 377: change `{showRowNumber && <th>{rowNumberTitle}</th>}` to `{showRowNumber && <th scope="col">{rowNumberTitle}</th>}`.
3. Add `scope="col"` to the mapped column-header `<th>` at lines 382-390: add the attribute alongside the existing `key` and inline `style={{ width: column.width }}`.
4. Add `scope="col"` to the Action header `<th className="action-column-header">Action</th>` at line 393.
5. Do NOT add `scope` to the `report-header-row` heading `<th>` at lines 368-373 (it is a spanning banner, not a column header); leave it as-is.
6. Edit `Frontend/src/components/My Components/Table/style.css`. Extend the existing `.table-container thead th` rule (lines 132-139) by adding `position: sticky; top: 0; z-index: 2;` so the whole header band (both the report-header rows and the column-header row) sticks to the top while the body scrolls vertically. Sticky must be on the `th` cells (not `thead`) because the table uses `border-collapse: collapse` (lines 4 and 114), where sticky on `thead`/`tr` is ignored by most browsers.
7. Because `border-collapse: collapse` drops the th's own border when scrolled, replace the header's `border: 1px solid #a3a3a3;` with a `box-shadow: inset 0 0 0 1px #a3a3a3;` (or add `box-shadow: 0 1px 0 #a3a3a3;` as a bottom divider) so the gridline stays visible over scrolled rows. Keep the existing `background-color: #e4e4e4;` (an opaque background is required so body rows don't show through the sticky header).
8. Add a `.visually-hidden` utility class to `style.css` (it does not exist yet) so the caption is announced by screen readers but not painted: `.visually-hidden { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0 0 0 0); clip-path: inset(50%); white-space: nowrap; border: 0; }`.
9. Optional (defer unless requested): to also sticky the first/No column horizontally you would add `position: sticky; left: 0` to the first `td`/`th`; this is fragile with the existing colSpan report-header rows and variable column widths, so leave it out of this fix unless a follow-up explicitly asks for it (the recommendation lists it as optional).

```tsx
// Frontend/src/components/My Components/Table/BasicTable.tsx

// BEFORE (line 362)
<table id={tableId}>
  <thead>
    ...
      <tr>
        {showRowNumber && <th>{rowNumberTitle}</th>}
        {normalizedColumns.map((column) => {
          const key = column.key.toString();
          return (
            <th
              key={key}
              style={{ width: column.width }}
            >
              {column.title ?? NameConvert(key)}
            </th>
          );
        })}
        {shouldShowActions && (
          <th className="action-column-header">Action</th>
        )}
      </tr>
  </thead>

// AFTER
<table id={tableId}>
  <caption className="visually-hidden">{title}</caption>
  <thead>
    ...
      <tr>
        {showRowNumber && <th scope="col">{rowNumberTitle}</th>}
        {normalizedColumns.map((column) => {
          const key = column.key.toString();
          return (
            <th
              key={key}
              scope="col"
              style={{ width: column.width }}
            >
              {column.title ?? NameConvert(key)}
            </th>
          );
        })}
        {shouldShowActions && (
          <th scope="col" className="action-column-header">Action</th>
        )}
      </tr>
  </thead>

/* Frontend/src/components/My Components/Table/style.css */

/* BEFORE (lines 132-139) */
.table-container thead th {
    background-color: #e4e4e4;
    border: 1px solid #a3a3a3;
    color: #000000;
    font-weight: bold;
    white-space: nowrap;
}

/* AFTER */
.table-container thead th {
    background-color: #e4e4e4;          /* opaque so scrolled rows don't show through */
    box-shadow: inset 0 0 0 1px #a3a3a3; /* keep gridline; border collapses away when sticky */
    color: #000000;
    font-weight: bold;
    white-space: nowrap;
    position: sticky;
    top: 0;
    z-index: 2;
}

/* ADD anywhere in style.css */
.visually-hidden {
    position: absolute;
    width: 1px;
    height: 1px;
    padding: 0;
    margin: -1px;
    overflow: hidden;
    clip: rect(0 0 0 0);
    clip-path: inset(50%);
    white-space: nowrap;
    border: 0;
}
```

**Verify:**
- Run `npm run build` (or `npm run dev`) in `Frontend/` and confirm no TypeScript/JSX errors from the new caption/scope attributes.
- Open any report (e.g. an Import Permit report) in the browser, set the page size to 1000 (BasicTable.tsx pageSizeOptions, line 497), and scroll the grid vertically: the gray column-header band (and the centered report-header lines) must remain pinned at the top of `.table-container`.
- Inspect the rendered table in DevTools: every column `<th>` (including the 'No' and 'Action' headers) has `scope="col"`, and the table's first child is `<caption class="visually-hidden">` showing the report title; the caption must not be visible on screen.
- Run an axe/Lighthouse accessibility pass (or `npx @axe-core/cli`) on a report page and confirm the prior 'th elements should have scope' / 'table has no caption' findings are gone.
- With VoiceOver/NVDA, navigate a data cell and confirm the column header name is announced (proves the scope association works).

**Pitfalls / rollback.** Order matters in HTML: `<caption>` must be the FIRST child of `<table>`, before `<thead>`. Placing it after thead will render but is invalid and some assistive tech ignores it.; Do not put `position: sticky` on `<thead>` or `<tr>` — the table uses `border-collapse: collapse` (style.css lines 4 and 114), under which sticky on thead/tr is ignored by Chrome/Safari. It must go on the `th` cells.; The sticky header background MUST stay opaque (#e4e4e4); if it were made transparent, scrolled body rows would bleed through. Note the `.report-viewer-container` override at line 184 sets the header background to #ffffff — that is still opaque, so it remains fine, but verify sticky also looks correct inside the ReportViewer skin.; Because all thead rows (the colSpan report-header banner rows AND the column-header row) get `top:0`, they all pin to the very top and stack on top of each other only if their heights differ; in practice the browser stacks them in document order so they appear as one sticky block. If a future requirement needs the banner to scroll away while only column headers stick, switch to per-row `top` offsets instead.; The change is purely additive/CSS; rollback is reverting the two files. No data migration, no API contract change.

> 🔍 **Verifier note.** Severity Low is appropriate and accurate: this is an accessibility/UX enhancement, not a functional or security defect, and the app is an internal government admin reporting tool rather than a public-facing site. Every concrete claim in the finding holds up against the actual code. The two CSS line ranges are both relevant (52-56 = overflow container; 112-139 = the ReportViewer thead styling where a position:sticky;top:0 rule would be added). The recommendation (scope="col", visually-hidden caption, sticky thead/first column) is sound. No corrections to location needed.


<a id="f-dark-theme-dead-and-tables-hardcode-light"></a>
#### 🔵 LOW — Dark theme is supported in code but has no UI toggle, and report tables hardcode light colors

**ID:** `dark-theme-dead-and-tables-hardcode-light` &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Phase:** P3 &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Category:** Theming &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Effort:** Medium &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Confidence:** High

**Location(s):**
- `Frontend/src/redux/theme/themeSlice.ts:15-21`
- `Frontend/src/App.tsx:90-93`
- `Frontend/src/components/My Components/Table/style.css:24-31`
- `Frontend/src/components/My Components/Table/style.css:43-56`
- `Frontend/src/components/My Components/Table/style.css:112-120`

**Problem.** A toggleTheme reducer exists and App.tsx switches AntD between darkAlgorithm/defaultAlgorithm (90-93), but toggleTheme is never dispatched anywhere in the UI (no theme switch control), so dark mode is unreachable dead code. Separately, the report table CSS hardcodes light values (.container background #ffffff, th background #FAFAFA/#e4e4e4, color #000000 — style.css:24-31, 43-56, 112-120), so even if dark mode were enabled the report grids would render black-on-white, ignoring the theme.

**Impact.** A persisted Redux/redux-persist 'dark' value (or any future toggle) would produce an inconsistent, partly-broken UI where chrome is dark but report tables stay white. Low impact today because the toggle is unreachable, but it is latent debt and confusing dead code.

**Recommendation.** Either remove the unused dark-theme code path, or expose a theme toggle and convert the hardcoded table colors in style.css to AntD theme tokens (CSS variables) so the grid follows the active algorithm.

**🛠 How to Fix _(est. 45 min)_**

Remove the unreachable dark-theme code path entirely (no UI ever dispatches it, the report grids are an intentional SSRS white-paper facsimile that must stay light, and the persisted value is latent debt). Hardcode AntD to defaultAlgorithm, drop the unused themeSlice/selectors, and bump the redux-persist version so any stale `mytheme: 'dark'` in users' localStorage is purged. This is lower-risk than building a real dark mode for a sensitive gov system. (If product instead wants a real toggle later, see pitfalls.)

1. In Frontend/src/App.tsx, delete the theme selector and the conditional algorithm. Remove the imports `theme as antdTheme` from 'antd' (keep `ConfigProvider`), `useSelector` from 'react-redux', and `RootState` from './redux/store' (verify none are used elsewhere in this file first — they are not). Delete line 28 `const { mytheme } = useSelector((state: RootState) => state.theme);`.
2. In the same ConfigProvider theme object (App.tsx:90-93), replace the conditional `algorithm: mytheme === 'dark' ? antdTheme.darkAlgorithm : antdTheme.defaultAlgorithm,` with the static `algorithm: theme.defaultAlgorithm,` (import `theme` from 'antd'), or simply delete the `algorithm` property entirely since defaultAlgorithm is AntD's default.
3. In Frontend/src/layouts/app/App.tsx, delete the dead read on line 55 `useSelector((state: RootState) => state.theme);`. Then check whether `useSelector` and `RootState` are still used elsewhere in that file — `RootState` import is on line 33 and `useSelector` on line 32; if no other usage remains, remove both imports.
4. Delete the now-unused Redux theme slice: remove the file Frontend/src/redux/theme/themeSlice.ts (it exports `toggleTheme`, `ThemeState`, and the reducer, none of which will be referenced after the edits above).
5. In Frontend/src/redux/store.ts, remove the theme reducer wiring: drop the `import themeReducer, { ThemeState } from './theme/themeSlice';` line, remove the `theme: ThemeState;` field from the `RootState` interface, and remove `theme: themeReducer` from `combineReducers`. Because `combineReducers` requires at least one reducer, either keep redux-persist for a future-proof empty root or remove the store entirely if nothing else consumes it — see pitfalls. Simplest: replace the slice with a tiny no-op `app` slice OR keep the store but add a placeholder reducer; if the Provider/store is still mounted in main.tsx/index, do NOT delete the store file.
6. Bump the redux-persist migration version in store.ts `persistConfig` from `version: 1` to `version: 2` so previously persisted state (including any `mytheme: 'dark'`) is discarded on next load instead of rehydrating a dead key.
7. In Frontend/src/components/My Components/Table/style.css, leave the report-grid colors as-is. They are an intentional SSRS/ReportViewer white-paper facsimile (.table-container at 112-139 and .report-viewer-container at 146-193) and must render black-on-white for print/official parity — do NOT tokenize them to follow a (now-removed) dark algorithm.
8. Run a project-wide grep to confirm no dangling references remain: `grep -rn "toggleTheme\|themeSlice\|state.theme\|mytheme\|darkAlgorithm" Frontend/src/` should return nothing after the edits.

```tsx
// Frontend/src/App.tsx
// BEFORE
import { ConfigProvider, theme as antdTheme } from 'antd';
import { useSelector } from 'react-redux';
import { RootState } from './redux/store';
...
function App() {
  const { mytheme } = useSelector((state: RootState) => state.theme);
  ...
          algorithm:
            mytheme === 'dark'
              ? antdTheme.darkAlgorithm
              : antdTheme.defaultAlgorithm,

// AFTER
import { ConfigProvider, theme } from 'antd';
...
function App() {
  // single light theme; dark mode removed (was unreachable dead code)
  ...
          algorithm: theme.defaultAlgorithm,

// Frontend/src/layouts/app/App.tsx
// BEFORE
import { useSelector } from 'react-redux';
import { RootState } from '../../redux/store.ts';
...
  useSelector((state: RootState) => state.theme);   // dead read -> DELETE
// AFTER  (remove the line; remove the two imports if now unused)

// Frontend/src/redux/store.ts
// BEFORE
const persistConfig: PersistConfig<RootState> = {
  key: 'root',
  storage,
  version: 1,
};
// AFTER
const persistConfig: PersistConfig<RootState> = {
  key: 'root',
  storage,
  version: 2,   // bumped to purge stale persisted { theme: { mytheme: 'dark' } }
};

// Frontend/src/redux/theme/themeSlice.ts  ->  DELETE FILE
```

**Verify:**
- Build the frontend: `cd Frontend && npm run build` (or `tsc -b`) — it must compile with no unused-import / missing-module errors, proving themeSlice/RootState.theme references are fully removed.
- `grep -rn "toggleTheme\|themeSlice\|state.theme\|mytheme\|darkAlgorithm" Frontend/src/` returns zero matches.
- Run the app (`npm run dev`), open any report grid (e.g. a BasicTable report). The table renders the expected SSRS white-paper look (white .container, gray #e4e4e4 header band, black text) — unchanged from before.
- Regression for stale persisted state: in DevTools Application > Local Storage, set `persist:root` to include a theme with mytheme:'dark', reload — with version bumped to 2, redux-persist discards the old payload and the UI stays light (no half-dark chrome).

**Pitfalls / rollback.** combineReducers must contain at least one reducer. If `theme` was the ONLY slice, removing it makes `combineReducers({})` invalid and an empty RootState. Either (a) keep the store but register a trivial placeholder reducer, or (b) if nothing else in the app reads the store/Provider, also remove the redux Provider + PersistGate from main.tsx/index.tsx and delete store.ts. Grep `grep -rn "redux/store\|<Provider\|PersistGate\|useSelector\|useDispatch" Frontend/src/` BEFORE deleting to confirm the store is otherwise unused.; Do not skip the `version: 1 -> 2` bump: without it, redux-persist will rehydrate the old persisted blob and (if you removed the theme reducer) may log a non-fatal reducer-mismatch warning while leaving the dead key in localStorage.; Order: remove the consumers (both App.tsx files) BEFORE deleting themeSlice.ts and editing store.ts, otherwise the build breaks mid-edit on missing imports.; Rollback is trivial (revert the commit); no DB/server changes and no API contract is touched — this is frontend-only.; If product later wants a REAL dark mode instead of removal: keep the slice, add a toggle control (e.g. in HeaderNav.tsx) that dispatches toggleTheme, and tokenize ONLY the chrome — but keep .table-container / .report-viewer-container hardcoded light, since those grids are an intentional print/official ReportViewer facsimile.

> 🔍 **Verifier note.** Severity Low is appropriate and the finding does not overstate impact — it explicitly notes the toggle is unreachable today and impact is latent debt/dead code. Cited line ranges all match real code. Minor nuance: the colors that most directly govern the report grid are in the .table-container block (style.css:112-139, partially cited at 112-120), while :24-31/:43-56 cover the more generic global table and .container wrapper; both are accurate, so no location correction is needed. Recommendation (remove dead path, or expose a toggle plus tokenize the table CSS) is sound.


<a id="f-no-i18n-and-no-antd-locale"></a>
#### 🔵 LOW — No internationalization and no AntD locale despite Myanmar/English requirement — all UI chrome is English-only

**ID:** `no-i18n-and-no-antd-locale` &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Phase:** P3 &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Category:** i18n &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Effort:** Large &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Confidence:** High

**Location(s):**
- `Frontend/src/App.tsx:32-94; Frontend/src/components/My Components/Table/BasicTable.tsx:494-496; Frontend/src/Report/Page/GenericReportPage.tsx:425-471,770-780`
- `Frontend/src/App.tsx:32-94`
- `Frontend/src/Report/Page/GenericReportPage.tsx:425-471`
- `Frontend/src/components/My Components/Table/BasicTable.tsx:494-510`
- `Frontend/src/Report/config/reportConfigs.ts`

**Problem.** There is no i18n framework (no react-i18next/useTranslation/i18n anywhere) and ConfigProvider (App.tsx:32-94) sets no `locale`, so AntD DatePicker, RangePicker, Pagination ('x-y of N total'), Empty, and Popconfirm all render their built-in English strings. All filter labels, button text ('Filter', 'Reset', 'Excel'), report titles, and the state/status option lists in reportConfigs.ts are hardcoded English. Myanmar script appears only once in the entire config — inside a code comment (reportConfigs.ts:6556) — never in user-facing UI. The project mandate (CLAUDE.md) requires Myanmar/English parity with the old Tradenet 2.0 RDLC reports, some of whose headers/columns are Myanmar.

**Impact.** Myanmar-speaking ministry staff get an all-English admin UI with no language option; date pickers and pagination cannot be localized; and reports cannot reproduce the Myanmar column/header text the old system showed, breaking the parity requirement the codebase is explicitly built around.

**Recommendation.** At minimum pass a `locale` to ConfigProvider (AntD ships my_MM / en_US locales) so date/pagination/empty chrome can localize. For real parity, introduce a lightweight i18n layer (react-i18next or a static dictionary keyed off the report config) so titles, filter labels, and column headers can carry both English and Myanmar strings as the RDLC originals do.

**🛠 How to Fix _(est. 2 hours)_**

Pass an AntD `locale` to the existing `ConfigProvider` in App.tsx so DatePicker/RangePicker, Pagination, Empty and Popconfirm chrome can render in Myanmar or English. Drive the choice from a new redux-persisted `locale` slice (mirroring the existing `themeSlice`), keep dayjs's locale in sync, and localize the one hardcoded English `showTotal` string in BasicTable. This is the verifier-endorsed low-priority fix; a full per-string i18n dictionary for filter labels/titles is noted as a follow-on, not done here.

1. Create a locale slice mirroring src/redux/theme/themeSlice.ts. New file Frontend/src/redux/locale/localeSlice.ts exporting `LocaleState { lang: 'en' | 'my' }` with `initialState = { lang: 'en' }`, a `createSlice({ name: 'locale', reducers: { setLanguage(state, action: PayloadAction<'en'|'my'>) {...}, toggleLanguage(state) {...} } })`, and export the actions plus the reducer as default.
2. Register the slice in Frontend/src/redux/store.ts: import `localeReducer, { LocaleState } from './locale/localeSlice'`, add `locale: LocaleState` to the `RootState` interface, and add `locale: localeReducer` to the `combineReducers({...})` call. It is automatically persisted because the existing `persistConfig` persists the whole root reducer.
3. In Frontend/src/App.tsx add imports at top: `import { useEffect } from 'react';`, `import enUS from 'antd/locale/en_US';`, `import myMM from 'antd/locale/my_MM';`, `import dayjs from 'dayjs';`, `import 'dayjs/locale/en';`, `import 'dayjs/locale/my';`. (`useSelector` and `RootState` are already imported.)
4. In App.tsx `App()`, read the language next to the existing theme: `const { lang } = useSelector((state: RootState) => state.locale);`, compute `const antdLocale = lang === 'my' ? myMM : enUS;`, and sync dayjs in an effect: `useEffect(() => { dayjs.locale(lang === 'my' ? 'my' : 'en'); }, [lang]);`.
5. Add the `locale` prop to the existing `<ConfigProvider>` opening tag in App.tsx (line 32): add `locale={antdLocale}` while leaving the entire `theme={{...}}` block unchanged. This localizes DatePicker/RangePicker, Pagination page-size/jump labels, Empty, and Popconfirm.
6. Localize the hardcoded pagination summary in Frontend/src/components/My Components/Table/BasicTable.tsx (lines 494-496). Add `import enUS from 'antd/locale/en_US'; import myMM from 'antd/locale/my_MM';` and read the language inside the component (`const { lang } = useSelector((state: RootState) => state.locale);` — BasicTable already uses hooks). Replace the inline English template `showTotal` with the locale's `Pagination.total_text` template, filling its {range0}/{range1}/{total} placeholders, with the original English string as fallback.
7. (Optional, recommended) Add a language switch beside the existing theme toggle. Grep `toggleTheme` under Frontend/src to find where the theme dispatch lives, then add a sibling Segmented/Switch dispatching `setLanguage`/`toggleLanguage` from the new localeSlice so ministry staff can pick Myanmar vs English.
8. (Follow-on, larger, out of scope for this Low finding) For full RDLC parity introduce a static dictionary keyed off Frontend/src/Report/config/reportConfigs.ts so report `title`, filter labels, the hardcoded 'Filter'/'Reset' buttons in GenericReportPage.tsx (lines 775/778), the 'From Date'/'To Date' placeholders (lines 430-431), and the 'No'/'Yes' boolean options (lines 460-461) carry both English and Myanmar strings. Track scope in ReportColumnComparison.md.

```tsx
// Frontend/src/redux/locale/localeSlice.ts  (NEW)
import { createSlice, PayloadAction } from '@reduxjs/toolkit';

export interface LocaleState {
  lang: 'en' | 'my';
}

const initialState: LocaleState = { lang: 'en' };

const localeSlice = createSlice({
  name: 'locale',
  initialState,
  reducers: {
    setLanguage: (state, action: PayloadAction<'en' | 'my'>) => {
      state.lang = action.payload;
    },
    toggleLanguage: (state) => {
      state.lang = state.lang === 'en' ? 'my' : 'en';
    },
  },
});

export const { setLanguage, toggleLanguage } = localeSlice.actions;
export default localeSlice.reducer;

// Frontend/src/redux/store.ts
-import themeReducer, { ThemeState } from './theme/themeSlice';
+import themeReducer, { ThemeState } from './theme/themeSlice';
+import localeReducer, { LocaleState } from './locale/localeSlice';
 interface RootState {
   theme: ThemeState;
+  locale: LocaleState;
 }
 const rootReducer = combineReducers({
   theme: themeReducer,
+  locale: localeReducer,
 });

// Frontend/src/App.tsx
-import { ConfigProvider, theme as antdTheme } from 'antd';
+import { useEffect } from 'react';
+import { ConfigProvider, theme as antdTheme } from 'antd';
+import enUS from 'antd/locale/en_US';
+import myMM from 'antd/locale/my_MM';
+import dayjs from 'dayjs';
+import 'dayjs/locale/en';
+import 'dayjs/locale/my';

 function App() {
   const { mytheme } = useSelector((state: RootState) => state.theme);
+  const { lang } = useSelector((state: RootState) => state.locale);
+  const antdLocale = lang === 'my' ? myMM : enUS;
+  useEffect(() => {
+    dayjs.locale(lang === 'my' ? 'my' : 'en');
+  }, [lang]);

       <ConfigProvider
+        locale={antdLocale}
         theme={{ /* ...unchanged... */ }}
       >

// Frontend/src/components/My Components/Table/BasicTable.tsx (around 491-496)
   <Pagination
     showSizeChanger
-    showTotal={(total, range) =>
-      `${range[0]}-${range[1]} of ${total} total`
-    }
+    // ConfigProvider localizes page-size/jump labels; localize the summary too:
+    showTotal={(total, range) => {
+      const p = lang === 'my' ? myMM.Pagination : enUS.Pagination;
+      return p?.total_text
+        ? p.total_text
+            .replace('{range0}', String(range[0]))
+            .replace('{range1}', String(range[1]))
+            .replace('{total}', String(total))
+        : `${range[0]}-${range[1]} of ${total} total`;
+    }}
```

**Verify:**
- cd Frontend && npm run build (or npx tsc --noEmit) — confirms the new slice, store wiring, and ConfigProvider `locale` prop type-check against antd 5.26 (both antd/locale/en_US and antd/locale/my_MM resolve; verified present under node_modules/antd/locale/).
- Run the app (npm run dev), open any report under GenericReportPage, click a DatePicker/RangePicker — with lang='en' the calendar/month names render in English; switch language to 'my' (dispatch setLanguage('my') or via the new toggle) and observe the same picker now shows Myanmar month/weekday labels and the Pagination 'x-y of N total' summary switches to the my_MM template.
- Verify persistence: switch to Myanmar, refresh the browser — redux-persist restores state.locale.lang='my' so the UI stays Myanmar (the existing persistConfig already persists the whole root reducer).
- Empty-state check: load a report that returns zero rows — the AntD Empty 'No data' text follows the selected locale instead of the built-in English string.

**Pitfalls / rollback.** Do NOT modify the existing `theme={{...}}` object on ConfigProvider — only add the sibling `locale` prop; reformatting the large theme block risks merge noise. The dayjs locale must be set globally (dayjs.locale) AND the dayjs/locale/my data imported, otherwise RangePicker still shows English month names even with the AntD locale set — AntD's date components read dayjs's active locale, not ConfigProvider alone. Because persistConfig persists the whole root, existing users have a persisted root without `locale`; redux-persist merges, so the slice's initialState (lang:'en') fills the gap on first load — no migration needed. The my_MM Pagination.total_text uses {range0}/{range1}/{total} placeholders; the English fallback string keeps pagination working if a future antd minor renames them. The full filter-label/title/column i18n (step 8) is explicitly out of scope for this Low finding — don't attempt the per-report dictionary in the same PR or it balloons into the 'Large' effort the audit flagged.

> 🔍 **Verifier note.** Real on the no-locale/no-i18n/English-chrome axis (verified verbatim), but impact narrative exaggerated. CompanyProfile.tsx refutes the no-Myanmar-headers and broken-parity claims. Actionable low-priority fix: pass locale (my_MM/en_US) to ConfigProvider to localize date pickers and pagination. reportConfigs.ts was cited without a line range; its only Myanmar (line 6556) is a comment, consistent with the finding.


---

### Frontend — Code Quality

> The frontend is a React 19 / TS 5.8 / Ant Design 5 app built on the "antd-multi-dashboard" template, with a strong, modern strict-mode tsconfig and Prettier. However, code quality is undermined by three structural problems: (1) the lint gate is silently broken — ESLint 9 is installed but configured with a legacy .eslintrc.cjs and an unsupported `--ext` flag, so `npm run lint` errors out without linting and 172 real errors / 9 warnings (mostly `no-explicit-any`, missing-deps, unused vars) go undetected; (2) roughly half the shipped code is dead template demo material (94 dashboard components, 64 stories, 28 mock JSON files, a literal `SideNav copy.tsx`) that accounts for nearly all the `any`/`@ts-ignore` usage and pads the bundle of a government production app; (3) the data layer has genuine bugs and anti-patterns — a `useFetchData` hook with a stale-closure dependency bug that bypasses auth, a `BasicHttpServices` layer that lies to the type system by casting every response to `PaginationType` and deep-clones via `JSON.parse(JSON.stringify())`, and an `AnyObject` escape-hatch type. The folder named with a literal space ("My Components") is an import-fragility hazard. Strict mode passes `tsc` only because `any` and `@ts-ignore` suppress the errors it would otherwise catch.

<a id="f-broken-eslint-gate"></a>
#### 🟡 MEDIUM — ESLint 9 paired with legacy .eslintrc + unsupported --ext flag: lint gate runs nothing

**ID:** `broken-eslint-gate` &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Phase:** P2 &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Category:** Tooling / CI quality gate &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Effort:** Medium &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Confidence:** High

**Location(s):**
- `Frontend/package.json:11`
- `Frontend/.eslintrc.cjs:1`
- `Frontend/package.json:69`

**Problem.** ESLint 9.28.0 is installed (package.json:69) but config lives in the legacy `.eslintrc.cjs` flat-config-incompatible format, and the lint script is `eslint . --ext ts,tsx --report-unused-disable-directives --max-warnings 0`. Running the documented command fails: ESLint v9 dropped `--ext` and requires `eslint.config.js`, so it prints "ESLint couldn't find an eslint.config.(js|mjs|cjs) file" and lints nothing. Only by forcing the deprecated `ESLINT_USE_FLAT_CONFIG=false` env var does it run, and then it reports 181 problems (172 errors, 9 warnings).

**Impact.** The team's only static-analysis quality gate is dead. CI either skips lint or passes it vacuously, so 172 real errors (66+ `no-explicit-any`, unused vars, missing hook deps, empty patterns) accumulate undetected. New regressions in this government reporting app land with no automated review.

**Recommendation.** Migrate to ESLint flat config: create `eslint.config.js` using `typescript-eslint`, `eslint-plugin-react-hooks`, and `eslint-plugin-react-refresh`, delete `.eslintrc.cjs`, and change the script to `eslint src` (no `--ext`). Then triage the 172 errors. Verify `npm run lint` exits non-zero on a seeded violation before trusting CI.

**Example:**

```
// current (package.json) — fails under ESLint 9
"lint": "eslint . --ext ts,tsx --report-unused-disable-directives --max-warnings 0"
// fixed: eslint.config.js (flat) + script
"lint": "eslint src --report-unused-disable-directives --max-warnings 0"
```

**🛠 How to Fix _(est. Tooling migration + CI wiring: ~1 hour. Triaging the ~172 accumulated lint errors: ~1 day (separate task).)_**

Migrate the Frontend lint setup from the dead legacy `.eslintrc.cjs` (incompatible with ESLint 9) to a flat `eslint.config.js`, fix the `lint` npm script to drop the unsupported `--ext` flag, add the missing `typescript-eslint` meta-package, then wire a real lint step into CI so the gate actually runs.

1. Add the missing meta package: in Frontend run `npm install -D typescript-eslint@^8.34.0`. (`@typescript-eslint/parser` and `@typescript-eslint/eslint-plugin` 8.34.0 are already in devDependencies, and `@eslint/js` + `globals` are already in node_modules; only the umbrella `typescript-eslint` package is absent — verified it is NOT installed.)
2. Create Frontend/eslint.config.js as an ESM flat config (the project has `"type": "module"` in package.json, so `.js` is ESM — do NOT use `.cjs`). Use `tseslint.config(...)` from `typescript-eslint`, extend `js.configs.recommended` and `...tseslint.configs.recommended`, set `languageOptions.globals = globals.browser`, register the `react-hooks` and `react-refresh` plugins, spread `reactHooks.configs['recommended-latest'].rules` (this is the flat-config rules object exposed by eslint-plugin-react-hooks@5.2.0), and re-add the existing `'react-refresh/only-export-components': ['warn', { allowConstantExport: true }]` rule that was in .eslintrc.cjs. Append `...storybook.configs['flat/recommended']` (eslint-plugin-storybook@9.0.9 exposes the `flat/recommended` key — confirmed) so the storybook lint rules carried over from the old `plugin:storybook/recommended` extend are preserved.
3. Set `ignores: ['dist', 'storybook-static', '*.config.js', '*.config.ts']` as the first config object so the config file and build output are not linted (replaces the old `ignorePatterns: ['dist', '.eslintrc.cjs']`). Scope the TS rules block with `files: ['**/*.{ts,tsx}']`.
4. Edit Frontend/package.json line 11: change the `lint` script from `eslint . --ext ts,tsx --report-unused-disable-directives --max-warnings 0` to `eslint src --report-unused-disable-directives --max-warnings 0`. The `--ext` flag is removed in ESLint 9 (flat config infers extensions from the `files` globs); narrowing to `src` avoids linting config/build files.
5. Delete Frontend/.eslintrc.cjs. Leaving it alongside eslint.config.js is harmless to ESLint 9 (flat config takes precedence) but is dead/misleading config — remove it.
6. Run `npm run lint` once. Expect it to now actually execute and report the backlog (~181 problems: ~172 errors, ~9 warnings per the audit). Triage these in a separate follow-up commit — do NOT mix the large auto-fix/manual-fix churn with this tooling migration. For a quick first pass run `npx eslint src --fix` to clear mechanically-fixable items, then address the remaining `@typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any` (66+ hits), unused vars, missing hook deps, and empty patterns by hand.
7. Wire lint into CI so the gate is real (the audit verifier confirmed NO workflow currently runs lint). In Frontend/.github/workflows/test.yml, add a step before the `Build application` step (after `Clean install dependencies`): name `Lint`, `run: npm run lint`, with `working-directory` matching where the other steps run. Also bump the stale `node-version` matrix (currently `16.x`/`18.x`) to a version ESLint 9 and Vite 6 support, e.g. `20.x`, since ESLint 9 requires Node >=18.18 and the current matrix would fail install. Confirm the workflow's default working directory points at Frontend (the workflow file lives under Frontend/.github, so paths resolve relative to repo Frontend dir when checked out — verify the runner's cwd, set `defaults.run.working-directory: Frontend` if the repo is checked out at the monorepo root).

```json
// 1. Frontend/package.json:11 — fix the script (--ext is removed in ESLint 9)
// before:
"lint": "eslint . --ext ts,tsx --report-unused-disable-directives --max-warnings 0",
// after:
"lint": "eslint src --report-unused-disable-directives --max-warnings 0",

// 2. Frontend/package.json devDependencies — add the meta package (NOT installed yet).
//    @typescript-eslint/parser ^8.34.0 and @typescript-eslint/eslint-plugin ^8.34.0
//    are already present; add the umbrella package that re-exports them for flat config:
"typescript-eslint": "^8.34.0",

// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
// 3. NEW FILE: Frontend/eslint.config.js  (flat config, replaces .eslintrc.cjs)
//    patchLang for this file is really JS; shown here together for context.
//
// import js from '@eslint/js'
// import globals from 'globals'
// import reactHooks from 'eslint-plugin-react-hooks'
// import reactRefresh from 'eslint-plugin-react-refresh'
// import storybook from 'eslint-plugin-storybook'
// import tseslint from 'typescript-eslint'
//
// export default tseslint.config(
//   { ignores: ['dist', 'storybook-static', '*.config.js', '*.config.ts'] },
//   {
//     files: ['**/*.{ts,tsx}'],
//     extends: [
//       js.configs.recommended,
//       ...tseslint.configs.recommended,
//     ],
//     languageOptions: {
//       ecmaVersion: 2020,
//       globals: globals.browser,
//     },
//     plugins: {
//       'react-hooks': reactHooks,
//       'react-refresh': reactRefresh,
//     },
//     rules: {
//       ...reactHooks.configs['recommended-latest'].rules,
//       'react-refresh/only-export-components': [
//         'warn',
//         { allowConstantExport: true },
//       ],
//     },
//   },
//   ...storybook.configs['flat/recommended'],
// )
//
// 4. DELETE Frontend/.eslintrc.cjs
```

**Verify:**
- From Frontend/, run `npx eslint --print-config src/main.tsx | head` — it should print a resolved flat config (proves eslint.config.js is picked up) instead of the v9 'couldn't find an eslint.config file' error.
- Run `npm run lint`; confirm it exits non-zero (code 1) and prints the real backlog of problems, rather than exiting 2 with the 'ESLint couldn't find an eslint.config.(js|mjs|cjs)' message that the broken command produced (reproduced: `npx eslint . --ext ts,tsx` currently errors with exactly that).
- Seed a deliberate violation to prove the gate bites: add `const x: any = 1;` to a file under src/, run `npm run lint`, confirm it reports `@typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any` and exits 1; remove the seed and (after triage) confirm a clean exit 0.
- After the triage commit, run `npm run lint` and confirm exit 0 with `--max-warnings 0` honored.
- Push a branch and confirm the CI `Test` workflow now shows a `Lint` step that runs `npm run lint` and fails the job on lint errors.

**Pitfalls / rollback.** Ordering: install `typescript-eslint` and create eslint.config.js BEFORE deleting .eslintrc.cjs and before changing the script, so you never have a window where lint is more broken than now. Because package.json has `"type": "module"`, eslint.config.js is ESM — use `import`/`export default`, not `module.exports`; a CJS-style file will throw at load. Do NOT separate the two churn sources: keep the tooling migration (config + script + CI) in one commit and the ~172 error fixes in a follow-up, so the large diff doesn't bury the migration and can be rolled back independently. `eslint-plugin-react-hooks@5.2.0` flat rules live under `configs['recommended-latest']` (the `recommended` key is the legacy/eslintrc form) — using the wrong key silently applies no rules. `eslint-plugin-storybook` uses the `flat/recommended` key for flat config (the bare `recommended` is legacy). The CI node matrix is `16.x`/`18.x`; ESLint 9 requires Node >=18.18.0, so `16.x` will fail — bump it or the new lint step errors for an unrelated reason. Rollback: restore .eslintrc.cjs and the old script, remove eslint.config.js; the legacy path then works again only via `ESLINT_USE_FLAT_CONFIG=false` (deprecated), so prefer fixing forward.

> 🔍 **Verifier note.** Core defect is real and fully reproducible (exit-2 failure + 172 errors under legacy mode). Adjusting because: (a) severity should be Medium — pure quality-gate/tooling issue, no functional impact; (b) the "CI passes lint vacuously" framing is inaccurate — verified that no CI workflow runs `npm run lint`, so the lint step simply doesn't exist in CI, compounding the broken local command. Locations are all correct. Also note the legacy config extends plugin:storybook/recommended, so a flat-config migration must include eslint-plugin-storybook (already a devDependency) in addition to the plugins the finder listed.


<a id="f-dead-template-code"></a>
#### 🟡 MEDIUM — Half the codebase is unused antd-multi-dashboard template demo code shipped to production

**ID:** `dead-template-code` &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Phase:** P2 &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Category:** Dead code / maintainability &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Effort:** Medium &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Confidence:** High

**Location(s):**
- `Frontend/src/components/dashboard/`
- `Frontend/public/mocks/`
- `Frontend/src/layouts/app/SideNav copy.tsx:1`
- `Frontend/package.json:2`

**Problem.** The app retains the entire `antd-multi-dashboard` starter template: 94 components under `src/components/dashboard/`, 64 `.stories.tsx` files, 28+ mock JSON files in `public/mocks/`, and demo pages (Projects, corporate, social, marketing, logistics). None are referenced by `src/Report` or `src/routes` (grep returned 0 references from report/routes into `components/dashboard`). All 12 `@ts-ignore` comments and nearly all high-`any` files (CampaignsCard, CampaignsAdsCard, TransactionsCard) live exclusively in this dead template code. `src/layouts/app/SideNav copy.tsx` (285 lines) is an editor-duplicate file imported by nothing. package.json still calls itself `antd-multi-dashboard`.

**Impact.** A government trade-reporting system ships thousands of lines of fake marketing/social/logistics dashboards and mock data, inflating bundle size, attack surface (mock JSON, demo routes), and onboarding confusion. It also pollutes lint/grep results, hiding real issues in noise. Reviewers cannot tell template scaffolding from production code.

**Recommendation.** Delete the unused template tree (`components/dashboard`, demo pages and their routes, `public/mocks`, `SideNav copy.tsx`) and the stories for components you do not maintain. Rename the package and update README. Run the build after removal to confirm nothing real depended on it.

**🛠 How to Fix _(est. 2-3 hours)_**

unused

1. Confirm the safe-deletion set with a final grep from the LIVE tree only. From Frontend/, run: grep -rEn "components/dashboard" src | grep -v '^src/components/dashboard' (must be empty), and grep -rEn "ProjectsDashboardPage|CorporateAboutPage|CorporateTeamPage|CorporateFaqPage|CorporateContactPage|CorporatePricingPage|CorporateLicensePage|UserAccountLayout|CorporateLayout" src | grep -vE 'src/(pages/(corporate|dashboards|userAccount)|layouts/(corporate|userAccount))/' (must be empty). Both were empty during the audit; re-confirm before deleting.
2. Delete the dead template component tree: `git rm -r 'Frontend/src/components/dashboard'` (118 tracked files; this removes all 11 of the 12 @ts-ignore sites and the high-`any` CampaignsCard/CampaignsAdsCard/TransactionsCard).
3. Remove the dashboard barrel re-export so the build doesn't fail on a missing module. In Frontend/src/components/index.ts delete line 21 `export * from './dashboard';`. (Leave lines 1-20 + 22 intact — Card, PageHeader, Logo, Container, etc. are used by the real Report pages and layouts.)
4. Delete the editor-duplicate sidebar: `git rm 'Frontend/src/layouts/app/SideNav copy.tsx'`. Verified nothing imports it — only `src/layouts/app/SideNav.tsx` is imported (by App.tsx line 27).
5. Delete the unmounted demo pages and their barrels. These export ProjectsDashboardPage / Corporate*Page / userAccount pages but are NOT referenced in src/routes/routes.tsx (router mounts only auth, errors, Certificate, /User, /Report, /Timeline, /Test, and /dashboards/default→HowToUsePage): `git rm -r 'Frontend/src/pages/dashboards' 'Frontend/src/pages/corporate' 'Frontend/src/pages/userAccount'`.
6. Delete the matching unused layout shells that wrap those demo pages: `git rm -r 'Frontend/src/layouts/corporate' 'Frontend/src/layouts/userAccount'` (the 12th @ts-ignore lives in layouts/corporate/index.tsx). Keep layouts/app, layouts/dashboards, layouts/guest — routes.tsx uses DashboardLayout and GuestLayout.
7. Fix the two barrels that re-export the now-deleted modules. In Frontend/src/pages/index.ts remove the three lines `export * from './dashboards';`, `export * from './corporate';`, `export * from './userAccount';` (keep Home, Sitemap, authentication, errors). In Frontend/src/layouts/index.ts remove `export { CorporateLayout } from './corporate';` and `export { UserAccountLayout } from './userAccount';` (keep AppLayout, DashboardLayout, GuestLayout).
8. Delete the orphaned mock JSON. After step 2-7 the only remaining mocks consumers are the deleted stories/pages. Remove the static-served fixtures: `git rm -r 'Frontend/public/mocks'` (35 files — they are copied verbatim into dist/ by Vite, so this also trims shipped static assets).
9. Remove stories that referenced the deleted tree/mocks so `npm run build-storybook` and lint stay green: the 64 *.stories.tsx under components/dashboard go with step 2; additionally delete the remaining template-only stories that import public/mocks JSON: Frontend/src/components/PricingTable/PricingTable.stories.tsx, Frontend/src/components/TimelineCard/TimelineCard.stories.tsx, Frontend/src/components/NotificationsCard/Notifications.stories.tsx. (Keep the PricingTable/TimelineCard/NotificationsCard components themselves unless a follow-up confirms they are unused — they are still in the components barrel.)
10. Rename the package and refresh metadata in Frontend/package.json: change `"name": "antd-multi-dashboard"` to `"tradenet-admin-report-frontend"`, drop the stale template fields `"_id": "antd-multi-dashboard@1.1.0"` and `"readme": "ERROR: No README data found!"`. Replace the template Frontend/README.md content with a short project README describing the TradeNet Admin Report frontend (Vite + React 19 + AntD 5 + Redux + axios) and run commands.
11. Run the verification build (step in 'verification'). If `tsc` reports a missing-module/unused-export error, it is pointing at a real remaining reference — fix that import rather than restoring the template.

```ts
// Frontend/src/components/index.ts  — remove the dead barrel re-export
  export { UserAvatar } from './UserAvatar/UserAvatar';
  export * from './Nprogress';
- export * from './dashboard';
  export * from './Container';

// Frontend/src/pages/index.ts  — drop unmounted demo page barrels
  export { HomePage } from './Home.tsx';
  export { SitemapPage } from './Sitemap.tsx';
- export * from './dashboards';
- export * from './corporate';
- export * from './userAccount';
  export * from './authentication';
  export * from './errors';

// Frontend/src/layouts/index.ts  — drop unused demo layouts
  export { AppLayout } from './app';
  export { DashboardLayout } from './dashboards';
  export { GuestLayout } from './guest/Guest.tsx';
- export { CorporateLayout } from './corporate';
- export { UserAccountLayout } from './userAccount';

// Frontend/package.json
- "name": "antd-multi-dashboard",
+ "name": "tradenet-admin-report-frontend",
  "private": "false",
- "readme": "ERROR: No README data found!",
- "_id": "antd-multi-dashboard@1.1.0",
  "version": "1.4.2",
```

**Verify:**
- From Frontend/: `npm run build` (runs `tsc && vite build`). A clean compile proves no router-mounted code depended on the deleted template — this is the authoritative gate.
- `npm run lint` (eslint with --max-warnings 0): warning/error count should drop sharply now that the high-`any` and all @ts-ignore template files are gone. `grep -rn '@ts-ignore' Frontend/src` should now return 0 matches.
- `npm run build-storybook` should still succeed (confirms no orphaned story imports a deleted module/mock).
- Smoke-test the running app (`npm run dev`): sign in and exercise the live routes /dashboards/default, /User/List, a /Report/* page, /Timeline/Detail — all render; navigating to the old demo paths (e.g. /corporate/about) 404s as before since they were never mounted.
- Confirm the static surface shrank: `ls Frontend/dist/mocks` after build should not exist (no more fake Campaigns/SocialComments/Trucks JSON shipped).

**Pitfalls / rollback.** Ordering matters: delete the modules (steps 2,5,6) BEFORE or together with removing their barrel re-exports (steps 3,7) — if you delete a folder but leave `export * from './dashboard'` in components/index.ts, `tsc` fails on a missing module (that's expected and is your safety net, not a regression). Do the package.json `name` change as its own concern: renaming can affect any tooling keyed off the package name (changesets is present), and if a Docker/CI step references the old name `antd-multi-dashboard` it must be updated — grep the repo root and infra for that string before renaming. Do NOT remove the shared components still wired into the real app via components/index.ts (Card, PageHeader, Logo, Container, NProgress, BackBtn, RefreshBtn, FaqCollapse, EmployeeCard, ContactForm, SitemapCard) — Report pages and layouts import them. PricingTable/TimelineCard/NotificationsCard/SocialMediaCard remain exported from the barrel; only their *.stories.tsx are safe to delete now — defer deleting the components themselves to a follow-up that confirms zero live importers. Everything is one git commit, so rollback is `git revert`/`git checkout` if the build surfaces an unexpected dependency.

> 🔍 **Verifier note.** Two minor inaccuracies that do not change the verdict: (1) the finding says "94 components under src/components/dashboard" — actual is 47 component .tsx files (118 files total including stories/assets/index). (2) Bundle-size impact is overstated: this is a Vite build, so unimported/unrouted demo components are tree-shaken out of the production JS bundle. The mock JSON in Frontend/public/ IS shipped verbatim (served as static assets), so the attack-surface/footprint concern there is real but small. The dominant and fully-valid impacts are maintainability, onboarding confusion, and lint/grep noise that hides real issues. Medium severity is appropriate for a dead-code/maintainability finding; not security-critical (the demo routes/pages are not even mounted in the router, so "demo routes" attack surface is essentially nil). Recommendation to delete the template tree, rename the package, and rebuild is sound.


<a id="f-any-and-anyobject-escape-hatch"></a>
#### 🟡 MEDIUM — Pervasive `any` (66 sites) and an AnyObject catch-all type defeat strict mode

**ID:** `any-and-anyobject-escape-hatch` &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Phase:** P2 &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Category:** Type safety &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Effort:** Large &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Confidence:** High

**Location(s):**
- `Frontend/src/types/AnyObject.ts:1 (core defect); Frontend/src/services/BasicHttpServices.ts:14-52; Frontend/src/Report/Page/GenericReportPage.tsx:653-654,788; Frontend/src/components/My Components/Table/BasicTable.tsx:33,48,121`
- `Frontend/src/types/AnyObject.ts:1`
- `Frontend/src/hooks/useFetchData.tsx:4`
- `Frontend/src/components/dashboard/default/CampaignsCard/CampaignsCard.tsx:1`

**Problem.** tsconfig has `strict: true` (good), but it is widely circumvented: 66 `: any` annotations across src, a `types/AnyObject.ts` that opens with `/* eslint-disable @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any */` and defines `{[x:string]:any}`, and 12 `@ts-ignore` comments. `AnyObject` is the return type threaded through the entire data/table layer (`BasicHttpServices`, `BasicTable`, `GenericReportPage`), so report rows are effectively untyped. `tsc --noEmit` currently exits 0 only because these escape hatches suppress the errors strict mode would raise.

**Impact.** The benefit of strict mode is nullified along the core report data path. Field renames, missing properties, and shape mismatches between the .NET backend DTOs and the React tables compile cleanly and only surface as runtime bugs or blank/incorrect report cells.

**Recommendation.** Replace `AnyObject` on real (non-template) paths with generated/handwritten DTO interfaces matching the backend models; make `BasicHttpServices`/`useFetchData`/table generics carry `<T>`. Add `@typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any` as an error in the new flat config and burn down the 66 sites, starting with `src/Report` and `src/services`.

**🛠 How to Fix _(est. 1.5 days)_**

Make the `no-explicit-any` lint rule an error so new `any`/`AnyObject` usages are blocked, then thread real `<T>` generics through the report data path (`BasicHttpServices` → `useFetchData` → `GenericReportPage` → `BasicTable`) and replace `AnyObject` with handwritten DTO interfaces on the report (non-template) paths. Leave vendor dashboard template `any` sites alone per the verifier.

1. Open Frontend/.eslintrc.cjs. The repo uses the CLASSIC eslintrc config (there is no flat eslint.config.* file even though eslint ^9.28.0 is installed). It currently has NO explicit no-explicit-any rule — it only inherits the default 'warn' from 'plugin:@typescript-eslint/recommended'. ADD an explicit error rule to the `rules` object: `'@typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any': 'error'`. (Do NOT switch to a flat config; the verifier confirmed the classic config is correct here.)
2. Run `npm run lint` (script: `eslint . --ext ts,tsx ...` in package.json) to enumerate every offending site now that it's an error. Expect ~66 `: any` reports plus the AnyObject definition file. Use this list to scope the burn-down to src/Report and src/services first.
3. Fix the genuine type-mismatch bug in Frontend/src/services/BasicHttpServices.ts: `GetSingle` is annotated `Promise<AnyObject>` but its body constructs and returns a `PaginationType` (lines 14-19). Decide the real contract: GetSingle is called only from Frontend/src/hooks/useFormload.tsx:20 to fetch one record. Re-type it as a generic `GetSingle = async <T = unknown>(url: string): Promise<T>` and return `resp.data as T` instead of the bogus JSON.parse(JSON.stringify(...)) round-trip cast to PaginationType. Apply the same `<T = unknown>` generic + `resp.data as T` pattern to Post, Put, Delete, ToggleUserActive so callers can supply the response DTO; keep `Get` returning `PaginationType` (it is list-shaped).
4. In Frontend/src/hooks/useFetchData.tsx, change the hook to a generic `const useFetchData = <T = unknown>(url: string)` and type the state as `useState<T[]>([])` (or `useState<T | undefined>(undefined)` depending on usage) and `useState<unknown>(null)` for error — removing both `: any` at lines 4-5. Note the existing missing-dependency bug: fetchData's useCallback deps array is `[]` but it closes over `url`; add `url` while you are here (this is why the effect re-fetches via the separate `[url]` effect).
5. In Frontend/src/types/AnyObject.ts, keep the type only as a generic-default fallback but stop treating it as the data contract. Add a doc comment marking it deprecated for report rows. Do NOT delete it yet — PaginationType, BasicTable, and BasicTableColumn all use it as their `<T extends AnyObject = AnyObject>` default constraint, so removing it breaks compilation until all generics are supplied.
6. Create per-report DTO interfaces. ReportColumnConfig (Frontend/src/Report/config/reportTypes.ts:22) keys columns by string `key`/`dataIndex`, so define interfaces whose field names match the backend response JSON property names (camelCased .NET DTO members) for the reports being migrated, e.g. `interface MemberRegistrationRow { companyName: string; registrationNo: string; companyCount: number; ... }`. Put them next to each report page (ListOfDirectors.tsx, MemberRegistrationReport.tsx, CompanyProfile.tsx) or in a shared Frontend/src/Report/config/reportRowTypes.ts.
7. Thread the generic through GenericReportPage.tsx. Replace `PaginationType<AnyObject>` in fetchRows (lines 653-654) and the `<BasicTable<AnyObject>>` instantiation (line 788) with the report-specific row type. Since GenericReportPage is config-driven, add an optional type parameter to the page or carry the row type on ReportPageConfig (e.g. `ReportPageConfig<TRow extends Record<string, unknown> = Record<string, unknown>>`) and instantiate `<BasicTable<TRow>>`; for reports without a DTO yet, fall back to `Record<string, unknown>` rather than `AnyObject`.
8. Change the BasicTable generic default from `AnyObject` to a non-`any` shape. In Frontend/src/components/My Components/Table/BasicTable.tsx (lines 33, 48, 121) and Frontend/src/types/PaginationType.ts (line 3), change `<T extends AnyObject = AnyObject>` to `<T extends Record<string, unknown> = Record<string, unknown>>`. This keeps dynamic-key indexing working (string render keys) without an `any` index signature, so field typos in `dataIndex` against a concrete `T` are caught.
9. Burn down the remaining flagged report/service sites surfaced by lint: NameConvert.ts:2 `let character: any;` → type it `let character: string;` (it indexes a string). Audit the AnyObject imports in src/Report/Page/ListOfDirectors.tsx, MemberRegistrationReport.tsx, CompanyProfile.tsx and the hooks (useFormActions, useFormload, useFileUpload*, BasicStepForm) and replace `AnyObject` with the new DTO/`Record<string, unknown>` as appropriate.
10. Leave the vendor dashboard template files (e.g. Frontend/src/components/dashboard/default/CampaignsCard/CampaignsCard.tsx:1 and other src/components/dashboard/* any sites) as-is; per the verifier these are template noise and low-value. If lint now errors on them and you want green CI immediately, add a scoped `overrides` entry in .eslintrc.cjs for `src/components/dashboard/**` that sets `'@typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any': 'warn'`, so the error gate protects src/Report and src/services without forcing a template rewrite.

```ts
// Frontend/.eslintrc.cjs — ADD the rule (it does NOT currently exist; default was 'warn' via recommended)
  rules: {
    'react-refresh/only-export-components': [
      'warn',
      { allowConstantExport: true },
    ],
+   '@typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any': 'error',
  },
+ // Optional: keep vendor template noise non-blocking
+ overrides: [
+   {
+     files: ['src/components/dashboard/**/*.{ts,tsx}'],
+     rules: { '@typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any': 'warn' },
+   },
+ ],

// Frontend/src/services/BasicHttpServices.ts — fix the real shape lie + add generics
-export const GetSingle = async (url: string): Promise<AnyObject> => {
-  const resp = await axiosInstance.get(url);
-  const data = await resp.data;
-  const responseData: PaginationType = JSON.parse(JSON.stringify(data));
-  return responseData;
-};
+export const GetSingle = async <T = unknown>(url: string): Promise<T> => {
+  const resp = await axiosInstance.get<T>(url);
+  return resp.data;
+};
-export const Post = async (url: string, data: unknown): Promise<AnyObject> => {
+export const Post = async <T = unknown>(url: string, data: unknown): Promise<T> => {
   const resp = await axiosInstance.post(url, data);
-  const temp = await resp.data;
-  const responseData: PaginationType = JSON.parse(JSON.stringify(temp));
-  return responseData;
+  return resp.data as T;
 };

// Frontend/src/types/PaginationType.ts and BasicTable.tsx — drop the any index signature
-export interface PaginationType<T extends AnyObject = AnyObject> {
+export interface PaginationType<T extends Record<string, unknown> = Record<string, unknown>> {

// Frontend/src/hooks/useFetchData.tsx
-const useFetchData = (url: string) => {
-  const [data, setData] = useState<any>([]);
-  const [error, setError] = useState<any>(null);
+const useFetchData = <T = unknown>(url: string) => {
+  const [data, setData] = useState<T[]>([]);
+  const [error, setError] = useState<unknown>(null);
   ...
-  }, []);
+  }, [url]);

// Frontend/src/Report/Page/GenericReportPage.tsx — replace AnyObject on the data path
-    async (query: BasicTableQuery): Promise<PaginationType<AnyObject>> => {
-      const response = await axiosInstance.post<PaginationType<AnyObject>>(
+    async (query: BasicTableQuery): Promise<PaginationType<TRow>> => {
+      const response = await axiosInstance.post<PaginationType<TRow>>(
...
-      <BasicTable<AnyObject>
+      <BasicTable<TRow>
```

**Verify:**
- Run `npm run lint` in Frontend/ — it must report 0 errors for src/Report and src/services (no `@typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any` violations there). With the rule at 'error', a freshly added `: any` anywhere outside the dashboard override should fail lint.
- Run `npx tsc --noEmit` in Frontend/ — must still exit 0. Because BasicTable's default changed from `AnyObject` (any index) to `Record<string, unknown>`, any report whose `dataIndex` references a field absent from its DTO `TRow` will now fail compilation; fix those (that is the intended bug-surfacing).
- Negative test: temporarily mistype a `dataIndex` in one migrated report config (e.g. `companyName` → `compnyName`) against its concrete DTO `TRow` and confirm `tsc --noEmit` now errors where it previously compiled clean.
- Manually load a migrated report (e.g. Member Registration) in the app, apply filters, and confirm rows + the columnTotals footer still render — proving the generic re-type did not change runtime behavior.
- Confirm `grep -rn ': any' src/Report src/services` returns nothing (NameConvert.ts:2 fixed) and `GetSingle` no longer claims to return PaginationType.

**Pitfalls / rollback.** Do the .eslintrc.cjs rule flip and the generic threading together in one branch — flipping the rule to 'error' alone will turn ~66 warnings into build-breaking errors across the whole tree (including vendor dashboard files), so either land the dashboard `overrides` exclusion in the same change or keep the rule at 'warn' until the burn-down is done. Order matters: change PaginationType/BasicTable defaults from AnyObject to Record<string,unknown> BEFORE deleting/deprecating AnyObject, otherwise every generic default breaks at once. The GetSingle return-type change is behavioral: its sole caller (useFormload.tsx:20) does `JSON.stringify(await GetSingle(...))`, so returning `resp.data` directly instead of a PaginationType wrapper is what was always intended, but smoke-test the form-load path. AnyObject cannot be deleted until all 14 importing files are migrated; rollback is a single git revert of the branch since no data/migration is involved.

> 🔍 **Verifier note.** Severity Medium is correct: this is a latent-bug/maintainability risk (backend DTO renames or shape drift compile cleanly and surface as blank/incorrect report cells at runtime), not a correctness or security break. The actionable fix is narrower than stated: replace AnyObject with real DTO interfaces on BasicHttpServices/GenericReportPage/BasicTable and thread <T> generics; the dashboard/* any sites are vendor template noise and low-value to burn down. The recommendation should target .eslintrc.cjs (classic config), not a flat config, and could simply promote the existing no-explicit-any warning to error.


<a id="f-duplicate-date-and-dead-moment"></a>
#### 🔵 LOW — moment is a dependency but unused; dayjs date-format helpers duplicated across report pages

**ID:** `duplicate-date-and-dead-moment` &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Phase:** P3 &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Category:** Dependencies / duplication &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Effort:** Trivial &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Confidence:** High

**Location(s):**
- `Frontend/package.json:32`
- `Frontend/src/Report/Page/GenericReportPage.tsx:110`
- `Frontend/src/Report/config/reportConfigs.ts:1`

**Problem.** `moment@2.30.1` is declared in package.json but a full-tree grep finds zero `moment` references in `src` — it is pure dead weight (moment is also officially in maintenance mode). The codebase standardized on `dayjs` (10 files), but date-formatting logic is re-implemented ad hoc: a `formatDate` helper in GenericReportPage.tsx:110 plus 36 scattered `dayjs(...).format('YYYY-MM-DD')` call sites, several with their own null/invalid-date handling.

**Impact.** Shipping an unused ~70KB date library bloats the bundle and invites future contributors to reintroduce a second date system (the inconsistency the system prompt warned about). Duplicated format/parse logic means a formatting fix (timezone, invalid-date display) must be applied in many places and is easy to miss, risking inconsistent dates across reports.

**Recommendation.** Remove `moment` from package.json. Extract the `formatDate`/`formatBoolean` helpers into a single `src/utils/format.ts` and import everywhere instead of inlining `dayjs().format(...)`.

**🛠 How to Fix _(est. 45 min)_**

Drop the unused `moment` runtime dependency, then centralize the duplicated date/boolean formatting into the existing `src/utils/index.ts` barrel as parameterized helpers (output format + empty-value fallback are arguments, since callers use three different formats and three different fallbacks), and replace the per-file copies with imports.

1. Remove `moment` from Frontend/package.json: delete line 32 (`"moment": "^2.30.1",`) from the `dependencies` block. Note the verifier correction: the declared range is `^2.30.1`, not pinned `2.30.1`. There are zero `moment` references anywhere under `Frontend/src` (verified via grep), so nothing else needs touching for the removal.
2. Regenerate the lockfile so the dependency is actually dropped from the tree: run `cd Frontend && npm install` (project uses npm — `package-lock.json` lockfileVersion 3, no yarn/pnpm lock present). Commit the updated `package-lock.json` alongside `package.json`.
3. Add two shared, parameterized helpers to the existing barrel `Frontend/src/utils/index.ts` (named exports, consistent with `numberWithCommas`/`getNameInitials` already there). Do NOT create a new `src/utils/format.ts` file — reuse the existing barrel to avoid a second utils entry point. The date helper must be parameterized on output format AND empty/invalid fallback because callers use three formats (`YYYY-MM-DD`, `DD/MM/YYYY`, `YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm`) and three fallbacks (`'N/A'`, `''`, `'-'`). See patch for `formatReportDate` and `formatBoolean`.
4. Update the 6 files that inline a `formatDate`/`formatLegacyReportDate` copy. There is no path alias configured (no `@/` in tsconfig/vite), so use relative imports. In each file: (a) add `import { formatReportDate } from '<relative>/utils';`, (b) delete the local helper definition, (c) replace call sites preserving the original format and fallback.
5. GenericReportPage.tsx (Frontend/src/Report/Page/GenericReportPage.tsx): remove the local `formatDate` (lines 110-117) and `formatBoolean` (lines 119-129). Import `{ formatReportDate, formatBoolean }` from `../../utils`. Replace `formatDate(value)` call sites with `formatReportDate(value, 'YYYY-MM-DD', 'N/A')` (this file's helper falls back to the raw string when present and 'N/A' when empty — keep that behavior via the `fallback`/`onEmpty` args in the patch). `formatBoolean` is only used here (lines 186, 372) — move it but keep identical Yes/No logic.
6. reportConfigs.ts (Frontend/src/Report/config/reportConfigs.ts): remove `formatLegacyReportDate` (lines 327-330). Import `{ formatReportDate }` from `../../utils`. Replace its body usages and the 8 inline `dayjs(String(filters.X)).format('DD/MM/YYYY')` call sites (lines 6696/6699, 12285/12288, 12399, 13022/13025, 13076/13079) with `formatReportDate(filters.X, 'DD/MM/YYYY')`. Keep the existing `dayjs` import (line 1) — it is still used elsewhere (e.g. line 328 originally, plus any remaining direct calls).
7. CompanyProfile.tsx (Frontend/src/Report/Page/CompanyProfile.tsx): remove local `formatDate` (lines 122-129, fallback `''`/raw string, format `DD/MM/YYYY`). Replace with `formatReportDate(value, 'DD/MM/YYYY', '')`. Also the title-string call sites at 347-349 use `dayjs(filters.ToDate).format('DD/MM/YYYY')` — optionally convert to `formatReportDate`. Keep the `dayjs`/`Dayjs` import (line 24) since `dayjs()` is still used (line 221) and the `Dayjs` type is referenced.
8. CardListsByCompanyRegistrationNumber.tsx (Frontend/src/Report/Page/CardListsByCompanyRegistrationNumber.tsx): remove local `formatDate` (lines 82-88, format `DD/MM/YYYY`, fallback `''`). Replace calls with `formatReportDate(value, 'DD/MM/YYYY', '')`. The standalone `dayjs` import (line 4) can be removed if no other `dayjs()` call remains in the file (it does not — only the helper used it); verify before deleting.
9. ListOfDirectorsByCompanyRegistrationNo.tsx (Frontend/src/Report/Page/ListOfDirectorsByCompanyRegistrationNo.tsx): identical local `formatDate` (lines 40-46, `DD/MM/YYYY`, fallback `''`). Replace with `formatReportDate(value, 'DD/MM/YYYY', '')` and remove the now-unused `dayjs` import (line 4) after confirming no other `dayjs()` usage.
10. MemberRegistrationReport.tsx (Frontend/src/Report/Page/MemberRegistrationReport.tsx): local helper (lines ~57-61, `YYYY-MM-DD`, fallback `'N/A'` when empty, raw string when invalid). Replace with `formatReportDate(value, 'YYYY-MM-DD', 'N/A')`. Keep `dayjs`/`Dayjs` import (line 13) — `dayjs()` still used at line 132.
11. ExportsDrive.tsx (Frontend/src/Report/Page/ExportsDrive.tsx): local `formatDate` (lines 63-64) uses format `YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm` and fallback `'-'` (no isValid guard). Replace with `formatReportDate(value, 'YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm', '-')`. Keep the `dayjs` import (line 18) only if other `dayjs()` calls remain; this file's only use was the helper, so remove it if unused.
12. Run `npm run lint` and `npm run build` (which runs `tsc && vite build`) in Frontend to confirm no unused-import errors (eslint runs with `--max-warnings 0`) and the type check passes.

```ts
// 1) Frontend/package.json — remove the dead dependency (line 32)
// BEFORE
    "lodash": "^4.17.21",
    "moment": "^2.30.1",
    "mqtt": "^5.13.1",
// AFTER
    "lodash": "^4.17.21",
    "mqtt": "^5.13.1",

// 2) Frontend/src/utils/index.ts — add shared, parameterized helpers
import dayjs from 'dayjs';

/**
 * Centralized report date formatter.
 * @param value     raw value (string | Date | number | null | undefined)
 * @param format    dayjs output format, e.g. 'YYYY-MM-DD' | 'DD/MM/YYYY' | 'YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm'
 * @param onEmpty   what to return when value is falsy (callers use 'N/A' | '' | '-')
 * @param onInvalid what to return when value is non-empty but unparseable.
 *                  Defaults to the original string (matches GenericReportPage/MemberRegistration);
 *                  pass onEmpty for the CompanyProfile/CardLists behavior, or '-' for ExportsDrive.
 */
export const formatReportDate = (
  value: unknown,
  format = 'YYYY-MM-DD',
  onEmpty = '',
  onInvalid?: string,
): string => {
  if (!value) {
    return onEmpty;
  }
  const raw = String(value);
  const parsed = dayjs(raw);
  return parsed.isValid()
    ? parsed.format(format)
    : (onInvalid ?? raw);
};

export const formatBoolean = (value: unknown): string => {
  const text = value?.toString().toLowerCase();
  if (value === true || text === 'true') return 'Yes';
  if (value === false || text === 'false') return 'No';
  return value?.toString() ?? 'N/A';
};

// 3) Example call-site replacement (GenericReportPage.tsx)
// BEFORE
const formatDate = (value: unknown) => {
  if (!value) return 'N/A';
  const parsed = dayjs(value.toString());
  return parsed.isValid() ? parsed.format('YYYY-MM-DD') : value.toString();
};
// AFTER (delete local helper; at use sites)
import { formatReportDate, formatBoolean } from '../../utils';
// ... formatReportDate(value, 'YYYY-MM-DD', 'N/A')   // invalid falls back to raw string by default
```

**Verify:**
- `grep -rn "moment" Frontend/src` returns nothing and `grep "moment" Frontend/package.json Frontend/package-lock.json` returns nothing after `npm install`.
- `cd Frontend && npm run build` succeeds (tsc + vite build) with no unused-variable / unused-import TS errors and no leftover references to the deleted local `formatDate`/`formatLegacyReportDate`/`formatBoolean` definitions.
- `cd Frontend && npm run lint` passes with 0 warnings (the script enforces `--max-warnings 0` and `--report-unused-disable-directives`), catching any now-unused `dayjs` import.
- Manually load each affected report in the running app and confirm date columns render exactly as before: Generic/Member reports show `YYYY-MM-DD` (empty -> N/A), Company Profile / Card Lists / Directors-by-CRN show `DD/MM/YYYY` (empty -> blank), Exports Drive shows `YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm` (empty -> -), and Import Licence subtitles still show the `DD/MM/YYYY` From/To range.
- Toggle a boolean-rendered column in a Generic report and confirm it still shows Yes/No (formatBoolean behavior unchanged).
